(Do note that CRMLTX was the previous code for this programme)

You're facing increasing pressure to act swiftly, maintain relevance, and stay ahead of the curve in today's rapidly changing landscape. 

As a senior leader, you're expected to spearhead transformation efforts in response to these challenges.

In this programme, you will be equipped with practical frameworks and actionable strategies to lead and drive transformation effectively. Our approach synthesises current research and hands-on consultancy insights from working with senior leadership teams across various levels and layers in agencies. 

To make the most of this experience, we ask that you come prepared with
learning_outcomes:
Understand the crippling challenges and differentiating success factors of transformation
Explore the essential dimensions of strategy, leadership and culture, and what needs to be done differently to multiply the odds of success
Gain practical frames and actionable tools to apply and/or adapt for organisational transformation efforts
who_should_attend: You are a leader who is preparing for or in the midst of a divisional, departmental or organisational transformation

title: Foundation for Uplifting Service by Ron Kaufman
description:
(Do note that ELFUS13 was the previous code for this programme)

In our highly customised world, where customer experience is constantly evolving, people expect to be recognised and uniquely served. To stand out, your service must be Unbelievable! 

Uplifting Service provides service excellence workshops you need to continuously improve internal and external service performance. This suite of powerful tools enables the foundation of your superior and sustainable service culture.
learning_outcomes:
Learn a framework to continuously improve the customer service performance and employee engagement.
Build a common service language to help employees from different departments and levels communicate more quickly and easily.
Enable employees to evaluate processes and actions from the customer’s point of view and improve the customer journey– for external customers and internal colleagues.
Empower team members to earn compliments, resolve persistent customer complaints, and exceed customer expectations.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is keen in Service Excellence.

title: Masterclass in Gestalt Interventions
description:
This masterclass offers a holistic approach to working in large, complex social systems, with a strong emphasis on the core Gestalt concepts of use of self and personal presence. It includes lectures, experiential exercises, skills practice with feedback, case studies and analysis, reflection and journaling, and reading materials. Learning takes place in a range of settings, including individual work, pairs, groups, ad hoc structures, and plenary sessions.

Three internationally recognized Gestalt teachers and practitioners lead the programme.

Method of the Masterclass

Please note that there will be a pre and post class virtual session for this programme.

Pre-class Virtual Session: 20 July 2026, 8:00am - 10:00am
Physical Classroom: 3-6 August 2026, 9:00am - 5:00pm and 7 August 2026, 9:00am - 1:00pm
Post-class Virtual Session: 1 October 2026, 8:00am - 11:00am
learning_outcomes:
Experience and learn Gestalt theory and practice
Conceive of organisations as complex social systems existing within the context of a broader environment
Develop greater self-awareness and competence in use-of-self as an instrument of change and making a difference with your presence
Master the four primary models: Cycle of Experience, Unit of Work, Four Roles of the Gestalt Intervener, and the RSVP Fundamentals of Leadership Model
Recognise resistance to change and adopt a respectful and creative attitude toward resistance using the Gestalt Paradoxical Theory of Change
Enhance skills in attending to and intervening at multiple levels of system
who_should_attend:
You are an Organisation Development practitioner in the Singapore Public Service. You would like to develop your competence in adapting OD Interventions in your practice through designing and facilitating complex and unpredictable situations. Officers who also play an OD role in other functions are welcome. 

To qualify for entry to the Masterclass in Gestalt Interventions Programme, you will need to have attended either of these programmes: OD Graduate Certificate Programme (MSGCOD) / Intermediate Programme for OD Practitioners (MSIPOVL) / OD Practitioner Certification Programme (MSODCP). If you do not have these qualifications but believe that this programme is for you, please write in with reasons why.

title: Foundational Curriculum for Frontline Service Officers
description:
(Do note that CRFCFSO was the previous code for this programme)

Are you in your first year of frontline service work?

This curriculum equips you with the essential service skills you will need in your first year as a frontline service officer.

From serving as one Public Service using the CARE Service Principles to mastering empathetic communication, this curriculum will empower you to deliver effective services to citizens with confidence and resilience. Secure your spot today to upskill and become a confident frontline service officer. 

Please note that this is a blended curriculum comprising a series of curated e-learning programmes and two in-person training programmes. Access to e-learning programmes will be available within 2 weeks from your registration date until 70 days after the last day of your in-person class.

E-Learning Component

Learn at your own pace with our e-learning programmes:

First 100 Days: Onboarding for Service Officers
CARE Service Principles
Understandin
learning_outcomes:
By the end of the curriculum, you will be able to:

Articulate the role and responsibilities of frontline service officers.
Apply CARE service principles in interactions with citizens across various service touchpoints. 
Demonstrate personal resilience in managing change and difficult situations.
Apply empathetic communication techniques to manage difficult customer situations and enhance customer satisfaction.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is in the first year of frontline service.

title: Career Fitness for Mid-career Officers (Senior Officers)
description:
(Do note that CRCRE10 was the previous code for this programme)

When was the last time you pause and reflect about how far you've come in your career?

This programme provides a structured approach to rediscover your career identity and transform your wealth of experience into meaningful opportunities. Through structured approaches and hands-on exercises, discover how to transform your wealth of experience into meaningful opportunities while managing work-life commitments to shape the second half of your next CareerFitness journey with renewed purpose and impact.
learning_outcomes:
At the end of this programme, participants will be able to:

Discuss what career development is and why it is important
Rediscover personal values, interests and strengths to reinvent the second half of their career
Evaluate and leverage transferable skills and career interests to create new career possibilities
Explore career development resources and support to gather career insights
Recognise how structure and clarity lead to meaningful coaching conversations
Develop an actionable career plan with specific milestones and support mechanisms
who_should_attend: Public service officers (MX 13 and above)

title: Career Fitness for Mid-career Officers (Support Officers)
description:
(Do note that CRCRE20 was the previous code for this programme)

Seeking fresh energy and purpose in the next half of your professional journey? 

This programme guides you to review your years of valuable experience and translate them into meaningful new opportunities. 

Through proven frameworks and practical approaches, you'll rediscover your unique strengths, translate them into exciting career possibilities, and shape the second half of your CareerFitness journey. 

Whether you're looking to deepen your expertise in your current role or explore new horizons, this programme provides the structured guidance and practical tools to help you create impact while maintaining work-life harmony.
learning_outcomes:
At the end of this programme, participants will be able to:

Discuss what career development is and why it is important
Rediscover personal values, interests and strengths to reinvent the second half of their career
Evaluate and leverage transferable skills and career interests to create new career possibilities
Explore career development resources and support to gather career insights
Recognise how structure and clarity lead to meaningful coaching conversations
Develop an actionable career plan with specific milestones and support mechanisms.
who_should_attend: Public service officers (MX 13(I) and below)

title: Driving Customer Experience (CX): An Organisational Systems Approach
description:
(Do note that CROSAA was the previous code for this programme)

A consistent and high-quality service experience, across internal and external stakeholders, is key to fulfilling your agency’s mission. Yet many agencies struggle with fragmented efforts, unclear service vision, and siloed ownership of service culture.

In this two-day workshop, participants will:

Co-create a shared service vision that aligns cross-functional teams.
Activate the whole agency by using practical tools and service levers to build shared ownership, especially beyond frontline teams.
Improve what matters, with the right metrics and feedback loops to sustain and reinforce culture change.

The programme blends diagnostics, real-world examples, and practical tools to equip you with actionable strategies and peer insights. Walk away with a clear plan to delivering a consistent, mission-aligned service experience across your agency.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the importance of a co-created shared service vision to deliver consistent service experience for internal and external stakeholders.

Assess the right service culture levers and tools to activate whole of agency to have shared ownership of service culture.

Recommend organisational changes based on the insights from the feedback loops to measure and reinforce service culture in their agency.
who_should_attend: Senior Assistant Directors & above leaders who want drive a consistent customer experience across functions and ranks through the use of service culture levers and tangible clear metrics.

title: Service Unwrapped: AI-gniting Service Innovation
description:
(Do note that CRSUSU1 was the previous code for this programme)

Service Unwrapped: AI-gniting Service Innovation takes you on a journey of discovery, unravelling the complexities of AI implementation layer by layer. This programme progressively unwraps practical insights and tools to help you harness GenAI for your service challenges.

In this programme, you'll experiment with building a practical GenAI protoype for an actual service pain point you're facing, using accessible no-code platforms like Pair and AI Bots*. 

We begin by helping you develop a clear problem statement, then gradually guide you through building your prototype solution. No technical background required.

*Pair & AI Bots are accessible via GSIB
learning_outcomes:
1.    Describe the potential of GenAI in enhancing service delivery using examples from both public and private sectors to inspire innovative thinking in agency contexts

2.    Outline strategies and best-practices to overcome common GenAI adoption challenges in the service delivery context

3.    Identify service delivery workflow challenges that are well-suited for GenAI integration

4.    Prototype a simple GenAI service bot through hands on practice that can be implemented in their organisation with readily available tools (e.g. Pair, AI Bot)
who_should_attend: You are an officer in the Service Delivery function.

title: Service Unwrapped: Insights to Impact
description:
Service Unwrapped: Insights to Impact equips officers with the skills to turn customer data into action. This programme takes officers beyond technical data analysis, emphasising the journey from uncovering customer insights to engaging stakeholders for service improvements.

Through hands-on practice, participants strengthen their sensemaking skills, synthesise findings into compelling problem statements, create prototypes that communicate ideas persuasively—enabling insight-driven decisions that improve service delivery and customer experience.

Working on real workplace projects, participants will benefit from peer sharing and collaborative problem-solving. Participants will also have the opportunity to participate in a clinic session to share progress updates, receive feedback, and refine their approaches with support from fellow participants.

By combining sensemaking skills with practical communication strategies, this programme empowers officers to bridge the gap between custome
learning_outcomes:
Apply sensemaking methods to analyse customer data and uncover meaningful actionable insights. 
 
Translate insights into well-defined problem statements. 
 
Develop strategies to communicate customer insights persuasively to influence service decisions.
who_should_attend: Public officers in various functional roles that support their agency’s service delivery.

title: Service Values Workshop For Frontline Officers
description:
(Do note that CRSVW13 was the previous code for this programme)

Designed in partnership with ROHEI, the Service Values Workshop offers a multi-sensory, immersive learning experience. Through experiential learning activities, you'll learn to apply the Whole-of-Government CARE service principles, enabling you to deliver seamless and coordinated service to our citizens.
 

Note: This course is conducted at Rohei Campus (Devan Nair Institute, 80 Jurong East Street 21, #05-03)
learning_outcomes:
Appreciate the significance of understanding the customers' perspective and putting them top of mind, displaying empathy and proactively resolving their issues, and placing people at the core in service delivery.
Employ strategies to build and display emotional resilience, and learn to reframe and bounce back from difficult or challenging situations, while adopting a fresh perspective for delivering workplace excellence.
Work collaboratively and effectively as part of a team within and across agencies and with multiple stakeholders, optimising for the greater good.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who regularly interacts with members of the public.

title: The ODExcelerator Programme for Advanced OD Practitioners
description:
Ready to elevate your OD practice and create system-wide impact? Sign up here.

Introducing the ODExcelerator Programme – a bespoke accelerator programme targeted at advanced OD practitioners – designed to deepen their competence, enhance their confidence and maximise system-wide impact while being deployed on OD consulting projects across different agencies or even at the Whole-of-Government levels.

The ODExcelerator Programme provides a structured, practice-driven pathway for advanced OD practitioners to deepen their consulting capability while progressing towards completion of the Executive Certificate for OD Professionals (ECOD) requirements. Through the programme, CSC-ILOD intentionally curates and plans practitioners’ consulting engagements, deploying them across appropriate OD projects and providing ongoing project supervision and mentoring throughout the journey.

By integrating practicum, supervision, and applied learning into a cohesive experience, the ODExcelerator enables
learning_outcomes:
1. Broadened Perspective & Knowledge: By working on consulting gigs outside of their organisation, OD practitioners will gain exposure to diverse organisational challenges and cultures. This broader perspective allows them to apply good practices from other agencies and contexts to solve problems in their own organisation, driving innovation and fresh solutions.

2. Enhanced Consulting Skills: The practicum allows practitioners to develop hands-on consulting skills, such as stakeholder management, diagnosis, intervention design, and facilitation. These skills will directly benefit the organisation, as practitioners bring back more refined expertise to manage internal OD projects, ensuring greater effectiveness and impact.

3. Professional Growth and Increased Confidence: This programme provides OD practitioners with the valuable opportunity to be mentored & supervised by senior OD practitioners with significant experience in both public and private sector contexts. As they grow in conf
who_should_attend:
You are an advanced-level Organisation Development practitioner with a strong passion in OD and a strong motivation to serve the Singapore Public Service and would like to develop your competence and confidence to support your leaders in achieving strategic outcomes and enhancing organisational health. 

ODExcelerator Prerequisites 

The ODExcelerator Programme is designed for advanced OD practitioners who are ready to deepen their consulting capability while progressing towards the completion of the Executive Certificate for OD Professionals (ECOD) requirements. The prerequisites are generally as follows: 

OD Practitioner Foundation Programme; AND
One of the following: Graduate Certificate in Organisation Development OR Intermediate OD Practitioner Programme (previous programme title) OR OD Practitioner Certification Programme (ODCP) OR Graduate Diploma in Leading and Sustaining Change (LSC). 

Selection Process:

Applicants who meet the programme prerequisites will be screened for e

title: Writing Empathetic Emails with GenAI
description:
(Do note that CREAI was the previous code for this programme)

Discover the power of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) in enhancing email communication with our upcoming 1-day programme. 

 

The ‘Writing Empathetic Emails with GenAI’ (CREAI) programme is designed for public officers who are in service functions. It equips service officers with the knowledge and skills to leverage AI tools like Pair and SmartCompose to craft empathetic email responses to their customers. They will gain insights into the role and benefits of AI in email communication, and how to complement it with human connectedness and empathy.

 

Join us and harness the potential of AI in improving email structure, content, and tone, ensuring a seamless blend of technology and empathy in your correspondence with the public!

 

You are required to bring your GSIB laptop to class so that you can access to two AI tools easily, i.e. Pair and SmartCompose.

 

Please ensure that you have access to Pair and Smar
learning_outcomes:
By the end of this workshop, you will be able to:  

Explain the role and benefits of AI in enhancing service delivery to customers via email communication within the public service.  
Identify the limits of AI in understanding context and human emotions, reinforcing the irreplaceable value of human empathy.  
Apply AI tools (Pair Chat and SmartCompose) to improve the structure, content, and empathetic tone of emails. 
Draft responses to emails using AI while ensuring that issues are addressed with appropriate empathy.
who_should_attend: Public officers who are in service functions and are keen to learn how to leverage AI to write emails to customers. It is recommended for officers to attend ‘[Virtual Classroom] Writing with Empathy’ programme CRWEV10 before attending this programme.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Writing with Empathy
description:
(Do note that CRWEV10 was the previous code for this programme)

In the course of your work, you would have to write and respond to members of the public through emails. It is important that you foster positive and impactful interactions with the public through your writing. In this programme, you will learn to build trust and rapport with your audience by practising CARE and applying the key empathy principles. You will also gain a better understanding of their concerns and aspirations to write with empathy in your communications to the public.

This programme is a virtual version of 'Engaging with Empathy: Writing'. There will be pre-programme as well as mid-programme assignments in between synchronous sessions.
learning_outcomes:
Apply the CARE principles in your written communications
Write to your intended audience with clarity (on purpose, message to be delivered and outcomes to achieve)
Display empathy in your writing
Adopt personalised and nuanced correspondences
Discern issues that should or should not be addressed
Adopt an appropriate approach when writing to the public
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who wants to gain a better understanding of writing in the Public Service.

title: ComplianceConnect: Regulatory Tech
description:
Transform your regulatory practice with technology. This programme combines exposure to cutting-edge RegTech solutions at the MTX (Milipol TechX), an international public safety technology exhibition that brings together global innovators, technologists, and policymakers to advance transformative solutions, with hands-on training using government AI tools. Learn to identify AI-suitable regulatory challenges, prototype solutions using WOG tools like Pair and MS365 Copilot, and develop implementation strategies through our structured AI Impact Framework.

Join fellow practitioners in examining how technology-enabled, risk management approaches are shaping the future of regulatory excellence and gain valuable insights to drive your agency's compliance and enforcement transformation forward. Through interactive workshops, mini-hackathons, and peer learning, you'll gain practical skills to transform how your agency approaches compliance monitoring, investigation, enforcement, and regulatory
learning_outcomes:
Advocate the use of new technology to enhance regulatory practices and integrate them with existing processes.
 
Determine the applicability of emerging technologies to your agency's regulatory approach and evaluate feasibility (i.e. determine costs, benefits, and risks associated with new technology applications), test, scale and implement technology enabled solutions to improve regulatory efficiency and effectiveness.
 
Identify opportunities for regulatory innovation through stakeholder feedback, analyse trends to support adaptive regulatory strategies, and conduct After Action Review (AAR) on the implemented new technology to improve future application.
who_should_attend: This programme is open to all middle and senior level Regulatory, Compliance and Enforcement officers.

title: HR Analytics and Visualization: Drawing Insights From Data Analysis to Support Decision Making
description:
(Do note that CRHAZ12 was the previous code for this programme)

Are you keen to hone your skills in data analytics to develop and implement HR strategy?

In this 2-day programme, you will learn to leverage data to understand potential issues and trends in the workforce, create interactive data visualisations to analyse workforce flow dynamics, and develop hypotheses to diagnose HR problems. Draw insights from data analyses to support decision making and discover what it takes to tell a compelling data story.


We offer different HR analytics courses that are tailored to different training needs across the HR analytics value chain. Please select and register for the HR analytics programme that best suits your learning needs.

V11DMR focuses on building skills in data management and reporting – the first link in the analytics value chain. If data is not managed or accessible, analytics cannot start.

P12HAZ focuses on the analytical processes after data management; more specifically, th
learning_outcomes:
Recognise the end-to-end analytics process that is applied to most HR problems
Conduct descriptive and diagnostic analyses on HR issues/ problems
Create interactive data visualisations to communicate HR data and metrics effectively
Lead and direct HR analytics efforts with clear problem definition and hypotheses
Interpret analytics outcomes to improve decision making
Develop data storytelling capabilities to enhance communication effectiveness
who_should_attend:
You are a HR Officer who is required to analyse and interpret HR data to solve HR issues/problems. 

Note: Participants should be familiar with basic Excel functions (e.g., SUM, COUNT, IF, AND, OR and Pivot Tables) and basic statistical concepts (e.g., Mean, Media, Variance, Standard Deviation, Probability). Otherwise, it is recommended to attend [Virtual Classroom] HR Analytics: Data Management and Reporting before signing up for this course.

title: Thinking and Writing Clearly for Policy Officers
description:
(Do note that CRFPP10 was the previous code for this programme)

This programme is designed for individual contributors in the Policy and Planning function.

It aims to sharpen essential skills of Deep Critical Thinking, and Clear and Concise Writing (under the Policy and Planning Competency framework), essential for excelling in policy work.  The programme includes a 2-hour self-paced eLearning module followed by an interactive 2-day classroom experience.

You will better appreciate, through interactive learning activities and case study discussions, the factors that lead to effective policy design, implementation, and evaluation. You will also learn how to communicate your policy intent, analysis, and recommendations concisely and clearly.
learning_outcomes:
At the end of the programme, P&P officers will be able to:

Define policy problems;
Analyse some common challenges in the policy process; and
Write and convey policies concisely and with clarity.
who_should_attend: New Policy and Planning (P&P) officers

title: Critical Thinking for Support Officers
description:
(Do note that CRFRCTS was the previous code for this programme)

This programme addresses the cognitive and emotional aspects of decision-making, helping learners identify and overcome their biases and emotional triggers. Through hands-on experience with AI tools for information gathering and analysis, combined with peer-learning sessions, learners would develop a comprehensive toolkit for enhanced critical thinking and decision-making in their professional roles. The practical approach also ensures immediate application of learned concepts to real-world challenges.
learning_outcomes:
Apply structured analytical frameworks to identify root causes and make sound judgement in various situations.  
Recognise and mitigate personal cognitive biases and emotional triggers that affect decision-making, while using models to enhance self-awareness and critical thinking capabilities.
Use AI to enhance information gathering and obtain diverse viewpoints to support the process of critical thinking.
who_should_attend: Support Officer

title: International Relations Starter Programme
description:
(Do note that CRIRSP1 was the previous code for this programme)

Designed for public officers new to international relations (IR), this two-day foundation course focuses on hands-on practice, with experienced IR practitioners guiding participants in developing essential diplomatic skills and knowledge of Singapore's approach to international work. The programme helps officers transition confidently into their IR roles, equipping them to navigate international engagements and represent Singapore's interests effectively..
learning_outcomes:
Understand the fundamental importance of international relations, Singapore's foreign policy framework and the role of public officers in advancing Singapore's international interests through the Whole-of-Government approach.
Apply essential diplomatic protocols, cultural sensitivities, and business etiquette required for effective engagement with international counterparts in various official settings, while keeping up our international relations interests.
Demonstrate practical skills in managing international work, including relationship building, negotiation strategies, and crisis management in diplomatic contexts.
who_should_attend: You are a public officer new to or recently assigned to roles involving international engagement across whole-of-government (less than three (3) years in an IR role). Officers must be Singaporean citizens to be eligible for this programme.

title: Ops-Tech Integration for Service Directors
description:
(Do note that CROTI10 was the previous code for this programme)

As a Service Director, are you increasingly looking for ways to innovate service delivery using technology? Implementing such innovations would require you to think about how to bridge operations and technical aspects, and to redesign your processes with considerations for technology affordances.

Join us in this programme to experience frameworks and tools that support closer collaborations between ops and tech teams to drive tech-enabled service innovation. Connect with industry experts, fellow Service Directors, and try your hand at solving real-life ops-tech integration issues!

This programme is organised in partnership with Singapore Management University, Executive Development (SMU-ExD), and supports the Skills for Digital Transformation (SDT) framework for Directors.
 

The programme is held at SMU's Campus. Details of the finalised location will be provided in LEARN DC upon placement.
learning_outcomes:
Discover opportunities for technology-enabled solutions in service operations
Explore methods to facilitate a user-centred and collaborative culture, to accelerate service delivery innovations effectively with technology partners
Understand the need for agility in balancing the user perspective with service provider capabilities and constraints in implementing technology-enabled innovations
who_should_attend: You are a Director or Deputy Director who leads the service or operations function in your organisation.

title: Spokesmanship - 2-day
description:
(Do note that CRSPM11 was the previous code for this programme)

 

Arm the media with well-crafted messages that are clear and concise, communicating your corporate messaging effectively. Understand how to engage the media from the perspective of a journalist. 

 

Media spokespersons need to be armed with the techniques, skills and knowledge in managing the media, regardless of the situation and the method of engagement. Strategic management of the media boosts brand and image of corporations. 

 

Deploying an effective training methodology of reinforcing knowledge and skills through hands-on practicum in a professional, realistic environment, this workshop is designed to impart participants with the tools, techniques, knowledge and skills in managing the media effectively, regardless of way the media engages them. 

 

This training is customised to suit the unique training needs of individual media spokespersons so that they will be able to engage the media looking good, sounding
learning_outcomes:
Gain insights to the basic dos and don’ts of virtual and in-person media engagement platforms
Be equipped with the essential skills of an effective media spokesperson such as techniques on facing the camera, screen presence, crafting bridging statements and key messages
Craft effective self-introductions
Plan and prepare how to face the media in the event of a crisis
Practice managing crisis communications through a camera simulation exercise
Gain tips and tricks on speech and presentation Delivery
Handle door-stop interviews
Manage media conferences/ press briefings
Gain experience through hands-on TV interviews and virtual interviews simulation exercises

title: OD Practitioner Foundation Programme
description:
(Do note that MSOD101 was the previous code for this programme)

Organisation Development (OD) is an increasingly influential field of practice in the Singapore Public Service - one that is employed to support organisational transformation, manage complex change, and enhance organisational performance, while ensuring that employees stay engaged in the process.

If you have just joined the OD function and would like to learn more about how you, as an OD practitioner, can contribute to your organisation's health and effectiveness, this is the programme for you.

In this updated version of the OD Practitioner Foundation Programme, you will gain an overview of the OD practice field, its history, and how it has evolved in the Singapore Public Service. You will undergo a unique, immersive experiential exercise, and learn a proven methodology that enables you to make sense of the complexities of organisational behaviour and the actions you can take to make a significant impact. The programme
learning_outcomes:
Gain an overview of the OD practice field, its history and how it has evolved in the Singapore Public Service
Develop insights into seeing organisations as systems, and some of the common and predictable challenges they face
Appreciate how OD practice can contribute towards organisational health and performance
Grow your network with the OD practitioner community in the Public Service
who_should_attend: You are an OD practitioner who is interested to learn the definition and practices of OD, the OD cycle and the role of OD practitioners in organisations. This programme is also recommended for officers involved in the Public Service Transformation (PST), Organisational Excellence (OE) and organisational change.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Design Play! for Support Officers
description:
(Do note that CRDSOVL was the previous code for this programme)

Ever wondered about the importance of Design Thinking and how to apply it in the workplace? Design Thinking is more than attractive design, it is about understanding and addressing your users’ needs to achieve impactful outcomes.

As public officers, it is important to empathise with our stakeholders and citizens so that we are able to uncover unmet needs and effectively address them. Design Thinking will teach you how to view issues from different perspectives, how to produce and experiment with ideas to improve work processes and service delivery.

If you are a Support Officer, sign up for this introductory programme now to learn more about the human-centred design process and how it can be relevant to your work!
learning_outcomes:
Describe the human-centred design methodology, and understand how it relates to problem-solving at work
Recognise the importance of empathy with users through tools like Jobs-To-Be-Done and journey mapping
Discover how to use storytelling to present a cohesive concept to stakeholders
Demonstrate an iterative mindset when doing prototyping and user testing
who_should_attend: You are a support officer who is interested in grasping the basics of human-centred design methodologies and mindset

title: Balancing Service With Authority
description:
The citizenry landscape has dramatically changed and an enforcement officer in the current climate needs to be tenacious and firm, yet sensitive and empathetic to citizens. You must be able to think on your feet and remain calm in critical situations, and also be proactive and sincere when engaging the community. Today’s changing landscape challenges all levels of enforcement officers to balance their core duty, which requires the portrayal of ‘hardness’, with the need to portray their ‘softer’ side in order to connect better with citizens.  

This face-to-face programme focuses on collaborative problem solving and cross-agency exchanges. You will benefit from the knowledge exchanges on the current enforcement issues and understand how to balance the hard and soft approaches. You will also learn how to build deeper public trust by projecting a more humane approach in service delivery, albeit facing tough citizens.
learning_outcomes:
Demonstrate empathy and authority when carrying out enforcement work
Display firmness and respect during enforcement delivery
Maintain your composure when faced with confrontation or accusations
Keep a consistent front when handling repeated unreasonable requests
Apply techniques to better understand the needs and expectations of members of the public
who_should_attend:
You are a frontline enforcement officer who wish to equip yourself with the appropriate enforcement engagements and approaches to deal with complex enforcement issues. 

This programme is for Singapore Public Officers only.

title: Multipliers: Bringing Out the Best in Your People
description:
(Do note that CRMULTS was the previous code for this programme)

Have you ever wondered why some leaders drain “intelligence” while others make the people around them “smarter”? Why do some leaders attract talent, while other leaders repel them? Now imagine if you could get at least 2x the intelligence and contribution from your teams. What could effective performance and results look like?

In today’s challenging landscape, leaders everywhere are facing increasing pressures to create more with less – to navigate uncertainty, lead change and build future-readiness in both their teams and themselves. We need all the intelligence and contribution from every team member to thrive and win.

Join us in this impactful workshop to master the Multipliers mindset and behaviors in real-life scenarios. Engage in simulations, discussions, activities, and experiments to uncover your multiplying and diminishing behaviors. Learn how to amplify the growth and success of your people and teams effective
learning_outcomes:
Discover the mindsets and practices that set Multiplier leaders apart from Diminisher leaders.
Enhance awareness of your behaviours that multiply or unintentionally diminish the potential of your people and teams.
who_should_attend: You are a Director or an experienced Deputy Director looking to multiply the potential of your people and teams to bring about better performance.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Wayfinding Career Fitness for your Team
description:
(Do note that CRWCTVL was the previous code for this programme)

Directors play an essential role in developing the careers of officers. In this webinar we will explore the role of Directors in building and sustaining an environment that enables and empowers officers to develop their careers. As a supervisor yourself, discover how you can have meaningful and impact career development conversations with officers.
learning_outcomes:
Discern your role as a leader to drive purposeful career conversations in your team that spark motivation, identifies growth opportunities, and brings clarity to development paths
Understand common career challenges and apply strategies to overcome them
Identify career development resources for the team, to create practical next steps

title: Managing Sousveillance in Enforcement
description: The proliferation and ubiquity of smart devices have forever changed the enforcement landscape. In particular, it has led to the rise of "sousveillance", where members of the public have no qualms recording audio and visual recordings of officers in action. Such recordings often find their way to social media, which places agency reputations at risk and imposes additional stress on the officers. In addition, there is a growing expectation for officers to fulfil dual functions of service and enforcement, which may not always be appreciated by the general public and can lead to misunderstandings being amplified by sousveillance. Officers need to thus keep themselves updated on the best ways to handle sousveillance situations in a way that focuses not only on deescalation, but also reduces the potential scope and reach on social media. Officers will also benefit from being made aware of healthy responses available to cope with the unique stressors resulting from by being the target of sou
learning_outcomes:
Describe sousveillance and its impact on enforcement officers and agencies
Describe a variety of constructive behavioural responses in a sousveillance situation
Have increased self-awareness of their preferred behavioural responses when faced with a conflict and a wider range of options
Identify the characteristics of a media issue and the impact on the organisation, and explain the role of enforcement officers in media communication
Manage affected officer’s emotions and actions in response to social media crisis and sousveillance
Recall effective communications strategies for officers responding to media/citizens/businesses on the ground and escalation of ground cases
who_should_attend: Public officers who engage with members of public in regulatory, compliance, enforcement and operational job functions.

title: Negotiations and Networking Course
description:
(Do note that CRNNC10 was the previous code for this programme)

Through the course, participants will be equipped with the necessary skills and knowledge to:

(a) negotiate at and facilitate intergovernmental meetings; and

(b) build and maintain networks in an international context.

(c) Participants will develop an understanding of negotiation principles and strategies, as well as facilitation techniques for intergovernmental meetings, with a focus on practical application through role-playing exercises. .
learning_outcomes:
⁠Negotiate effectively in bilateral and multilateral (UN/ASEAN) settings, while taking into account cultural nuances and dynamics as well as reconciling the interests of different stakeholders.
Cultivate partnerships and collaborations at international and regional platforms (e.g., Friends of the Chair, Like-minded Groups).
Negotiate and draft agreements and documents (e.g. statements and resolutions) while advancing Singapore’s interests and defending our redlines.
Facilitate effectively to create relevance to others, bearing in mind the overarching goal of advancing and defending Singapore’s national interests.
Understanding the positions and interests of various counterparts at multilateral fora, and developing the acumen to assess who to cultivate and which levers to apply to achieve desired outcomes. 
Learn how to build and maintain international networks.
Engage stakeholders in bilateral and/or multilateral settings, taking into account cross-cultural sensitivities and politicall
who_should_attend: Junior public officers with Category 1 security clearance, handling international relations-related work as part of their portfolio.

title: [Virtual Classroom] E-Accessibility for an Inclusive Service Delivery
description:
(Do note that CREISVL was the previous code for this programme)

With increased digitalisation, more transactions, including public services, are moving online. However, persons with disabilities may find themselves unable to access important information or perform digital transactions if the content and platforms are not accessible to them.

 

 

In partnership with SG Enable, this programme introduces you to e-accessibility and the different types of assistive technology. You will gain a better understanding of the business case of making your digital content accessible. Through sharing of best practices, guidelines and experiential activities, learn the importance of e-accessibility and discover how you can make your agency’s content e-accessible.
learning_outcomes:
Gain awareness of the business case for e-accessibility
Understand the guidelines, codes and practices for e-accessibility
Learn through hands-on experience with e-accessibility tools and practice developing a basic actionable plan
who_should_attend: Officers in Service Policy, Standards and Partnership, Customer Experience Strategy and Digital Services Management job functions who are looking at enhancing digital content to allow persons with disabilities to access content easily.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Mediation Skills for Public Officers
description: As a service or enforcement officer, you will need to be equipped with mediation skills to resolve common conflicts. In this programme, you will be introduced to the art of mediation, and learn techniques and skills that you can apply to resolve conflicts between disputing parties. You will have the opportunity to apply the skills you learn through virtual role-playing as a mediator, enabling you to experience firsthand how different conflicts can be mediated or resolved.
learning_outcomes:
Discover your preferred mode of conflict resolution and areas for improvement
Discuss about the structure of conflicts and how to apply this in practice
Develop confidence in your ability to handle conflicts and difficult parties
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer involved in service or enforcement, and you regularly interact with members of the public.

title: Understanding ASEAN
description:
(Do note that CRUNA10 was the previous code for this programme)

What is Singapore's relationship with ASEAN? How can Singapore safeguard and advance its national interests through ASEAN?

In this programme, you will develop a deeper understanding of ASEAN, including its history, principles and structural frameworks. You will gain greater insight into the challenges and opportunities facing ASEAN, and how these relate to Singapore’s national interests. You will gain skills and knowledge to effectively engage with regional counterparts within ASEAN . You will also benefit from valuable insights from senior government officials and experienced ASEAN practitioners, who will share their expertise on understanding and navigating ASEAN. 

About The Speakers

Besides hearing from speakers from the S. Rajaratnam School of International Studies (RSIS), you will also hear from experienced diplomats like Mr Ong Keng Yong, currently Executive Deputy Chairman of RSIS, NTU and formerly ASEAN Secreta
learning_outcomes:
• DESCRIBE Singapore’s interests in ASEAN 
• EXPLAIN ASEAN’s history, principles and objectives, as well as mechanisms, and structures
• EXPLAIN the roles of ASEAN’s various platforms in promoting cooperation among its member states and external partners
• DISCUSS impacts of regional and international developments on Singapore and ASEAN


*This programme will not cover the specifics of each country in Southeast Asia. Rather, it will focus on ASEAN, the regional platform.
who_should_attend: You are a junior to mid-level public officer with some experience dealing with or working with ASEAN or international relations.

title: Directors Leadership Series
description:
(Do note that MSDLS was the previous code for this programme)

Launched in November 2025, the Directors Leadership Series(DLS), a first of its kind in CSC’s programmes, is designed to support experienced Directors like yourself in your ongoing leadership and learning journey.

The DLS is for those who have been in the Director role for several years in the Public Service and are thinking about what lies ahead for themselves and their leadership amidst the changing world. 

You may sense a growing desire to carve out time and space from work to take stock of where one is. Perhaps something deeper is calling you to lead from a different place or to contribute in a different way? Perhaps meaning, impact and fulfillment should look different from here?

If this resonates, the DLS may be the learning experience for you!
learning_outcomes:
The DLS is a transformative learning series that offers you an exciting opportunity to

Uplift your leadership competencies to meet the demands of this changing world
Support you in discovering and creating a purposeful next chapter in your continued leadership journey
Advocate a Public Service Leadership identity and cultivate a Director-level community.

The DLS goes beyond a typical programme and focuses on leading from within,  community formation and leadership skills enhancement that applies across contexts and functions.
who_should_attend:
You are a seasoned MX9-equivalent officer who has served as a Director for 6-12 years, demonstrating substantial leadership experience in at least one Director-level posting. While you have built your career independently of formal talent schemes like the PSLP, Administrative Service, or agency-specific programmes, you have not attended any significant developmental opportunity in the last two years (e.g., sponsored post-grad studies, milestone programmes - DDE, GLP, HRLP etc., executive coaching).

You are now keen to uplift your leadership competency and affirm your leadership identity and purpose, alongside a community of like-minded Directors. You are open to pause to think deeply and do some self-work to discover new possibilities; experiment with new knowledge, techniques and habits; and be curious to relate to and learn from others on a similar journey as you.

title: Communicating with Impact - A Clinic
description: From talking one-on-one to leading a Town Hall, we’re all expected to bring our best selves to the conversation – but some of these situations are more difficult than others. How do we inspire others instead of just stating facts or our opinions or ideas? How do we step into difficult conversations when we aren’t comfortable doing it or we don’t really know how to do it well? How do we facilitate sensitive conversations, disagreements and conflict between people? How do we develop our own leadership presence to support bringing our best selves into each conversation? This is a practical clinic for learning and practicing ways to communicate with greater impact.
learning_outcomes:
Learn and practise the skills to be inspirational: develop a vision, transform it into a compelling message, and use storytelling to make it memorable
Understand and practise handling difficult conversations, conflict and disagreements in a way that minimises negative feelings and impact
Understand and practise connecting with oneself to create the foundation for leadership presence, creating a vision and staying centered even when others waver
who_should_attend:
Directors and above 

Trainers
Cathy Johnson
This is Cathy Johnson, a dynamic leadership trainer with over three decades of international experience and a passion for transforming leaders through impactful, practical training. She holds an MBA from the Kenan-Flagler Business School, and is an ICF-certified professional coach, a Certified Speaking Professional, and an experienced facilitator who has worked extensively throughout Asia.

Over the past decade Cathy has served as an Associate Facilitator with CSC, collaborating with various public agencies such as the Attorney-General’s Chambers, Singapore Tourism Board, Monetary Authority of Singapore, Housing and Development Board, and the Singapore Police Force.

Cathy’s training methodology prioritises practical, experiential learning that can be readily applied in the workplace. Her expertise includes supporting leaders in developing executive presence, managing difficult conversations, delivering compelling presentations, and inspirin

title: [Virtual Classroom] Navigating Career Fitness for Your Staff
description:
(Do note that CRNCSVL was the previous code for this programme)

In this session, we will explore how managers can partner effectively in their officers' career development, creating value for both individuals and organisation. Through this session, supervisors will learn basic career coaching techniques to transform everyday workplace interactions into meaningful development conversations, and guide their staff to explore career aspirations, leverage their strengths, and set clear development goals.

title: Data Analytics Clinic (For In-house only)
description:
(Do note that CRDAC10 was the previous code for this programme)

Ready to bridge the gap between learning and real-world application?

You've mastered the fundamentals through training and courses. Now it's time to apply those skills to the complexity of actual business challenges. Our Data Analytics Clinic is designed for teams who are ready to move beyond generic learning scenarios and tackle their real data projects with confidence.

How it works:

Our half-day clinic pairs your team with seasoned data analytics consultants who work directly alongside your people, providing hands-on guidance through your actual data challenges. Think of it as personalised coaching for your specific analytical needs—no textbook examples, no simulations, just focused support for the problems you're actually facing.

Examples of challenges:

Complex datasets that don't fit standard analytical approaches 
Technical roadblocks stalling your data projects 
Unstructured analysis that isn't delivering clear
who_should_attend:
You are a Singapore Public officer whose team works with data and you're looking to bridge the gap between training and real-world application. You want expert guidance to tackle your actual data challenges and develop your team's analytical skills through hands-on coaching.

About

title: Masterclass in 6 Conditions for Team Effectiveness
description:
Ready to advance your OD practice and drive team effectiveness? Sign up here.

This comprehensive two-module certification masterclass equips experienced OD practitioners with cutting-edge tools to diagnose and enhance team effectiveness across the public service. Built on the evidence-based 6 Team Conditions (6TC) model and Team Diagnostic Survey (TDS), you'll gain both foundational knowledge and advanced coaching capabilities to drive meaningful team transformation. Developed at Harvard University by world-renowned experts on teams and teaming – Dr. Richard Hackman, Dr. Ruth Wageman and Dr. Erin Lehman – the 6 Team Conditions and TDS predict up to 80% of a team's effectiveness.

To maximise your learning impact, this advanced-level masterclass will be delivered through a series of mixed modalities. To kick-start your learning journey, you will gain access to the 6 Team Conditions e-learning portal, which introduces you to the science of team effectiveness and provides the conceptual
learning_outcomes:
Understand what team effectiveness is and how to measure it
Learn about the 6 Conditions and how they influence behaviour, performance, and results in teams
Know how to debrief a Team Diagnostic Survey (TDS) with team leaders and teams
Learn how to put in place the 6 Conditions using a range of artful tools and techniques
Work on cases to practise facilitation and coaching skills when debriefing TDS reports, designing team launches, and facilitating team coaching interventions
Receive peer and instructor feedback to enhance your practice

 

Why This Masterclass?

Evidence-based methodology proven in public sector contexts
Progressive certification pathway from foundation to advanced practice
Builds internal capability for sustainable team development
Combines virtual accessibility with intensive face-to-face skill building
Life-time access to a comprehensive advanced-level tools library of team effectiveness interventions 

 

Trainers

 

Dr. Melissa J. Sayer, MBA

Melissa is the Man
who_should_attend:
You are an Organisation Development practitioner in the Singapore Public Service seeking to deepen your expertise in diagnosing and facilitating team effectiveness using an evidence-based methodology and advanced team coaching capabilities. This advanced masterclass will prepare you to lead team development initiatives within and across government agencies to create system-wide impact.

 

Prerequisites 

The programme is designed for OD practitioners who have facilitation experience (departmental / divisional level and above), preferably with leadership teams OR coaching experience. To qualify for the masterclass, you will need to have attended the OD Practitioner Foundation Programme (MSOD101) and demonstrate prior experience facilitating or coaching teams. You may also apply if you are stepping into a team facilitator/coach role and have a specific scenario for application. Please complete the following form fully, including your facilitation or coaching experience, to apply for thi

title: [Virtual Classroom] Managing Public with Mental Health Issues
description:
(Do note that CRMCMVL was the previous code for this programme)

Have you ever faced a member of the public (MoP) who was behaving strangely but you are not sure if it was because he/she had any underlying mental health issues and you were thus at a loss as to how you should handle the situation? Understand how best to handle MoPs with mental health issues, and participate in group discussions on scenarios and virtual role playing.
learning_outcomes: Identify, evaluate and manage various mental illnesses/disorders encountered in their workplaces
who_should_attend: You are a public officer in service operations functions and engaged in public-facing role.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Engaging Customers Professionally Face-to-Face
description:
(Do note that CRFCSVL was the previous code for this programme)

Despite the proliferation of technology and digitalisation, face-to-face interactions remain a critical touchpoint between public agencies and customers. It is through these interactions that connections are built, and trust established, with members of the public. Therefore, it is critical for us to have the skills to ensure each experience leaves a lasting, positive impression on our customers, and when faced with difficult situations, manage your emotions, so that you remain calm, non-judgemental and respond with thoughtful words and behaviours.
learning_outcomes:
Recall CARE Service Principles of the Singapore Public Service
Describe and apply quality service standards along the 4R process in face-to-face situations
Explain the connections between behaviour and impressions created
List ways to promote green service behaviour
Demonstrate positive vocal and visual skills when delivering service
Remain calm and manage behavioural responses to challenging service situations
Serve with firmness during difficult service encounters
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who interacts with external customers.

title: Vetting with Empathy
description:
(Do note that CREEV10 was the previous code for this programme)

 

Email interactions are opportunities to foster positive and impactful relationships with the public. It is thus important for supervisors to know what to look out for when vetting email replies drafted by their team. In this course, learn about the PAC-SSS framework and how to apply key empathy principles to email replies so that you can help your team build trust and rapport with your audience. You will also pick up tips on how you can use GenAI tools to enhance your vetting work. 

Please note that there will be pre-course assignments before the session.
learning_outcomes:
Apply the PAC-SSS framework in vetting replies
Evaluate email replies using GenAI tools to incorporate appropriate empathy
Adopt a personalised writing approach that displays understanding of the issue at hand when vetting
Develop a systematic approach to vet your officers' written communications
who_should_attend: You are a first-line manager or equivalent who need to vet written correspondences to the public.

title: Graduate Certificate in Organisation Development
description:
(Do note that MSGCOD was the previous code for this programme)

Organisation Development (OD) capabilities are increasingly critical to sustaining organisational performance and health, especially as growth, disruptions and constant change accelerate. By helping organisations flourish through their people, OD builds transformative capacity and stronger organisations in volatile and complex environments.

CSC’s OD milestone programmes are structured to enable dynamic, collaborative learning grounded in real and relatable Public Service contexts. They combine in-person classroom and self-directed learning to build both the depth and breadth of expertise required of an effective OD practitioner.

Accredited by Civil Service College, the Graduate Certificate in Organisation Development Programme is designed with a strong focus on practical workplace application. It features a combination of plenary sessions, experiential components, small learning groups and assignments to broaden perspect
learning_outcomes:
Build your capability and credibility as an Organisational Development (OD) practitioner by designing and delivering effective OD interventions that drive positive change within your organisation.
Enhance your self-awareness and interpersonal skills to become a more effective OD practitioner by identifying and leveraging your strengths, addressing your developmental needs, and understanding the impact of your actions on others.
Expand your knowledge of OD theories and frameworks and apply them to drive positive change in areas such as organisational change, team development, group dynamics, and stakeholder engagement.
Apply a systemic approach to organisational diagnostics by using relevant examples and context from the public service to identify and address underlying issues that impact organisational effectiveness. 
Design and implement system-wide change efforts using knowledge in organisation design, complexity and change.
who_should_attend:
Who should attend: You are an Organisation Development practitioner in the Singapore Public Service. You would like to develop your competence and confidence to support your leaders in achieving strategic outcomes and enhancing organisational health. This learning opportunity is also open to officers who play an OD role in other functions. 

Entry Requirement: To be eligible for the Graduate Certificate in Organisation Development Programme, you must have completed the OD Practitioner Foundation Programme (MSOD101) and demonstrate at least two years of relevant experience in organisation development or related functions.

title: Foundational Workshop for Enforcement Supervisors
description:
(Do note that CRFWS10 was the previous code for this programme)

Are you leading a team of frontline enforcement officer? What knowledge and mindset do you need to have in order to effectively lead your team? In this foundational programme, you will learn about governance, investigation and incident management theories, and be introduced to methods that will enable you to better lead frontline officers in performing their duties.
learning_outcomes:
Review enforcement implementation plans (including strategies, activities and resources) to optimise desired outcomes while maintaining a citizen-centric perspective
Create feedback loops between enforcement implementation and regulation with the aim of enhancing compliance
Ensure all evidence related to the case are appropriately obtained, documented, processed, and stored in accordance with agency policy and procedures to support case resolution
Assess and coach officers to respond professionally to difficult and exceptional incidents that disrupt daily operational activities (e.g. major threats to officer, agency, stakeholders and/ or general public)
Establish processes and systems, and adjust resources (e.g. manpower and assets, outsourced partners) to achieve enforcement outcomes
Coach officers to use appropriate interviewing techniques to obtain important information about the incident
Support officers to carry out case management processes with professionalism and integrity
who_should_attend: Supervisors who engage with members of public in regulatory, compliance, enforcement and operational job functions.

title: Foundational Workshop for Frontline Enforcement Officers
description:
(Do note that CRFWE13 was the previous code for this programme)

 

 

 

Join us at this one-day workshop to dive in-depth into the key concepts about the legal aspects of enforcement in Singapore, frontline incident management, investigative and crime scene management skills, and the basics of operation planning.

This introductory workshop aims to equip you with the necessary knowledge and skills to perform your duties well. Please complete the 1.5 hours e-learning on LEARN before coming for the workshop.
learning_outcomes:
Respond professionally to difficult and exceptional incidents that disrupt daily operational activities (e.g. major threats to officers, agency, stakeholders and/or the general public)
Plan and/or execute daily operations according to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) to optimise enforcement functions
Appreciate different privacy laws, security standards, and frameworks across countries and jurisdictions
Use appropriate interviewing techniques to obtain important information about the incident
Investigate potential enforcement violations (online and offline) by collecting and analysing evidence while considering the facts of the case
Carry out each step in the case management process with professionalism and integrity
who_should_attend: Public officers who engage with members of public in regulatory, compliance, enforcement and operational job functions.

title: Authentic Leadership Conversations for Managers
description:
(Do note that CRALCM was the previous code for this programme)

Managing difficult or uncomfortable discussions with direct reports, peers, or reporting officers can be a sensitive undertaking, especially when addressing delicate matters, diverse viewpoints, and intense emotions.


The Authentic Leadership Conversations for Officers programme provides conceptual models and concrete skills in carrying out authentic and impactful conversations that will help you address high-stakes conversations well.


It is a powerful, highly participative and practical programme that uses role-plays and real work situations commonly faced by learners. The programme will provide you with new insights and practical skills that you can apply immediately to tap into the power of conversations to transform the organisational culture for better engagement and alignment, translating to significant results.
learning_outcomes:
Improve the quality of conversation with all stakeholders - management, peers, team members, members of the public etc. 
Consciously build psychological safety with “point easy culture” so that all can speak openly and safely in a respectful manner.
Express opinions candidly on sensitive matters in a way that will not aggravate the situation 
Address difficult and sensitive situations with respect, authenticity and compassion.
Manage relationships to increase trust levels and improve relationship.
Identify potential problems or conflict situations early and undertake possible interventions.
Carry out difficult high stakes conversations with various stakeholders.
Take action to materialise matters discussed during conversations
who_should_attend: You are a manager who regularly engages in conversations with direct reports, peers, or reporting officers. It is particularly beneficial for those who need to address sensitive issues, manage diverse viewpoints, and navigate emotionally charged situations in their professional roles. It is also suitable for managers seeking to enhance their leadership communication skills, improve team dynamics, and foster a more engaged and aligned organisational culture.

title: 2-Day Protocol Workshop For Officers Sent On Liaison Duties
description:
(Do note that CRPWLO2 was the previous code for this programme)

As a liaison officer/planner, you will need to possess in-depth protocol skills and knowledge in order to carry out your responsibilities effectively. You will need to be aware of cross-cultural sensitivities, taboos and mannerisms, so as to respond and carry out your duties in a professional manner. Besides possessing fundamental protocol knowledge, you will also be required to understand and carry out specific protocol that goes into planning and handling an event, such as VIP management, liaison organisation and gift handling.

This two-day programme aims to equip you with an in-depth training of protocol knowledge and skills needed for you to carry out your duties as a liaison officer/event planner. In this programme, you will be covering both the core subject of VIP handling, liaison and comms (item no.7) as well as a more specific understanding of VIP management (item no.9). The core subject of VIP handling covers t
learning_outcomes:
Comprehend and practise principles of protocol and business etiquette, and be good minders to VVIPs and overseas guests at events and functions  
Identify and demonstrate cross-cultural sensitivities and mannerisms towards guests  
Extend appropriate courtesies in greeting, receiving, and sending off guests with correct formalities
who_should_attend: You have been or are likely to be entrusted with the responsibility to look after VVIPs and foreign guests. This workshop is open to Singapore Public Officers only.

title: 1-Day Protocol Workshop For Officers Sent On Liaison Duties
description:
(Do note that CRPWLO3 was the previous code for this programme)

As a new liaison officer, you will be required to possess fundamental protocol knowledge to carry out your responsibilities effectively. You will need to be aware of cultural sensitivities, taboos and mannerisms, so as to respond and carry out your duties in a professional manner.

This one-day programme is suitable for new liaison officers who wish to have a foundational protocol training, by introducing the key protocol skills needed for officers to exercise their liaison duties professionally.

Note: Please refer to this diagram illustrating the difference between the 1-day and 2-day Protocol Workshop For Officers Sent On Liaison Duties.
learning_outcomes:
Comprehend and practise principles of protocol and business etiquette, and be good minders to VVIPs and overseas guests at events and functions  
Identify and demonstrate cross-cultural sensitivities and mannerisms towards guests  
Extend appropriate courtesies in greeting, receiving, and sending off guests with correct formalities
who_should_attend: You have been or are likely to be entrusted with the responsibility to look after VVIPs and foreign guests. This workshop is open to Singapore Public Officers only.

title: Data Storytelling - Making Data Speak
description:
(Do note that CRDST10 was the previous code for this programme)

“Data are just summaries of thousands of stories - tell a few of those stories to make the data meaningful.” Dan Heath, Duke University Fellow.

While there is ample supply of hard skills and capabilities in many analytics functions, the softer skills of contextualisation and storytelling are in much shorter supply. Data storytelling - specifically how to tell a concise, coherent, and compelling story from the data to drive decisions and actions - is probably one of the most overlooked and undervalued aspects along the analytics value chain, critical in delivering the “last mile” of value.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the analytics value chain and the role Data Storytelling plays in it.
Discuss the key principles behind developing and delivering a concise, coherent, and compelling story from the data.
Use storyboarding techniques to organise and re-organise your data story into a coherent and persuasive narrative.
Select the right data visualisation to convey the right messages in your data story.
Translate the storyboard into a persuasive narrative that engages audience.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public officer who is adept at analysing data and finding solutions, but struggle with conveying your data-driven insights and proposed solutions to decision makers. This programme will equip you with the right skills and tools to address gaps in the final mile of value in analytics. Officers with experience in data visualisation/data analysis and/or having an interest in data storytelling would find this programme useful.

title: Leading and transforming at scale: Building blueprints for impact
description:
(Do note that CRLTS was the previous code for this programme)

Successful transformations require strong sponsorship, engaging leadership, clear directions and concrete plans. Only then can a transformation lead to the intended substantive and systemic changes in our work and the way we work in our organisations. Hence, this programme - Leading and Transforming at Scale: Building Blueprints for Impact - will equip you with the practical knowledge, frameworks, tools and mindsets to lead your organisation’s transformation. The contents build on the foundation of our previous transformation programme and now integrate a systemic orientation, an organisation frame to scale up, and the transformation leadership role.

In the programme, you will build a blueprint to map the plans and shifts needed for your real-life transformation project. In addition, you will learn and apply concepts and templates for implementation along the different stages of transformation. You will also receive team c
learning_outcomes:
By the end of the programme, you will be able to effectively lead your current and future transformation efforts with the following:

Apply the CSC-ILOD transformation framework and approach to your organisation’s transformation project
Develop concrete blueprints for driving and managing transformation
Identify key actions for shifts and intervention
Engage your key sponsors to secure commitment to drive the transformation sustainably
who_should_attend: You are a leader or staff tasked with driving transformation or projects supporting your agency’s transformation. For maximum effectiveness of the programme, it is important for the project team leader (e.g. Transformation director) to attend the programme with key members of the change team.

title: Finance Development Programme
description:
(Do note that MSFDP was the previous code for this programme)

The Finance Development Programme (FDP) is a milestone programme jointly developed by MOF Finance and Procurement Academy (FPA) and Civil Service College (CSC) for junior finance officers who are starting out their careers in the public service.

This five-day programme will help you better understand your role in Public Service Finance and build peer networks within the Public Service Finance community. The programme is designed to bridge classroom learning with workplace application, supported by curated interventions that guide you in translating new insights into practice.

You are required to complete the Finance Foundation Programme (FFP) as a pre-requisite to this programme.
learning_outcomes:
By the end of the programme, participants will be able to:

Articulate  strategic  Whole-of-Government  priorities  and  align  them  with respective agency financial objectives
Explain how government finance components interconnect and influence government decision-making
Analyse the critical role of finance officers in driving transformation and providing strategic support
Apply essential finance knowledge in key Finance functions, operations and policy formulation required of a future government finance officer
Examine financial processes to identify improvement opportunities that align with organisational and government finance objectives
who_should_attend: By nomination only. This programme is designed for junior Public Finance officers with less than 3 years of working experience in the Public Service. Participants must have consistently good performance and be identified with potential to take on middle management roles by nominating agencies.

title: Data Cleaning & Visualisation for Support Officers
description:
(Do note that CRDCV10 was the previous code for this programme)

With the abundance of data at your fingertips, you may have spent many hours trying to clean up and make sense of untidy data. Your stakeholders may also have requested for you to better visualise and present data to support business recommendations. 

In this hands-on programme* supported by real-life case studies, you will use Microsoft Excel tools and gain foundational skills on how to clean and visualise data optimally and present a cohesive narrative that can influence business decisions. 

 

*This is an intermediate programme on data cleaning and visualisation for support officers only.
learning_outcomes:
Appreciate the role of data cleaning and visualisation in the analytics value chain 
Recognise the importance of structuring data in a tidy data format for data analysis and visualisation 
Appreciate key concepts and principles of data visualisation to generate reports 
Perform the steps of data cleaning and visualisation on a dataset using Microsoft Excel add-ins and tools 
Build interactive data reports using visualisation tools like Pivot tables 
Form a cohesive narrative and present data visually for actionable business insights
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer in a supporting role (MX13(I), MX14, MX15 and below) who is keen to strengthen your data analytics skills to clean and visualise data, and support the data work required to make better decisions.

title: The AIBots Lab
description:
Ready to evolve from curious observers into skilled bot architects? This comprehensive programme begins with participants developing problem statements that form the foundation of your AIBots solutions. You'll then engage in hands-on prototyping, finetuning advanced settings and features that elevate your AIBots performance.

Through collaborative troubleshooting sessions and peer-to-peer learning, you'll learn to optimise your bot whilst benefiting from diverse perspectives that improve bot functionality and user experience. By the end of this intensive one-day programme, you'll be equipped with the technical skills and confidence necessary to implement effective AIBot solutions within your agencies.

This is an 8-hour classroom programme (1 day) with a 2-hour pre-workshop eLearning component. The eLearning will be shared upon successful registration.

Learners would need to bring their own GSIB laptops with internet access for this programme. (note: SE-cards and S-GSIB machines are n
learning_outcomes:
By the end of this workshop, participants will be able to:  

Refine the problem statement for your AIBots use case
Prototype (or build) a functional bot using basic and relevant advanced features in AIBots
Troubleshoot and resolve bot issues using RAG assessment and job aids
Evaluate the bot's performance and make necessary revisions.
who_should_attend: This programme is open to all Singapore Public Officers seeking to develop AI bot solutions to improve their work processes.

title: RPA for Personal Productivity
description:
(Do note that CRRPAPP was the previous code for this programme)

Have you found yourself doing repetitive work that is manual, tedious and takes up more time than it should? Robotic Process Automation (RPA) can help you with this! While RPA is traditionally viewed as an area of expertise for tech officers, there is an increasing number of tools for business users to easily adopt RPA for routine tasks.

“The most effective way to improve productivity is to eliminate work.”

– William E. Conway, Jr.

In this programme, explore suitable processes for automation and best practices for RPA implementation. There is also hands-on practice, where you can try your hand at building RPA bots for different use cases.
learning_outcomes:
Appreciate the automation journey and how RPA can be used for personal productivity
Identify suitable work processes for automation and whether process improvement is required
Describe the RPA implementation approach and best practices
Build RPA bots using UiPath StudioX and be comfortable in experimenting with the software’s functionalities
