Note: Laptops installed with the UiPath StudioX software will be provided for all participants during the programme.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who wants to adopt RPA to improve personal productivity.

title: Introduction to Python Programming
description:
(Do note that CRIPP10 was the previous code for this programme)

 

Python programming is a powerful one-stop tool that many are tapping on to not only solve complex data problems quickly and simply, but also enhance solutions. Thanks to Python’s low barriers to entry and versatility, even non-programmers can easily get a glimpse into the many possible fields where Python is able to help you maximise productivity.

Through hands-on exercises and resources in this programme, discover how to write simple programs that can assist you with data visualisation and developing automation for everyday tasks at work.
learning_outcomes:
Identify and explore automation using Python to improve process efficiency and service delivery.
Apply visualisation techniques using Python to convey insights and recommendations.
Execute basic descriptive analysis (mean, median, min, max, mode).
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who processes data and is keen to learn how to code with Python. You wish to learn how to programme daily tasks for automation and how to use a simple one-stop solution to visualise complex datasets. This programme is suitable for complete beginners with no prior programming knowledge. If you already have experience in another language, this programme will help you see the parallels between Python and the language that you already know. If you haven’t been programming in a while, this programme can be a good refresher of your dormant knowledge.

title: The Essence Of Lean Thinking
description:
(Do note that CRTET10 was the previous code for this programme)

In this current environment of increasing demand and limited resources, how can public agencies optimise resources and eliminate wastes to drive process and service improvements in the public sector?

Among process improvement methodologies, Lean is known for its simplicity to learn and apply in work processes. In this one-day programme, you will learn about Lean Thinking and its principles, particularly in reducing waste. There will also be hands-on opportunities to integrate knowledge of key Lean concepts, principles and tools.

Sign up for this workshop now to learn more about how Lean concepts and tools can be applicable in your work.
learning_outcomes:
Explain Lean thinking and its principles
Recognise and reduce wastes in processes
Identify common Lean tools and their applications
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer in a managerial role or in charge of process improvements. You are keen to learn more about Lean Thinking and its principles and tools.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Building your Career Mobility
description:
(Do note that CRBCMVL was the previous code for this programme)

Considering different career pathways has become increasingly common with individuals being more aware of their own career aspirations and actively seeking out opportunities to achieve their career goals. The Public Service also provides and encourages officers to take up opportunities such as secondments and structured job rotations (SJR), resulting in more officers experiencing changes in their careers.

While such experiences mark a new and exciting journey, we may also be apprehensive about how we can adapt to a new workplace culture and job scope, as well as building relationships with new colleagues.

Attend this 1-day virtual programme to learn how to apply the 4S Model of "Situation, Self, Support, Strategies" to identify resources and equip yourself with the right attitude and mindset to thrive in a new work environment, new colleagues and different work challenges.
learning_outcomes:
By the end of the programme, you will be able to:

Assess your current personal life stage, career interests and career experiences to identify short-term and long-term career priorities
Identify the support and resources needed for managing career changes
Develop a career transition action plan to manage upcoming career changes
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is keen to develop your mindset and competencies to be able to manage career transitions.

title: Introduction to Machine Learning Techniques (Code-free)
description:
(Do note that CRMLT10 was the previous code for this programme)

Organisations have started harnessing the power of their big data using modern technology like Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning, across sectors and job functions, e.g. Policy, Operations, HR, Finance or Procurement. This has led to better insights and productivity for the organisation. Take data science to the next level and learn the common machine learning techniques which may be used to help solve your agency’s business problems.

Through this programme, you will learn fundamental principles and techniques of machine learning. You will learn how to solve business problems using CRISP-framework by going through the entire cycle of modelling and testing for different use cases. You will understand how machine learning techniques can be used to predict behaviour, anticipate events, segment customers and even find outliers in your datasets.

You will also learn more about big data, and view demos of AI applicat
learning_outcomes:
Develop a basic understanding of the principles behind the algorithms of machine learning and CRISP-DM framework for data projects  
Learn and apply 4 main techniques in machine learning
Articulate use cases for the application of machine learning techniques to business problems within your agency.
Develop a basic understanding of deep learning, neural nets and big data, and the role they play in AI.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who wishes to learn more about the machine learning techniques and apply in an AI/ML project.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Driving Digital Excellence with The Six ‘I’s® - Unleash Your Innovation Potential
description:
(Do note that CRAPIVL was the previous code for this programme)

In the fast-paced world of public service, practical innovation is the driving force behind progress. As the government embraces digitalisation to elevate efficiency and effectiveness, public officers take the lead in shaping transformation.

This programme is your gateway to the essential tools and mindsets of The Six ‘I’s® of Innovation – a structured, purpose-driven approach to turning ideas into value. Each participant receives a copy of Yes, You Can Innovate by Award winning author, Natalie Turner, and a personalised innovation strengths profile.

As a public officer, your impact on the nation is profound. By unleashing your innovation potential, you hold the key to shaping a future of unparalleled public service excellence.
learning_outcomes:
Structured Innovation: Embrace a clear and purposeful path to innovation, empowering you to tackle challenges with confidence.
Harness Your Strengths: Discover your unique innovation abilities, amplify your skills, and learn how to refine them for lasting impact.
The Six ‘I’s® Mindsets: Immerse yourself in the transformative power of The Six ‘I’s® – Curiosity, Creativity, Critical Thinking, Courage, Commitment, and Clever Thinking – igniting your capacity to innovate.
Practical Tips and Tools: Gain hands-on, actionable tools within The Six ‘I’s® framework. Apply these techniques to your work in the Public Service for tangible and meaningful results.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is interested in having a deeper understanding of Innovation and/or are keen to initiate innovation projects in your teams or agencies.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Data Analytics – Basic Principles and Applications
description:
(Do note that CRDDAVL was the previous code for this programme)

With the abundant data and modern technology available today, there are rising expectations for non-analytics professionals to be proficient in data analysis to generate insights and improve decision making. Designed for non-analytics professionals, this foundational programme* will provide you with a good understanding and appreciation for data analysis, and how it can be used for better decision-making.

Through practical hands-on exercises, analyse a given data set and solve case studies using Microsoft Excel tools such as pivot tables and pivot charts, and identify potential opportunities within your agency to apply data analysis.

*This is a foundational programme that covers the whole process of data analytics using Excel, suitable for officers who wish to have an appreciation of data analytics.
learning_outcomes:
Explain what data analytics is and the different types of data analytics - descriptive, predictive, prescriptive, quantitative and qualitative
Describe the key questions that analytics can answer and how it can be used for better decision making
Identify key tools and methodologies used in analytical decision making
Apply basic data and statistical principles fundamental to data analytics
Analyse data using pivot tables and basic tools in Microsoft Excel
Apply principles of data visualisation
Describe common techniques in predictive analytics
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who needs to use data to improve performance and/or provide insights into data analytics.

title: Love Them Or Lose Them: Motivating And Engaging Your Staff
description:
(Do note that CRLEL02 was the previous code for this programme)

Faced with a rapidly changing environment and citizens' increasing expectations, Public Service organisations need to respond by being more effective in their performance. To do so, organisations need to fully engage and retain their officers as they are the building blocks of the organisation. However, managers like you, with your multiple work responsibilities, may find it challenging to do so.

This programme equips you with strategies, skills and practical tools to engage and retain your officers. It is specially designed for first-time line managers and is based on Wall Street bestseller, “Love 'Em or Lose 'Em: Getting Good People to Stay”.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the causes and costs of disengagement
Apply different strategies to effectively engage officers, ranging from millennial officers to longer serving officers
Use practical tools to quickly understand the values and needs of different officers
Know how you can use conversations to deepen trust and relationship with your officers
Gain self-awareness about your strengths and weaknesses as a retention focused manager
Conduct Stay Interviews with your officers instead of Exit Interviews
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who oversees individual contributors or a team of individual contributors. You want to be a talent magnet, engage and retain your officers so that they are committed, take ownership and go the extra mile.

title: Data Analytics with R
description:
(Do note that CRDAR10 was the previous code for this programme)

With so many different programming languages available, which is the most beneficial for data analytics?

R is open source, making it highly cost-effective for a project of any size, and is also specifically designed for statistical analysis and data wrangling. In this programme*, discover how to program in R and use R for effective data analytics, through a range of practical hands-on activities and guided resources.

*This programme is suitable for officers in data analytics or data science fields who need to perform statistical analysis on large datasets.
learning_outcomes:
Identify and complete the range of tasks involved in the data analysis process
Appreciate R libraries and data structures using R
Analyse different datasets using R programming
Perform data cleaning for preparation of data analysis in R
Create data visualisations to communicate insights derived from datasets
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is keen to pick up R to do data analysis. You should have attended CSC's course Data Analytics – Basic Principles and Applications (CRDDA10/CRDDAVL) programme, or are familiar with basic data analytics. You should also be familiar with foundational statistical concepts.

title: Cleaning and Preparing Data for Analysis
description:
(Do note that CRDCL10 was the previous code for this programme)

Do you know that people often spend 80% of their time cleaning data and only about 20% doing the analysis?

Good, clean data is not always readily available. The datasets we first encounter usually contain large volumes of data, stored in formats not easy to use, or contain inaccurate, incomplete or unreasonable data. Sometimes, there is also the need to combine data from different sources, which can be a tricky and messy job.

In this programme*, you will use a range of hands-on activities and case studies to learn how to reverse the effort required so that you can spend your time analysing the data instead and improve your productivity.

*This programme is suitable for officers who need to clean data at work.
learning_outcomes:
Recognise the importance of structuring data in a tidy data format and applying the steps to convert ‘messy’ data to ‘tidy’ data for data analysis
Describe the dimensions of data quality – validity, accuracy, completeness, consistency, uniformity – and demonstrate the ability to explore the data quality before starting data analysis
Describe the data cleaning workflow of inspecting the data, cleaning the data and verifying the cleaning results
Give examples of tools to use for data cleaning and preparation
Demonstrate the ability to inspect a dataset to check the validity and profile the data using statistical summaries
Perform the steps of data cleaning on a dataset using add-ins/ tools available in Microsoft Excel
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who has some basic understanding of data analytics and statistics, and you wish to prepare and process data effectively.

title: Basic Data Analytics for Support Officers
description:
(Do note that CRBDA10 was the previous code for this programme)

As a support officer, you may have observed an increase in data collation requests to support decision making in key work processes or services. Would you like to unlock the insights behind the data you handle and see their impact?

In this hands-on programme* supported by real-life case studies, you will use Microsoft Excel tools and gain foundational skills to analyse data and communicate insights to facilitate better decision-making.

*This is a foundational programme on data analysis using Excel for support officers.
learning_outcomes:
Explain what data analytics is and recognise different types of data analytics
State the six key steps in analytical decision making
Recognise basic data principles that form the foundation of data analytics
Analyse data using pivot tables and basic tools available in Excel
Produce Excel charts to analyse/ communicate data
Describe the trends in analytics in both public and private sectors
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer in a supporting role (MX14 & 13(I) and equivalent, or MX15 & below and equivalent) who is keen to tap on data analytics to help support the data work required to make better decisions.

title: Solving Problems With Lean
description:
(Do note that CRSPL10 was the previous code for this programme)

Are you keen to find out how public agencies can explore innovative ways to optimise limited resources and create more value for citizens?

This activity-based programme is designed to help you develop skills and knowledge on solving workplace process-related problems. You will be required to identify an actual process related problem at work you wish to solve.

Sign up now for a step-by-step guide through the Lean A3 thinking structured problem-solving approach by applying it on the problem you have identified.
learning_outcomes:
Apply key Lean tools and techniques
Solve an existing problem with the Lean A3 Problem Solving approach
Apply the Lean A3 Problem Solving approach to other projects and challenges
who_should_attend: You are a manager (or above) keen to use the Lean approach to solve and improve business process issues.

title: Starting A Data Science Project
description:
(Do note that CRDSP10 was the previous code for this programme)

Have you been tasked to use data to solve a work-related problem but have no idea how to get started, where to collect data or how to analyse the data and present your insights?

Through this 3-day crash course* in Data Science for non-technical professionals, you will gain a fundamental understanding of various Data Science techniques by preparing and analysing multiple datasets using Python and performing actual data visualisation using Tableau. You will also improve your overall data literacy, and be guided through the entire Data Science process, from scoping a problem and data preparation to analysis and presentation of findings.

*This programme is suitable for officers who have a background in data analysis and statistics.
learning_outcomes:
Explain the impact of solving problems with data

Define a well-scoped problem statement, solvable through a Data Science approach

Perform simple cleansing of structured data

Identify where practitioners should be engaged for more complex work and collaborate effectively with them

Communicate insights and discoveries to stakeholders through clear narratives and visualisations
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer keen to adopt a data-driven approach to improving policy decisions and operations as well as to understand what is involved in starting a typical data science project.

title: Strategic Communications and Engagement for Policy Development
description:
(Do note that CRSCP10 was the previous code for this programme)

For government policies to be effective, the ability to craft and communicate persuasive messages tailored to diverse citizen segments is crucial.


This blended programme, which consists of a 4-hour self-paced eLearning module and a 1-day classroom programme, will help you understand the importance of strategic communications and engagement. It will explain the process of public communications in policy development, and will equip you with the skills to develop persuasive narratives to secure buy-in from diverse stakeholders.
learning_outcomes:
At the end of the programme, you will be able to:
Explain how strategic communications and public engagement strategies contribute to better policy development and implementation;
Craft customised narratives for different stakeholders in strategic communications and public engagement strategies; and
Design a strategic communication and public engagement strategy for a new policy deliverable.
who_should_attend: You are a Policy and Planning officer and middle manager involved in developing, implementing, and communicating policies.

title: Business Intelligence And Dashboarding
description:
(Do note that CRAAD10 was the previous code for this programme)

To support data-informed decision making, you need to be able to analyse data, generate insights and present them convincingly to your stakeholders. Elevate your data analysis, visualisation and communication today by using Business Intelligence (BI), the tool-driven process of analysing data, generating insights and presenting them.

You will learn to use Microsoft’s Power BI tool to integrate, analyse data and create interactive dashboards through hands-on tasks. You will also learn how to communicate data-driven insights to stakeholders through a coherent and convincing story that has the power to influence decision-making.
learning_outcomes:
Appreciate what business intelligence is and its role in analytics
Describe how business intelligence and descriptive analysis can be applied to data
Identify the importance of analysing data visually and selecting the right method of data analysis
Analyse trends, spot outliers in data and find correlations
Use visual perception principles to design insightful and effective dashboards
Develop an interactive dashboard using Power BI to communicate insights coherently and convincingly
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer interested in learning how to use business intelligence tools to analyse trends, spot key issues and deliver data-driven insights. This programme will be relevant for public officers who regularly analyse data to generate insights and reports. As this programme involves working with formulae, you must be comfortable working with Power BI or similar business analysis tools.

title: Advanced Presentation Strategies
description:
(Do note that CRAVP10 was the previous code for this programme)

Are you an experienced Singapore Public Officer who regularly delivers formal presentations to key stakeholders? This two-day programme is designed to enhance and refine your existing presentation skills by focusing on advanced techniques and strategies for impactful communication. 

This programme is ideal for officers who already possess basic presentation skills and are looking to elevate their capabilities to effectively engage and influence their audience.
learning_outcomes:
Demonstrate the ability to analyse audiences using a structured four-step process, tailoring presentations for maximum impact.
Exhibit the ability to prepare and deliver persuasive presentations, captivating and influencing audiences effectively.
Acquire expertise in selecting visuals to effectively present and communicate data, guided by an understanding of visualisation objectives.
Recognise the requirements of hybrid and virtual presentations for improved communication and engagement.
Demonstrate the ability to present ideas effectively through storytelling.
Gain advanced proficiency in managing Q&A sessions, including thinking on your feet and demonstrating verbal agility and poise when responding to audiences.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer with prior experience in presentations who needs to give formal presentations to key stakeholders. This programme is suitable for officers who already have the basic skills of delivering presentations.

title: Presenting Evidence & Testifying In Court Trials
description:
(Do note that CRPCT13 was the previous code for this programme)

As an investigation officer, you may be required to attend court as an expert witness or to commence legal proceedings. In order to discharge your duties professionally, you need to know what is expected of you during the course of the legal proceedings in court.

You will familiarise yourself with the legal and prosecution system of justice in Singapore. You will also learn to present evidence and testify in court trials confidently.
learning_outcomes:
Examine the Singapore Legal System and administration of justice
Demonstrate the skills required to present evidence and testify in Court confidently
who_should_attend: You are an Enforcement Officer who conducts investigations within your jurisdiction, or a public officer who is involved in investigative work. You may also be called upon to attend court trials as an expert witness. This programme is open to Singapore Public Officers only.

title: Win-Win Mindset @ Work For Support Officers
description:
(Do note that CRDWM23 was the previous code for this programme)

When you have a win-win mindset, you will approach activities and relationships in the workplace in a positive manner and have a more productive and satisfying work-life. However, in order to inculcate such a mindset, you need to be able to manage the differences that exist among each another in order to bring about mutually beneficial outcomes to all involved. You can learn the necessary skills to bring about such a mindset.
learning_outcomes:
Illustrate some examples of win-win mindset at work
Use the Johari Window model to explain the benefits of self-disclosure
Identify different types of people and how they communicate
Use three approaches to relate with others for win-win in the workplace
Apply the Ladder of Inference and empathetic listening concepts in win-win conversations
Use different techniques to handle conflicts and resolve problems collaboratively
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who wishes to develop a win-win mindset to better support your team or department

title: Parliamentary Processes And Replies
description:
(Do note that CRDPARL was the previous code for this programme)

As a public officer, you may find yourself having to provide answers to questions posed to your ministry during parliamentary session.

In this programme, you will learn how to draft effective parliamentary replies which emphasises your organisation's key messages to the public. You will also learn the business of Parliament and understand the PQ system, its procedures and timelines.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the PQ system, its procedures and timelines.
Draft replies that address the main concerns of the Members of Parliament (MPs), their constituents and the public, and emphasise the organisations key messages to the public
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who may be required to write responses to parliamentary sessions in the course of your work.

title: Writing Minutes Of Meetings For Support Officers
description:
(Do note that CRWRM23 was the previous code for this programme)

A good minute-taker is one who possesses a variety of skills. These include being able to summarise and listen effectively during a meeting; writing clearly, concisely and tactfully when translating notes into a set of minutes.

In this programme, you will learn and practise taking notes and writing minutes of meetings effectively and efficiently.
learning_outcomes:
Understand how meetings are conducted and the different ways of recording minutes and notes of discussions can be recorded clearly and concisely. 
Practise taking notes and writing minutes by listening and viewing the recording of some meetings
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who wants to write effective minutes of meeting to better support your team or department.

title: Specialist Investigation Course
description:
(Do note that CRTBA13 was the previous code for this programme)

Investigation is primarily about observing, identifying and gathering evidence, conducting field inquiries as well as understanding legal constraints. You will learn the fundamentals of conducting an effective investigation by applying these critical knowledge and skills.
learning_outcomes:
Articulate investigation principles and processes
Criticise and review the latest investigative tools and technology, including their applications
Appraise various operating environments and legal issues
Execute various investigation methodologies
Implement best practices identified from past case studies
who_should_attend: You are an Enforcement Officer who conducts investigations within your jurisdiction, or a public officer who is involved in investigative work. This programme is open to Singapore Public Officers only

title: Fixed Asset Management
description:
(Do note that CRFAM12 was the previous code for this programme)

Does your job require you to administer and manage land, buildings, office equipment, furniture or other expensive government items?

If so, this programme is designed especially to help you better manage your organisation's fixed assets.
learning_outcomes:
Identify the requirements in the management of fixed assets in the Public Service
Identify internal control issues concerning fixed assets
Explain the activities relating to acquisition and retirement/reinstatement of assets
Explain the activities required in maintaining a Fixed Asset Register
Describe the guidelines in physically checking and safekeeping assets
who_should_attend: You are Singapore Public Officer involved in the administration and management of your agency's fixed assets.

title: ComplianceConnect: Designing Better Licensing Experience
description:
(Do note that CRDBLE was the previous code for this programme)

In this ComplianceConnect programme, discover how regulatory agencies are transforming regulatory and licensing experiences through innovative methods. Learn the regulatory review guiding principles developed by MTI and PSD, as well as MSF's application of risk-based approaches and automation that have made their licensing regime more effective, efficient, and stakeholder-centric.

Join fellow practitioners in examining how stakeholder-centric, risk-calibrated approaches are shaping the future of regulatory excellence and gain valuable insights to drive your agency's licensing transformation forward.

From the pre-workshop phase to the check-in session, participants will receive learning materials and resources to guide their review of their respective agencies’ licensing regimes.
who_should_attend: This programme is open to all middle and senior level Regulatory, Compliance and Enforcement officers.

title: Finance Foundation Programme
description:
(Do note that MSFFP10 was the previous code for this programme)

The Finance Foundation Programme is the first of a series of finance milestone programmes under the Finance Specialist Track launched by the Ministry of Finance.

The programme includes a comprehensive four-day classroom learning segment to help you better understand your role in the Public Service. The segment also provides practical insights to the rationale behind finance policies and funding evaluation. You can also create peer networks within the public finance community.
learning_outcomes:
Explain the government structure and public finance functions
Discuss the significant driving forces impacting Singapore's financial position and fiscal policies
Explain the key principles and policies in the management of resources
Describe the duties, responsibilities, and roles of finance officer as a manager, steward and business partner
Explain the common terminologies used in public finance
Identify control guidelines and requirements to ensure the integrity of operations, financial and accounting practices
who_should_attend:
You are a Singapore Public Officer with less than two years of experience in the Public Service's finance function. You may possess prior experience in finance from the private sector. 

Note: Public officers who have more than two years of experience but are nominated by their supervisors may also be considered.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Leading Your Team Through Change
description:
(Do note that CRLTCVL was the previous code for this programme)

In a global landscape that is VUCA (Volatile, Uncertain, Complex, Ambiguous), organisations, and leaders face more complexity, challenges and changes. The COVID-19 pandemic and Post COVID-19 have accelerated the magnitude and the speed of change and digitalisation.




Being “future-ready” and “change-ready” in the way we lead and work is a necessity and a competitive advantage for organisations. Organisations, leaders and managers have to anticipate and be pro-active, to drive and implement changes rather than being reactive towards external circumstances.




Change can also bring about positive outcomes in terms of continuous improvement, innovation, transformation, success and growth for the individuals and organisations. As the future becomes more complex and diverse, Leaders and Managers must have the change agility and the ability to inspire, lead and manage change effectively through the “People Side of Change” to
learning_outcomes:
Understand how to manage the types of changes effectively as a leader and manager
Apply the type of interventions and process needed during the change process
Manage the emotional and people side of change during change and transition
Identify and manage resistance and barriers to change
Enhance team members' agility and resilience towards change through a Growth Mindset
Obtain buy-in, engagement and support from your team members about the change
Plan and manage the change communication process
Overcome the barriers and resistance to change
Establish and implement change effectively
who_should_attend: You are a manager who leads your staff or teams through organisational change, and are keen to learn how to engage them through the change.

title: Managing Tension - A Core Leadership Skill
description:
(Do note that CRMTP10 was the previous code for this programme)

As a manager, how can you better manage tension to spur personal growth and build rapport with your team members? How do your leadership stance and personal values impact the way you manage this tension? Managers are expected to work with and through team members, peers and stakeholders to achieve results. When perspectives differ, tension is inevitable. How well you manage this tension will impact the quality of your relationships and how others experience you as a person. Tension is the signal that there are differences in the way something is being viewed. There are two typical ways of dealing with tension. The first is to see tension as wrong and try to take control and ‘make it right’. The other way is to try resolving this tension through dialogue or communication. Specially targeted at managers, this programme will help you find these answers.
learning_outcomes:
Understand and appreciate tension as a source of learning and growth
Acquire practical skills to manage your tension as part of your development at the personal and team levels
Name and frame your tension in a more empowering way
Be more self-aware and switch to a learner stance when managing tension
Uncover your own personal values and frame tension as dilemmas
Advocate, inquire and listen more deeply to discover more possibilities when dealing with tension
who_should_attend: You are a middle manager who oversees one or more teams and would like to learn how to effectively manage your tension so that you can better lead your team(s).

title: Authentic Leadership Conversations for Officers
description:
(Do note that CRALCO was the previous code for this programme)

The Authentic Leadership Conversations for Officers Programmes caters to non-managerial team members who want to speak candidly with peers and reporting officers to achieve desired outcomes without harming relationships. It provides conceptual models and concrete skills in carrying out authentic and impactful conversations, enabling you to effectively navigate high-stakes discussions.


It is a powerful, highly participative and practical programme that uses role-plays and commonly faced real work situations. The programme will provide you with new insights and practical skills that you can apply immediately to tap into the power of conversations.
learning_outcomes:
Improve the quality of conversation with all stakeholders - management, peers, team members, members of the public etc. 
Consciously build psychological safety with “point easy culture” so that all can speak openly and safely in a respectful manner.
Express opinions candidly on sensitive matters in a way that will not aggravate the situation 
Address difficult and sensitive situations with respect, authenticity and compassion.
Manage relationships to increase trust levels and improve relationship.
Identify potential problems or conflict situations early and undertake possible interventions.
Carry out difficult high stakes conversations with various stakeholders.
Take action to materialise matters discussed during conversations
who_should_attend: You are an individual contributor who wish to enhance your communication skills in professional settings.

title: Human Resource Managers Programme
description:
(Do note that MSHMP10 was the previous code for this programme)

Human Resource (HR) in the Public Service is more dynamic than before. Changes in the operating environment, such as cuts in manpower growth, competition for talent, shifting demographics, changing workforce attitudes and rising public expectations, mean that the Singapore Public Service needs to transform and keep up. HR plays an important role in actualising these transformations.

At the organisational level, you partner our management team to make your agencies future-ready by being the key driver and enabler of change. At the workplace, you motivate your colleagues and help them to manage change, train and develop them with the right knowledge, skills, mindsets and instincts to respond to the public confidently. The Human Resource Managers Programme (HRMP) will provide you with the perspectives, networks and knowledge to achieve these.

Targeted at experienced HR middle management, this 6.5-day milestone programme wi
learning_outcomes:
Understand the operating environment, trends of HR and impact on the Public Service​
Recognise the evolving HR role and move in tandem with HR leaders to embrace and implement HR strategies​
Become effective strategic business partners and change agents​
Deliver tangible and impactful results by implementing HR strategies that effectively engage and develop people​
Widen peer support network with other HR practitioners​
who_should_attend: You are an experienced HR professional in the Singapore Public Service and are committed to help your agency achieve its strategic goals. You have approximately five years of working experience in HR and are in a middle management role. You are likely to be an HR Manager, Assistant Director or Lead Analyst.

title: [Virtual Classroom] AI-seh! Didn't know AI can do this!
description:
In this hands-on webinar, you’ll learn how to go beyond basic prompts and tap into advanced techniques that get AI to think, ideate, and create with you. We’ll help you rethink your prompt strategy, craft visuals that wow, and unlock outputs that’ll make you go, “Ai-seh!”

 

Please note that the class will be held on Zoom, and the link will be sent to you via Postman. Please keep a lookout on your emails!

You would need your own COMET/GSIB laptops to access GovAI tools for this programme.
learning_outcomes:
Apply advanced prompt engineering techniques including role play, multimodal input and iterative prompting to improve the quality and relevance of AI-generated outputs. 

Use frameworks to turn Gen AI into your go-to teammate for crafting campaigns, writing copy, generating ideas, or getting unstuck at work. 

Create eye-catching visual content using AI tools by crafting better image prompts and through hands-on activities and demos.
who_should_attend:
Public officers who are looking to level up their prompting skills, explore effective and creative applications of AI in their daily work.

 

Please note that this is a re-run of the webinar series in Sept-Oct 2025.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Going Lean as a Team
description:
Please note: This is an in-house programme only — there are no public run dates. If you are interested in organising this programme for your team or agency, please contact your agency's Training Coordinators/HR.

 

(Do note that CRGLAVL was the previous code for this programme)

As the demands on the Public Service increase, how can we do more with less? How can we redesign and optimise our workflows and business processes?  

This ‘learn-by-doing’ programme integrates training and coaching. As business processes often involve multiple stakeholders, you’ll have opportunities to collaborate with your colleagues as an intact team and work on real-life projects. Apart from classroom learning of theories, you will be guided and coached through the entire life cycle of your team's project to resolve the identified process problem.

Sign up now for this hands-on, highly interactive programme that will enable you to immediately apply the theories and tools of the Lean approach back at work a
learning_outcomes:
Explain the key principles and philosophy of Lean Management 
 
Identify the value and opportunities of Lean Management in your teams
Use Lean tools to analyse and redesign workflows and drive business process improvement within your team
 
Solve existing process problem(s) using the Lean A3 Problem Solving approach
who_should_attend: You are keen to gain hands-on experience in applying Lean methodology and be closely guided at each step in improving existing processes at work. You must sign up as an intact team comprising the Project Sponsor/Manager and 3-7 team members. Team members may come from different departments or functions, as long as they are involved in the same work process in their line of work.

title: A Primer In Text Analytics - Unstructured Text And Sentiment Analysis
description:
(Do note that CRPTACS was the previous code for this programme)

75% of data in the world are in unstructured form, e.g. videos, audio or text. Within our organisations, we may have avoided using unstructured data due to the inherent difficulties in analysing it, as compared to structured data. However, can you imagine making critical policy or organisational decisions based on only some data?




Making sense of sentiments from unstructured text information is key to ensuring data is analysed in totality. In this programme, discover key concepts in unstructured data analytics and explore how you can apply them through in-class discussions and hands-on exercises. Conducted interactively with case studies, this programme will equip you to make better decisions using unstructured text information.




*This programme is suitable for officers who have background in basic data analytics and statistics.
learning_outcomes:
Differentiate the different types of unstructured data
Describe the approach to analyse unstructured data
Perform basic text analysis on transcribed data
Apply text analytics framework in making better business decisions
Present and communicate findings from unstructured data in an easy-to-understand and interesting manner
who_should_attend: You are keen to learn about unstructured text analytics and how to apply it to make better decisions. You should also be comfortable with having discussions and doing hands-on exercises using Python and R programming languages.

title: Basic Handling Difficult Customer Situations
description:
(Do note that CRBHDC was the previous code for this programme)

This programme equips frontline officers with practical tools to handle challenging customer interactions effectively. Through essential communication frameworks and customer service models, officers learn to identify different customer behaviours and respond appropriately with proven approaches to enhance service delivery. Officers will gain confidence through role-play scenarios and leave with effective strategies to manage difficult situations while maintaining professional standards and service excellence.
learning_outcomes:
By the end of this programme, officers will be able to:

Identify common behavioural patterns and customer needs in service interactions.
Apply fundamental skills to respond to challenging customer interactions.
Apply emotional regulation techniques to maintain professionalism in challenging situations.
who_should_attend: Support officers and senior officers in frontline service functions who are keen to enhance their knowledge and skills in managing complex situations with customers.

title: Advanced Handling Difficult Customer Situations
description:
(Do note that CRAHDC was the previous code for this programme)

This programme is designed to equip service frontliners with advanced skills to enhance their interactions with citizens. Learners will gain insights into customer psychology, understanding the thoughts, feelings, and motivations behind various behaviours. The programme covers effective communication techniques, focusing on navigating complex interactions, explaining policies clearly, and handling rejection cases with empathy. Learners will have hands-on practice in applying the CARE Service Principles and other key techniques. The programme also provides officers with tips on building personal resilience, equipping them with strategies to maintain composure and effectiveness in demanding situations.
learning_outcomes:
By the end of this programme, you will be able to:  

Identify how psychological factors and service components drive customer behaviours in service interactions.
Apply strategies for complex customer situations
Build resilience and support strategies for handling challenging service situations
who_should_attend: Senior officers, team leaders, and/or managers in frontline functions who possess basic customer handling experience and are keen to enhance their skills in managing advanced or complex customer situations.

title: Designing Customer Experiences using Data Analytics
description:
(Do note that CRPAE10 was the previous code for this programme)

With customers expecting a personalised experience across all channels now, how can you use data to anticipate customers' needs, make smart recommendations and enhance the overall customer experience? In this hands-on programme, you will learn how to use techniques such as Sensemaker, and clustering and association analysis. Through data-driven analytics, you will gain a clearer understanding of your customer profiles and improve the consumer journey.
learning_outcomes:
Improve a customer experience driven by data, analytics and technology
Discover how machine learning techniques can be used to improve the customer journey
Use visualisation tools like Tableau Dashboards to gain customer insights
Apply data clustering technique with the use of Sensemaker tool
Identify the role of technology and automation in analytics solutions
Learn how other agencies have used analytics to improve their customer experience
who_should_attend: You are a Service manager or team leader in the Singapore Public Service tasked with identifying a business problem or implementing solutions in your agency.

title: Elevating Customer Experience through Customer Insights Management
description:
(Do note that CRCECI was the previous code for this programme)

This programme is designed to equip Public Service officers with the skills to enhance customer experience through effective customer insights management. Learners will learn to describe the impact of customer insights for their organisation, identify critical data necessary for measuring and tracking customer experiences, and analyse the key drivers and gaps within existing customer journeys. By leveraging these insights, learners will develop an actionable plan to integrate a customer insights approach into their service operations and processes, ultimately fostering a more responsive and customer-centric organisation.
learning_outcomes:
Describe how customer insights management can improve customer satisfaction in various segments.
Identify the necessary data needed to effectively measure and track customer experience.
Analyse key drivers and gaps in existing customer and service journey using derived insights.
Develop a plan using data insights to integrate a customer insights approach to service operations and processes within the organisation.
who_should_attend: Middle managers and senior officers in the Service domain and/or in Customer Insights functions in their organisation.

title: Leading Customer Experience: Middle Managers' Programme
description:
(Do note that CRLCX was the previous code for this programme)

The Leading Customer Experience: Middle Managers’ Programme empowers participants to strengthen their agency’s customer experience delivery by leading with a customer-centric mindset. Through a blend of strategic insights and hands-on practice, managers will learn how customer experience connects with critical functions such as strategy, policy, operations, and technology.

This programme is designed to help middle managers become catalysts for service excellence by equipping them with the skills, tools, and strategic perspectives needed to unify cross-functional efforts, enhance responsiveness, and deliver greater value to customers.

Learn to apply a customer-centric service mindset that prioritises empathy and responsiveness to the customer’s needs.
Explore effective customer experience strategies to deliver greater customer value.
Create customer journey maps to illustrate seamless, omnichannel experiences that meet div
learning_outcomes:
Apply customer-centric service principles within their teams to enhance service delivery.
Analyse how customer experience strategies connect policy design, service operations, customer insights, and service innovations to deliver value for customers.
Create customer journey maps that illustrate omnichannel customer experiences, integrating insights from customer data and feedback.
Develop a service blueprint that details cross-functional collaborations to enhance customer experience through effective service delivery, escalation pathways, and recovery processes.
Design an implementation roadmap for prioritised service improvements, with stakeholder touchpoints for cross-functional service delivery.
who_should_attend: Managers in various functional roles that support their agency’s service delivery: • Strategy • Policy • Operations • Technology

title: Game Design for Engagement
description:
(Do note that CRGDE01 was the previous code for this programme)

Engagement sessions are critical for fostering understanding, gathering feedback, and building trust with teams or communities. While conventional methods like focus group discussions can be useful, how might we harness “gameful design” to create open and playful spaces, in which we can enable shared sense-making, build community and shared intention, and accelerate aligned action within our teams and communities?

In this programme, you will get to play cooperative or narrative story-games, opening up an expanded view of what a gameful design is and can be. You will also experience the emotional impact of these gameful interventions, and gain a new lens through which to think about and create gameful engagements. There will be hands-on and interactive segments where you will be able to apply some of these lessons and develop a new methodology in your engagement toolkit.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the fundamental principles of gameful design for engagement
Analyse different engagement scenarios to determine the suitability and potential impact of incorporating gameful design elements
Apply gameful design principles to develop and facilitate engagement sessions
Evaluate the different gameful design approaches in aligning desired engagement outcomes
who_should_attend: You are a public communication and/or engagement practitioner involved or will be involved in engagement work.  This programme is open to Singapore Public Officers only.

title: Clear Leadership Course
description:
(Do note that CRCLC was the previous code for this programme)

In today's dynamic and complex landscape, the need to lead, learn collaboratively and build partnerships has never been more crucial. This 4-day face-to-face Clear Leadership Course is intended to support directors and deputy directors who are responsible for developing individuals and teams within the organisation, or who want to lead in a way that creates high engagement, real alignment around decisions and full commitment to the team’s purpose.

The Clear Leadership Course provides a thorough introduction to the partnership-based theory of organisational design and leadership. At the same time, the practical programme equips you with essential tools and core skills to enhance vertical development, and techniques to engage in meaningful conversations. Through this unique approach, you will gain intra- and inter-personal clarity to lead and exercise collaboration in partnership-driven work.
learning_outcomes:
Specifically, we aim to use the Clear Leadership Course to teach you three interrelated skill sets:

1) The skills required to sustain partnership and collaboration

Partnership is a relationship in which all parties feel responsible for the success of their common purpose.

Clear Leadership proposes that interpersonal mush will cause partnerships to fail unless it is regularly cleared out

2) The skills required for leading learning in the midst of performing

Clear Leadership proposes that effective organisations are those that are able to learn and perform simultaneously. Most leadership courses are about how to lead performing. Clear Leadership is about how to lead learning – that is, how to help people learn from their collective experience.

3) The skills of self-differentiation

Clear Leadership proposes that the ability to use the skills of Clear Leadership is limited by a person’s level of self-differentiation – and that use of the skills increases a person’s level of self-dif
who_should_attend: The programme is ideal for directors who are looking to improve their leadership effectiveness and their ability to create and sustain effective partnerships with subordinates, peers and leaders in their organisation.

title: Leaders in Administration Programme
description:
(Do note that MSLAP was the previous code for this programme)

The Leaders in Administration Programme (LAP) is a 6-week full-time milestone programme for Public Sector Leaders (PSLs). Participants include Deputy Secretaries from ministries, Chief Executive Officers of public sector agencies, and other senior officers from the professional and uniformed services.

The programme includes governance, policy, and leadership-related sessions conducted locally and virtual/overseas study trips.
learning_outcomes:
Develop whole-of-nation strategies in response to the key shifts in our operating context
Nurture collective leadership as a Public Service Leadership corps to achieve the best outcomes for Singapore
Grow your ability to influence and rally diverse stakeholders and mobilise coalitions for a common cause
Articulate how you lead by example and inspire trust and a sense of purpose in your people, as stewards of your organisations.
who_should_attend:
You are a senior Singapore Public Sector Leader (PSL) who is a deputy secretary, chief executive officer or a Senior Officer from the professional or military service. Note: Participation in this course is by nomination and selection is administered by the Public Service Division.
