About

title: Policy Foundation Skills
description:
Policy Foundation Skills is a core foundation programme for new policy officers. Designed as an accelerated programme for junior policy officers, the programme equips participants with the essential capabilities needed from the outset of their policy journey. Participants will work through the core tasks of the policy process such as drafting and using policy paper outlines for quick iteration, identifying and presenting key data to support an argument, preparing slide decks to aid discussion versus make recommendation, and stewarding the policy till approval.

 

Through this programme, participants will also learn what good public policy entails, how policy moves from development to implementation, and how they play an important role in supporting senior leaders. Using frameworks, guided critique, discussion and applied exercises, participants will understand what effective policy work looks like in the Singapore public service and be equipped to contribute confidently in their roles
learning_outcomes:
By the end of the programme, participants will be able to:

Explain what “good” public policy entails.
Describe the broad policy development and implementation process, and the role of policy officers in supporting it.
Apply key principles and good practices in staffing the policy development process, including: 
Drafting a policy paper outline for quick iteration
Identifying and presenting key data to support an argument
Preparing a slide deck to aid discussion versus make recommendation
Preparing annotated agendas to support senior leaders during inter-agency or inter-department meetings; and
Capturing key follow-up actions to effectively advance the policy development process.
who_should_attend: New officers with less than 1 year of experience in the policy & planning function.

title: Fundamentals of Public Finance Management Module 1 - Ministry and Statutory Board Finance (CRFPFM1)
description:
(Do note that CRFPFM1 was the previous code for this programme)

If you are a Finance officer who has less than two years of experience working in the Public Sector or keen on learning about public finance, this is the course for you.

Through three blended learning modules, you will learn about Public Finance policies, government accounting and become familiar with central financial systems. This helps you understand your role and other roles in finance better.

You may attend the modules in no particular order.

NOTE: FPFM Module 1 will now be a Two Days classroom programme.
learning_outcomes:
Identify the underlying governance structure of Public Finance and the key roles played by Public Finance Officers in their agencies
Describe relevant Finance policies and IM, and understand the framework and key concepts in the preparation and administration of government budgeting
Explain the costing and budgeting approaches, approval, evaluation and risk management of Development Projects
Identify the various aspects of key financial operations to ensure the smooth running of government administration
Explain the overview and key concepts of financial management in Statutory Boards (SBs)
who_should_attend: You are a Finance Public Officer who has two years or less experience in the Public Service Finance Function.

title: Aligning Internal Stakeholders for Policy Middle Managers
description:
(Do note that CRASM10 was the previous code for this programme)

Do you work with multiple stakeholders across agencies and/or departments?



As a Middle Manager in the Policy and Planning domain, it might be challenging to find alignment across teams and government agencies on complex issues. Yet, it is important to reach common ground to ensure better policy outcomes. This 1-day programme will provide you with tools and frameworks to understand the motivations of stakeholders, and explore factors driving behavioural change. You will also practise applying these skills to achieve fruitful alignment with your internal stakeholders.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the importance of aligning internal stakeholders in policymaking;​ 

Learn skills and techniques to achieve alignment; and​

Apply these skills and techniques to achieve more effective policy outcomes.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer in a Policy and Planning role (MX11, MX10) who wants to be more effective in aligning internal stakeholders to achieve better policy outcomes.

title: Leading and Stewarding Change
description:
(Do note that ELSLC01 was the previous code for this programme)

Want to drive innovation and progress within your organisation? Then mastering the skill of leading and stewarding change is critical. In today's rapidly changing environment, understanding how change works is more important than ever to help organisations remain relevant and achieve their strategic outcomes.

This programme equips you with the knowledge and skills to effectively manage change, plan and sustain change efforts, build awareness of your organisation's approach towards change, craft a purposeful change narrative, manage potential resistance from stakeholders, and adopt concrete steps to embed sustainable change.

Learn from Public Service Leaders and practitioners who will share their change journeys and provide valuable insights. With access to a range of resources and tools, don't miss out on this opportunity to enhance your skills and drive positive change within your organisation. Join us today!
learning_outcomes:
Distinguish the types of change within an Organisation
Craft a Change Narrative and apply strategies to enhance it
Explain key change concepts and frameworks 
Adopt the principles of persuasion and engagement when planning a change conversation with stakeholders
Adopt concrete steps to embed sustainable change
who_should_attend: You are an Organisation Development Practitioner or Team Lead/Change Agent in your Organisation.

title: Thriving Teams: Cultivating a Culture for Enhanced Effectiveness and Well-Being
description:
(Do note that ELETD02 was the previous code for this programme)

“Team, team, team!” - a common exclamation we hear, often mingled with emotional nuances. Building a high-performing, yet cohesive and resilient team, is both an art and a science. There are several conditions to note, some good practices to build and develop teams, and some personal leadership and “use of self” skills to apply and adopt, be it in a fully in-person or hybrid team environment. Learn all these and more with our e-module and become an informed and adaptable team leader.
learning_outcomes:
Apply the frameworks and concepts of team effectiveness back to a team
Identify, build and sustain key team development elements in a team
Enhance understanding of key human factors for team effectiveness, like psychological safety, emotions, culture and well-being
who_should_attend: You are an OD Practitioner, and/ or a Team Leader in your organisation.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Process Improvement for Support Officers
description:
(Do note that CRPSOVL was the previous code for this programme)

Ever wondered why there is a need to relook and improve on processes? Why bother with fixing what is not broken?

In this one-day workshop, Support Officers will be given the opportunity to gain a deeper appreciation of the practicality and relevance of Process Improvement in their daily lives and at work. Officers will also learn about the two most prevalent process improvement methods and their application, namely Lean Management and Agile.

Sign up for this hands-on, highly interactive learning-by-doing workshop that will help you understand the importance and relevance in adopting process improvement in your lives and at work.
learning_outcomes:
Discover the application of process improvement both in and outside of work
Explain key principles of Lean Management and Agile
Explain how the two methodologies differ from each other
Identify how Lean Management or Agile can be used to improve a challenge faced at work 
Who Should Attend

You are a support officer looking to get a broad understanding of process improvement tools and how to apply them.

Outline

Module 1: Business Process Improvement 
 

Introduction to business processes
The evolution of process improvement methods

Module 2: Improve Business Process with Lean 
 

The concept of flow
TIMWOOD-I Wastes
Value Stream Mapping (End-to-end perspective)

Module 3: Root-Cause and Counter Measures
 

Use of Fishbone and 5 WHYs Analysis
Identify root causes with Data Analytics & Visualization
Lean counter-measures - Reduce Batch size, Mistake Proofing, 5S & Spaghetti Diagram

Module 4: Let's Try It
 

Hands-On Application - Participants to develop a value stream map and find th
who_should_attend: You are a support officer looking to get a broad understanding of process improvement tools and how to apply them.

title: Effective Budgeting Skills
description:
(Do note that CREBS13 was the previous code for this programme)

Budgeting is at the core of control and coordination of resources, and this makes it fundamental to the strategic outcome of any organisation.

This foundational programme introduces you to the essentials of budgeting through the S.T.R.A.T.E.G.I.C. framework—a structured and practical approach to planning, managing, and reviewing budgets with clarity and purpose. You will build a strong understanding of why budgeting matters, and how to apply key principles systematically in your day-to-day work.

More than just concepts, this course brings budgeting to life. Through hands-on activities, real-world case studies, and guided discussions, you will gain the confidence to navigate budgeting processes, make informed decisions, and contribute meaningfully to your organisation’s financial planning.
learning_outcomes:
Describe budgeting and its purpose
Distinguish the different types of budgets
Explain the principles of a good budget
Budget for the organisation and department using relevant tools and techniques
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is new to budgeting or would like to build foundational knowledge in the principles of good budgeting in the public sector. This is a fundamental, introductory course designed especially for junior officers and those at the beginning of their budgeting journey, regardless of department or role.

title: Engaging With Empathy - Managing In-Person Interactions
description:
(Do note that CRMPI10 was the previous code for this programme)

Are you keen to gain a sound understanding of key techniques and principles to create positive interactions? In this programme, learn how to strengthen care and empathy in service delivery through face-to-face interactions with the public.

There will be a pre-programme exercise to complete on CSC Empathy AI app. Details of the app will be in the course placement letter.
learning_outcomes:
Communicate in-person effectively by identifying the unspoken needs and concerns of customers/public.
Manage the complexities of face-to-face interactions with customers by demonstrating empathy.
Manage one's emotions and triggers when handling difficult customers.
Apply the CARE Service Principles when interacting with customers.
who_should_attend: You are a frontline officer in service operations functions who interacts frequently with the public in the course of your work. This programme is for Singapore Public Officers only.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Strategic Workforce Planning Lite
description:
(Do note that CRSWPLI was the previous code for this programme)

Much of resource planning in the last 20 years has revolved around coordinating, optimising, and planning for materials and resources to facilitate efficient and effective operations, so that organisations have the right resources at the right time, right quantity, and at the right price. 




Most can relate to matching resource requirements and quantities with the right supplier for the right price to be delivered at the right time. However, when it comes to the process of Strategic Workforce Planning (SWP), it is less well understood and less straightforward.




This 0.5-day programme is designed to help managers better understand strategic workforce planning; specifically:


What it is and what it is trying to accomplish for organizations
The key elements in the Strategic Workforce Planning process
What is in it for managers and how managers can contribute to the process
learning_outcomes:
Appreciate SWP as the wider resource planning process
Appreciate the key steps, considerations, challenges and constraints related to SWP
Develop an understanding of how SWP impacts work in organisations and how managers can participate in the process
who_should_attend: You are looking to gain an understanding of strategic resource planning, the strategic workforce planning process and are keen to identify key considerations in planning for your future workforce.

title: Learn To Lead Programme
description:
(Do note that MSLTLPV was the previous code for this programme)

One of the major transitions you will experience as a new manager is moving from being an individual contributor to someone who delivers by working with and through others. This requires you to reframe how you see your role in creating and adding value, and pick up new skills.

You may find yourself asking these questions:

How has my role changed, and what do I need to pay attention to?
How do I motivate, engage, and develop the people I lead?
How do I manage and communicate effectively with my supervisor and staff?

Designed to support new managers, the LTLP will help you appreciate the requirements and context of your new role, and grow your capabilities to lead and learn more effectively.
learning_outcomes:
Gain awareness of your leadership preferences to understand your strengths and growth areas when leading others 
Develop the mindsets, skills and behaviours required to lead and manage your staff 
Develop an action plan that guides your leadership development to support continuous learning back at the workplace 
Recognise the factors that shape your operating environment and organisation to better align to the Public Service priorities
who_should_attend:
You are a newly-appointed manager*, within your first 2 years of assuming a supervisory role in the Public Service. Depending on your agency, as a new manager, you may be a grade MX12 to 11 officer, or their equivalent, holding the designation of Team Leader, Assistant Manager, Manager, or Assistant Director. 
You should not attend this programme, if you:
• do not have any formal Reporting Officer duties, and/or 
• have attended the First Time Managers' Programme (FTMP) or the Manager LEAD (MLEAD) programme.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Evaluation Planning at Programme Design Stage
description:
(Do note that CRIPE12 was the previous code for this programme)

Programme evaluation enables the Government to ascertain if public spending delivers value and is cost effective. This is important because the Public Sector is accountable for the achievement of outcomes and must uphold public trust by ensuring our spending is value-for-money, especially given rising expenditures to address current and longer-term challenges.

Since 2020, the Ministry of Finance (MOF) has introduced requirements on evaluation planning for significant programmes via the Enhanced Evaluation Plan. The plan aims to align understanding and expectations for funding proposals between programme owners, MOF, and other stakeholders.

During this course, you will learn about Theory of Change, which articulates a logical pathway from intervention to desired outcomes/impact for the targeted audience. Through hands-on exercises, you will also learn how to set "SMART" (Specific, Measurable, Attainable, Relevant and Tim
learning_outcomes:
Explain how a policy/programme can benefit from evaluation planning
Apply Theory of Change concepts to articulate how a policy/programme leads to its desired outcomes
Practise "SMART" principles when setting indicators for a policy/programme
Practise filling in the Evaluation Details for a policy/programme, that sets out the requirements for the evaluation.
Apply Data Collection principles to collect information required to evaluate a policy/programme
who_should_attend: You conduct evaluation work, or are a Policy & Planning officer, Strategic Planning officer, or Finance officer involved in the planning and/or conducting of an evaluation for a programme. This course is especially relevant for officers with minimal knowledge on the covered topics and work on policies to encourage behavioural changes.

title: Be China Savvy: Understanding China and the Chinese Mindset
description:
(Do note that CRCSS10 was the previous code for this programme)

China's rise to become the world’s second largest economy is reflected in its close economic ties with Singapore. Given our longstanding bilateral co-operation and exchanges between Singapore and China, you may be involved in China-related projects, either working in China or hosting Chinese delegates. To build a strong working relationship with your Chinese counterparts, knowing how to engage them is a good first start. This interactive 1-day programme for the busy executive aims to equip you with the essentials – from hosting government officials, to using WeChat to communicate with the Chinese and how to carry a conversation.
 

[Please note: This programme will be held at external venue, outside of CSC]
learning_outcomes:
Distinguish the ‘Do’s’ and ‘Don’ts’ of Chinese etiquette and business protocol
 
Demonstrate how to interact effectively with Chinese counterparts using the correct business protocol and etiquette
 
Communicate effectively using appropriate business terms to reduce misunderstandings and build relationships
 
Frame communication positively and convincingly, through a sound understanding of socio-cultural and socio-linguistic differences to negotiate with Chinese counterparts 
 
Explain how to develop meaningful networks in terms of protocol and etiquette with the Chinese government officials
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer involved in international relations work, particularly with China. This programme is especially relevant for officers who need to interact with the Chinese for work or social purposes.

title: Spokesmanship - Half-day
description:
(Do note that CRSPM12 was the previous code for this programme)

Arm the media with well-crafted messages that are clear and concise, communicating your corporate messaging effectively. Understand how to engage the media from the perspective of a journalist. 




Media spokespersons need to be armed with the techniques, skills and knowledge in managing the media, regardless of the situation and the method of engagement. Strategic management of the media boosts brand and image of corporations. 




Deploying an effective training methodology of reinforcing knowledge and skills through hands-on practicum in a professional, realistic environment, this workshop is designed to impart participants with the tools, techniques, knowledge and skills in managing the media effectively, regardless of way the media engages them. 




This training is customised to suit the unique training needs of individual media spokespersons so that they will be able to engage the media looking good, sounding con
learning_outcomes:
Gain insights to the basic dos and don’ts of virtual and in-person media engagement platforms
Be equipped with the essential skills of an effective media spokesperson such as techniques on facing the camera, screen presence, crafting bridging statements and key messages
Craft effective self-introductions
Handle door-stop interviews
Manage media conferences/ press briefings
Gain experience through hands-on TV interviews and virtual interviews simulation exercises

title: Spokesmanship - 1-day
description:
(Do note that CRSPM10 was the previous code for this programme)

Arm the media with well-crafted messages that are clear and concise, communicating your corporate messaging effectively. Understand how to engage the media from the perspective of a journalist. 

Media spokespersons need to be armed with the techniques, skills and knowledge in managing the media, regardless of the situation and the method of engagement. Strategic management of the media boosts brand and image of corporations. 

Deploying an effective training methodology of reinforcing knowledge and skills through hands-on practicum in a professional, realistic environment, this workshop is designed to impart participants with the tools, techniques, knowledge and skills in managing the media effectively, regardless of way the media engages them. 

This training is customised to suit the unique training needs of individual media spokespersons so that they will be able to engage the media looking good, sounding confident de
learning_outcomes:
•	Gain insights to the basic dos and don’ts of virtual and in-person media engagement platforms

•	Be equipped with the essential skills of an effective media spokesperson such as techniques on facing the camera, screen presence, crafting bridging statements and key messages

•	Craft effective self-introductions

•	Plan and prepare how to face the media in the event of a crisis

•	Handle door-stop interviews

•	Manage media conferences/ press briefings

•	Gain experience through hands-on TV interviews and virtual interviews simulation exercises

title: Case Writing Workshop
description:
(Do note that CRRCW10 was the previous code for this programme)

Across public service organisations in Singapore, there has been an increasing need to codify and document experience in policy development and implementation, or best practices and operations behind key events such as the restructuring of government organisations.

Codification and documentation build up heritage and archival resources for the Public Service and facilitate knowledge transfer - an important aspect of sustaining organisational excellence and capturing important lessons in policy development and implementation.

The case study is one of the forms of capturing knowledge. It is a powerful tool that describes events, highlight issues, and present challenges in a way that engages readers. It is also a way to capture knowledge and lessons that might be lost over time through personnel and policy changes. One of the techniques in case study writing is through the use of narratives to document public sector knowle

title: Introduction to Case Writing
description:
(Do note that ELRCW11 was the previous code for this programme)

The case study is a powerful tool to engage readers by situating them in real and complex events. It may be a simple scenario about policy rationales, or a multi-dimensional narrative about conflicts and dilemmas in policymaking, the case study is a trove of knowledge and insights. Whether you are writing it for yourself or your organisation, storytelling with words is a life skill and you can apply these techniques in other areas of your work.

Anyone can write a case study. This 5-week course is designed for first-time case writers who are keen to use case studies for communications, training and knowledge-capture.  It will introduce you to the components of a case study and give you the skills to write a simple narrative effectively. Most of all, you will learn to write case studies by writing one yourself, with coaching from a case writing expert. So, bring along your own case study idea to make this course your own!
learning_outcomes:
Identify the key components of a case study
Apply writing techniques to write an engaging case study
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is keen to write case studies for communication, training and knowledge-capture.

title: Systems Thinking for Problem Solving and Strategy Creation
description:
(Do note that ELST was the previous code for this programme)

Are you tired of facing complex problems at work without a clear solution? Do you often find yourself stuck in a cycle of trial and error, unsure of how to navigate today's challenges? Join our self-paced Systems Thinking programme to transform your problem solving approach.

In today's fast-paced world, traditional methods fall short. We introduce you to the powerful concept of Systems Thinking and equip you with the tools to revolutionise your approach to problem solving and strategy creation.

By attending this programme, you will gain the knowledge and skills needed to tackle complex challenges with confidence. Take control of your learning journey and master the art of Systems Thinking to excel in your professional endeavours. Join us for a transformative self-paced learning experience.
learning_outcomes:
See systems, and recognise the complexities involved,
See that a reductionist approach will not be able to solve complex problems,
Use systems thinking tools to ask deeper questions and see invisible root causes,
Create solutions that are sustainable and effective,
Spot unintended consequences caused by quick-fix, symptomatic solutions,
Appreciate the key elements of culture and organisational transformation.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who seek to enhance your problem-solving and strategy creation skills by adopting a Systems Thinking approach.

title: Are you Ready for AI? Delivering a Successful AI Project
description:
(Do note that CRAIP10 was the previous code for this programme)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) are transforming how we work, play and live. The question is, are you ready for AI, and do you know what it takes to deliver an AI project in your organisation? 

In this programme, you will learn about the possibilities, limitations and trending uses of AI/ML. More importantly, you will find out what the end-to-end process is like to develop a simple AI/ML application and have the opportunity to develop one during the programme! You will also learn the basic know-hows, and build your confidence when planning, scoping and implementing an AI/ML project within your organisation.
learning_outcomes:
Develop a basic understanding of the types of AI/ML technologies and their potential application
Appreciate the challenges & success factors in delivering a successful AI/ML project
 
Experience the end-to-end process of developing a simple AI/ML application
 
Develop the basic know-how, correct mindset and confidence in scoping and implementing AI/ML projects
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who wishes to learn more about what it takes to deliver an AI/ML project or is tasked to implement an AI/ML project.

title: Team Leader Programme
description:
Singaporean public service leaders are tasked with leading change and collaboration across the Public Service to ensure that public agencies and officers are ready to embrace the future.

A key success factor for leaders today, facing fast-paced change and complexity, is to set their teams up for high performance. Teams are high-performing by design, not happenstance. The 6 Team Conditions research and practice show that 80% of the variance of a team’s performance is accounted for by six conditions.

The Team Leader Programme is designed with the busy leader in mind, to equip learners with practical knowledge and tools for designing, launching, and coaching to build a high-performing team.
learning_outcomes:
On completion of the Team Leader Programme, participants will be able to:

Explain science-based principles for leading teams and identify the critical stages of team development using research-proven methodologies.

Use a practical toolkit to design, launch, and coach teams by implementing the key components that drive high-performance team effectiveness.

Create customised action plans that enhance team effectiveness within their specific organisational context.

Use the Team Leader Diagnostic (TLD) to measure current team effectiveness levels and identify areas for improvement.

Access programme resources throughout their career to support ongoing team leadership and development initiatives.
who_should_attend: Deputy Directors and above

title: EES WOG Sharing
description:
(Do note that EVEEWOG was the previous code for this programme)

This session is meant to share WOG EES quantitative and qualitative results and how EES can be used as a way to understand organisation’s health across Singapore Public Service.
learning_outcomes:
Learning outcomes: 
 

Useful engagement practices from the ground 
Insights gleaned from WOG EES data 
Generative ideas for improving engagement in public service agencies
who_should_attend: HR/OD Directors and Staff Public Service Leaders

title: HR Newsroom: Business Partnership in Focus
description:
(Do note that EVHRBP was the previous code for this programme)

What skills are needed for HR to be a great partner to the business? Dive into our immersive experience and discover the core skills to elevate your strategic impact and influence!
learning_outcomes: Identify the skills required for HR to be a great partner to the business
who_should_attend: Any HR Officer

title: Turbocharging Your Leadership (Senior Leaders)
description:
(Do note that CRTYL02 was the previous code for this programme)

Research shows that leaders' negative behaviours matter just as much as positive behaviours for organisational and staff outcomes, including staff attitudes, job satisfaction, work performance and turnover. Leaders can be very competent in their work and still be perceived to display red flags. At the Public Service level, the Senior Leader 360 findings reveal that the most effective leaders are differentiated from others by their red flag behaviours.

In this workshop, we will discuss why leaders might unintentionally exhibit negative behaviours and how these behaviours can be mitigated, so that effective leadership performance is not undermined.
learning_outcomes:
Gain a deeper appreciation for the science of leadership
Discuss the factors behind leaders’ red flag behaviours
Explore strategies to mitigate negative leadership behaviours, while enhancing positive leadership behaviours
who_should_attend: You are a Senior Leader who is interested to turbocharge your leadership.

title: Internal Audit Foundation Programme
description:
(Do note that MSIAFP was the previous code for this programme)

The Internal Audit Foundation Programme is a milestone programme jointly developed by the Ministry of Finance’s Finance and Procurement Academy (FPA), AGD Assurance and Civil Service College (CSC), with the support of the IA community. It is designed for junior internal audit officers commencing their careers in the Public Service.




This five-day programme will help you gain a deeper understanding of your role within the Public Sector Internal Audit function and build peer networks across the community. To support the transfer of learning, curated interventions will extend your learning beyond the classroom into the workplace, enabling you to apply key concepts in real-world contexts.
learning_outcomes:
By the end of the programme, learners would be able to:
Explain the role of Internal Audit (IA) within existing governance structures
Recognise key driving forces, emerging risks, and transformation opportunities shaping the public sector IA landscape
Describe the responsibilities, expectations, and competencies required of IA officers
Apply key internal audit methodologies and tools in accordance with professional standards and internal audit principles
Describe recurring audit lapses across domains (e.g., procurement, grants, IT)
who_should_attend: New public officers with less than 2 years of experience in Public Sector Internal Audit.

title: Turbocharging Your Leadership (Directors)
description:
(Do note that CRTYL01 was the previous code for this programme)

Research shows that leaders’ negative behaviours matter just as much as positive behaviours for organisational and staff outcomes, including staff attitudes, job satisfaction, work performance, and turnover. Leaders can be very competent in their work and still be perceived to display red flags. 


At the Public Service level, the Senior Leader 360 findings reveal that the most effective leaders are differentiated from the others by the red flag behaviours.


In this programme, we will discuss why leaders may exhibit negative behaviours and how these behaviours can be mitigated, so that leadership strengths are not undermined.
learning_outcomes:
Gain a deeper appreciation of the science of leadership
Discuss the factors behind leaders’ red flag behaviours
Explore strategies to mitigate negative leadership behaviours, while enhancing positive leadership behaviours
who_should_attend: You are a director who has participated in the Director LCF360 in the past 2 years, and is interested to turbocharge your leadership.

title: Facilitation and Design Skills for OD Practitioners
description:
(Do note that CRFA101 was the previous code for this programme)

 

As an Organisation Development (OD) practitioner, you may frequently need to facilitate focus group discussions, corporate planning and action planning workshops. Very often, the quality of the discussion during such sessions is affected by the quality of facilitation. Investing in our ability to facilitate effectively can reap significant dividends for all parties involved.  How might you improve your competency in facilitation? What principles or considerations should you bear in mind when designing facilitated sessions? What core and soft skills do you need to hone to be an effective facilitator? What tools and techniques can you use to facilitate small and large group discussions?

 

This three-day programme will provide answers to these questions. It will also offer you an opportunity for hands-on practice and receive  feedback on your practice to help you become a skilful facilitator.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the purpose and significance of facilitation and identifying appropriate contexts for application.
Assess the specific requirements and apply critical thinking skills to design and deliver facilitated sessions, using the SPOT Framework.
Develop core facilitation skills such as effective questioning techniques, active listening, and synthesising of information.
Apply creative methods, tools, and techniques for facilitating small and large group sessions, including meetings, group discussions, corporate planning, visioning and staff engagement workshops.
Evaluate the delivery of facilitated sessions, ensuring alignment with objectives and desired outcomes.
who_should_attend: You are an OD Practitioner who has to facilitate and design interventions such as focus group discussions, action planning workshops, and corporate planning sessions. This programme is open to Singapore Public Officers only.

title: Data Visualisation For Effective Communications
description:
(Do note that CRSEC10 was the previous code for this programme)

In today's data-driven world, the art of visualisation turns complex insights into clear, persuasive narratives that resonate with your audience. 

This two-day programme combines virtual and in-person classroom learning for an immersive experience. Day 1 introduces you to core visualisation principles through online interactive sessions. On Day 2, you'll apply these principles in-person, diving deep into Tableau and working on case study to create and present impactful visualisations. 

* This is a foundational course designed for officers looking to learn how to create clear, meaningful charts and dashboards that help others easily understand complex data and make better decisions.
learning_outcomes:
Explain the importance of data visualisation in driving decisions and communication 
Apply core principles to create clear, effective visualisations 
Select appropriate chart types to convey your data story 
Create interactive dashboards and compelling visual narratives using Tableau 
Leverage AI tools to enhance your visualisation workflow and troubleshoot common challenges
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public officer who is responsible for producing or communicating statistics to your stakeholders.

title: Your Perception Points by Ron Kaufman
description:
(Do note that ELYPP13 was the previous code for this programme)

In this course, you will investigate where and when you deliver your service and discover how customers perceive and evaluate your service, and where you can improve your service.

You will find new ways to improve your service and create more service value for your customers and colleagues.
learning_outcomes:
Learn how customers perceive your service
Learn how customers evaluate your service
Identify where you can improve your service
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is keen on Service Excellence.

title: Workshop On Randomised Controlled Trials In Public Policy
description:
(Do note that CRRCT10 was the previous code for this programme)

How do we know if the interventions that we design are likely to be effective? How do we generate solutions in an evidence-based way? Randomised Controlled Trials (RCTs) are considered to be the gold standard in effectiveness research. In this workshop, you will learn about the role of RCTs and how they can enhance the policymaking process. Through case studies and hands-on exercises, you will also learn the technical aspects, common obstacles and trade-offs policymakers might face in conducting RCTs.
learning_outcomes:
Apply RCTs in the policymaking process
Design RCTs to test new interventions relevant to your area of work.
Work with a team to conduct RCTs.
Recognize the trade-offs and common obstacles in conducting RCTs.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer involved in policy formulation, analysis and evaluation, with at least two years of work experience.

title: Workshop On Emotional Intelligence (EQ) For Support Officers
description:
(Do note that CRWEQ30 was the previous code for this programme)

Having a high IQ does not automatically guarantee success. Other "intelligent" factors such as self-awareness, impulse control, persistence, self-motivation and empathy are qualities which mark people who excel in life. These basic qualities are now commonly known as "Emotional Intelligence (EQ)".

People with high EQ enjoy fulfilling relationships with others while those who lack EQ may suffer from poor health due to depression, eating disorders and other aggressive behaviours, which will have a negative impact on work performance.

You will learn how you can improve your relationships with others by developing your intrapersonal and interpersonal skills at this two-day workshop.
learning_outcomes:
Ask the right questions to get the right answers
Improve interactions with others
Influence one's actions resulting in better interpersonal interaction
Increase understanding of work motivation and job satisfaction
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who wishes to apply Emotional Intelligence to better support your team or department.

title: Building your Learning Agility for Career Fitness (Support Officers)
description:
(Do note that CRDLA23 was the previous code for this programme)

 

In today’s fast-changing workplace, staying adaptable is crucial. Your role may evolve, new tools may be introduced, or responsibilities may shift. Learn how to adapt quickly and efficiently and stay ahead in this programme that focuses on practical activities that directly relate to your job. You will leave with useful skills and a clear plan for personal and professional growth.
learning_outcomes:
At the end of this workshop, learners will be able to:

Understand Learning Agility: Know what it is and why it’s important.
Know Your Learning Profile: Discover how you learn best.
Overcome Learning Barriers: Find ways to quickly tackle obstacles.
Build Key Skills for the Future: Identify and start developing essential skills.
Create a Simple Learning Plan: Develop an actionable plan.
who_should_attend: You are a support officer seeking to identify learning and development opportunities, create tailored learning strategies, and build comprehensive action plans to proactively upskill or reskill in response to industry demands and personal aspirations.

title: Winning Compliance by World Class Benchmarking
description:
(Do note that ELWWC13 was the previous code for this programme)

Discover proven approaches that win more compliance in a less costly manner than enacting typical enforcement procedures. Learn the difference between enforcing compliance and winning compliance – based on the new R.U.L.E.S. for winning compliance.

[COMPLIMENTARY MODULE] EXCELLENCE AND YOU

This module provides an important overview of how leadership excellence, employee engagement, and great customer experiences provide for organizational excellence. It’s the formula for putting organizing all of the ideas shared in this curriculum.

This is one of the five eLearning modules of "Lead with Your Customer Foundation Series.
learning_outcomes: Apply R.U.L.E.S. for winning compliance through use of world-class case studies and interactive exercises to help you to build your own plan for winning compliance
who_should_attend: You are a Service operations and enforcement officers who oversee compliance work.

title: The Essentials of Contract Management
description:
(Do note that CRECM was the previous code for this programme)

Have you been tasked to manage a new procurement contract with a vendor? Do you know what are the components required in a contract, and What your roles and responsibilities are when managing a contract? What should you watch out for, to ensure the contract is managed effectively?


This programme is the revamped face-to-face two-day version of [Virtual Classroom] The Essentials of Contract Management. It focuses on the nine high-risk areas of contract management, helping you to avoid the common pitfalls. 


Learn through interactive e-learning, and scenario and peer discussions to uncover key steps to managing contracts effectively.
learning_outcomes:
By the end of this programme, you will be able to:
Identify the key components of a contract
Distinguish the different phases to manage a contract
Discover best practices to manage a contract in different phase
Reflect on possible ways to avoid common pitfalls
Uncover insights to apply back at the workplace
who_should_attend: You are new to managing contracts and have been tasked to oversee a contract for ITQ (Invitation to Quote), ITT (Invitation to Tender) or DA (Demand Aggregate) Procurement. You are recommended to complete Procurement e-Learning 1.1 (ELPELPN), before attending this programme.

title: The Singapore Synthesis Workshop Series
description:
(Do note that CRTSS10 was the previous code for this programme)

The Singapore Synthesis workshop series will challenge public officers to refresh their approaches to policymaking and raise the level of ambition and alignment across Singapore’s social and economic policies, based on deeper analytical rigour and a clearer articulation of purpose. The workshop series will take as the starting point, four public lectures delivered by Mr Ravi Menon in 2021 as the 9th S R Nathan Fellow, where he shared a vision for Singapore as an innovative economy, inclusive society, and inspiring nation.

Building on these lectures, Mr Ravi Menon will facilitate a programme for mutual learning among senior public officers over 4 workshops and 1 Brown Bag session. At each workshop, supported by a public service leader, learners will unpack the ideas behind each lecture, and hear a range of perspectives from inside and outside government, to provoke fresh thinking and new ideas to take Singapore forward. T
learning_outcomes:
1. Examine the prevailing assumptions, trade-offs, and belief systems underlying our policy thinking on socio-economic issues.

2. Use a multi-disciplinary lens to analyse social and economic policy issues and achieve better coherence across policies for more optimal outcomes.

3. Develop principles and analytical frameworks for making policy trade-offs and adjusting policies in response to new evidence or changed circumstances.
who_should_attend: You are a Senior Director/Director/Deputy Director with an interest in, and experience in policy and planning.

title: The Back Of The Napkin Workshop
description:
(Do note that CRBON13 was the previous code for this programme)

Visual thinking is a simple and powerful way to solve business problems as it activates your innate ability to see – both with your eyes and your mind's eye – and helps you discover ideas that you never saw, and develop these ideas in a rapid and intuitive way.
learning_outcomes:
Improve your innate ability to think and communicate visually
Solve problems using the Visual Problem-solving Toolkit
Identify the type of problems you face, what elements compose it and which simple pictures best clarify and solve that problem using the 6x6 Rule
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who wants to find a better way to solve problems through visual thinking.

title: The Design Thinking Toolbox
description:
(Do note that CRTDTTB was the previous code for this programme)

Are you interested to deep dive into the mindsets and tools of the human-centred design process and approach?

This intensive programme offers you the opportunity to delve deeper into design thinking and gain practical insights and tools on the human-centred design approach. Learn how design thinking methods can be applied to public sector challenges via hands-on activities. You will discover how to experiment with ideas to improve work processes and service delivery, as well as learn the importance of considering perspectives of different stakeholders.

If you are new to the human-centred design process, sign up for this programme now to experience applying the process of design thinking to a real problem.
learning_outcomes:
Understand and apply the end-to-end design thinking process and mindset
Recognise the importance of empathy to understand users and uncover unmet needs in public sector challenges
Apply practical tools, mindsets, and thinking skills to own areas of work with little guidance
Discover the concept and value of iteration and low fidelity in the design thinking process
who_should_attend: Public officers who are keen to understand how design thinking can be applied to policies, service delivery, communication and other public challenges.

title: Teaming for Winning: Enabling Leaders for Team Success
description:
(Do note that CRTFWP was the previous code for this programme)

Embark on a transformative journey with "Teaming for Winning: Enabling Team Leaders for Success" – a Teaming programme designed to catapult first-time project leaders and managers into the realm of team leadership. This 2-day experience delves into the two questions that most team leaders ask: "What are the conditions for successful teams?" and "How can I create these conditions as a leader?"

In Teaming for Winning, you will walk away with practical tools and strategies to implement immediately in your workplace. You'll learn to wield powerful models like GRPI and SBI, and harness the transformative power of effective communication. With a perfect blend of research and real-world application, all reflected through the lens of the Singapore Public Service, you'll emerge ready to tackle both the task and human aspects of team management with confidence.

Come and find out how you can create the ideal conditions for your pro
learning_outcomes:
Understand the conditions necessary for a high performing team
Gain awareness of your strengths and working style and your role as a team leader
Understand psychological safety and establish effective conversational practices in teams (e.g., giving effective feedback, listening, asking good questions)
Engage diverse team members and strengthen your influence to achieve desired project outcomes
who_should_attend:
You are a team lead in charge of an intact team, or you have been tasked to lead a temporary project team*. A temporary project team may take the form of a task force, an inter-departmental project, or a cross-agency project. **You are welcome to join this programme even if you are an informal leader without positional authority (i.e. you are required to mobilise people who may not necessarily be your direct reports).
$1,493.30/ participant
Including 9% GST of $123.30
