Public Service Learning Fest 2026​

Public Service Learning Fest 2026 brings learning to life

title: Strategic Social Media Management
description:
(Do note that CRSSM10 was the previous code for this programme)

This course equips public officers with the skills to manage social media accounts for Ministries, Statutory Boards, Organs of States, as well as political office holders (POHs), engage with online communities, and navigate the complexities of modern digital communication. The course incorporates insights from practitioners, practical exercises, and up-to-date strategies for social media management.
learning_outcomes:
Apply social media strategies to shape public and political discourse, and build better connections with diverse audiences through targeted messaging.
Gain insights into the key trends and best practices in managing social media for public agencies.
Learn approaches to optimise social media strategy and online presence/image of political office leaders, including voice, tone, style and content strategy for better outreach and resonance.
Identify strategies to build relationships with creators and influencers to strengthen digital strategy, including shifting perceptions and addressing mis-, disinformation.
Gain insights from trainer’s experience in handling major events and incidents.
who_should_attend: You are an intermediate-level middle and senior management level public officer supervising or leading digital teams.

title: Step Up Procurement: An Intermediate Course
description:
(Do note that CRPROC2 was the previous code for this programme)

Step Up Procurement: An Intermediate Course is a new procurement policy programme which replaces the Procurement Specialist Programme. It is a learning journey, continuing from Procurement e-learning 1.1. Topics covered include the Government Procurement Act and the subsidiary legislations as well as the different procurement contracting approaches. You will also learn how to deal with common procurement-related issues when working with users and suppliers.


The Step Up Procurement learning journey incorporates asynchronous bite-size digital learning content and face-to-face interactive case study discussions. It extends beyond the classroom and into your workplace with a carefully designed post-class application component to complete your learning. Step Up Procurement can also be supplemented with existing procurement programmes in specification writing, evaluation methodologies and contract management.
learning_outcomes:
Describe the importance of the Agreement on Government Procurement and Free Trade Agreements and how they are applied to the underlying principles and practices in Government Procurement
Discuss how to apply the different procurement approaches in various scenarios when awarding notice in GeBIZ
Demonstrate the ability to structure contracts to ensure fairness and value-for-money when evaluating bid amendment and contract variation requests
Communicate decisions on bid amendments and procurement approaches to suppliers and user agencies
Assess and provide feedback on supplier’s performance using the GeBIZ Supplier Rating Tool
who_should_attend: You perform procurement duties as a core function in your work and identify with the Procurement Competency Framework at Proficiency Level 2 and above. You have completed Procurement e-Learning 1.1 (ELPELPN).

title: Situational Risk Assessment For Enforcement Officers
description:
As a frontline enforcement officer, you need to accurately identify potential threats and take the necessary precautions to mitigate those threats. You must also react appropriately in difficult situations, and learn to recognise when the situation requires you to disengage and call for backup. Ask yourself: Are you fully prepared to do what’s necessary? Failure is not an option.

Specifically designed for civilian enforcement officers, this programme will equip you with the ability to identify, process and comprehend critical elements of the environment before and during enforcement operations. You’ll also learn how to take the necessary precautions to ensure everyone’s safety during enforcement operations.
learning_outcomes:
Describe what field intelligence and field reconnaissance are
Perform situational awareness and risk assessment prior and during enforcement engagements
Plan and implement field reconnaissance and enforcement operations
Adapt and respond in accordance with agency’s SOPs during enforcement operations
Evaluate a deployment or enforcement activity for reporting
who_should_attend: You are a frontline enforcement officer in the Singapore Public Service.

title: Soft Wiring for a Customer-centred Service Culture by World Class Benchmarking
description:
(Do note that ELSCC13 was the previous code for this programme)

Excellent results don’t happen by accident. The outcomes of any organisation are the reflection of the organisation’s influence on its employee experience, level of innovation and agility, as well as customer experience. Most efforts to improve often mistakenly focus on creating organisational changes and new policies and procedures - a process that is known as "hard-wiring" change.

However, the most successful organisations use a more effective alternative called "soft-wiring", which focuses on engaging employees to achieve buy-in, support, and high-performing delivery of an excellent experience to create a culture of service excellence. Through interactions and case studies in this programme, you will discover proven soft-wiring approaches to the following four areas.

[COMPLIMENTARY MODULE] EXCELLENCE AND YOU

This module provides an important overview of how leadership excellence, employee engagement, and great custo
learning_outcomes:
Gain insights into the best practices in "soft-wiring" approaches to build a customer-centred service culture
Analyse "soft-wiring" approaches used by world class organisations and create a "soft-wiring" implementation plan for your organisation to build a customer-centred service culture
who_should_attend: You are a Service Managers who is keen to deliver a great customer experience.

title: Senior Management Programme
description:
(Do note that MSSNMP1 was the previous code for this programme)

The Senior Management Programme (SMP) is a 6.5-week full-time milestone programme designed for experienced Directors. The SMP serves as a platform to support participants to lead effectively within their organisations. The programme will provide the foundation for them to take on more leadership responsibilities within the Public Service.
who_should_attend: Experienced Directors from the Administrative, Professional and Uniformed Services, who are in the Superscale grade and have the potential to take on senior leadership positions in the Public Service.

title: Serving Customers with Disabilities Effectively
description:
(Do note that CRSCD23 was the previous code for this programme)

Some of the customers that you serve as a frontline officer have multiple complex needs. To serve them empathetically, you will need to understand their needs and learn ways to communicate with them.




In partnership with SG Enable, this half-day programme will equip you with a better understanding of how to serve and communicate with persons with disabilities. You will gain an overview of the different types of disabilities, how to communicate and interact with persons with disabilities, as well as understand the challenges faced by them. This face-to-face programme will be held at Enabling Village.
learning_outcomes:
Be familiar with the challenges encountered and constraints faced by people with disabilities
Display empathy when communicating with persons with disabilities
Adopt effective ways to serve people with disabilities
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer in the Service Frontline function who serve customers with disabilities in the course of your work.

title: Rise Together: Mastering the Foundations of Mentoring
description:
(Do note that CRRTMFM was the previous code for this programme)

Ready to be a mentor at the workplace or provide a frame to your existing mentoring capabilities? This 2-day face-to-face foundational programme is designed for experienced middle managers and directors who are responsible for developing individuals and teams within the organisation, or who are interested in fostering a developmental climate through mentoring within the Public Service. While no prior experience in mentoring is required, this programme will be more useful for those who are about to embark on a mentoring relationship or are already functioning as a mentor, either formally or informally.

Explore the role of mentorship in building leaders in organisations, delve within to gain a deeper understanding of your uniqueness as a mentor, and learn core skills to build your mentoring capabilities. This practical programme will help you understand the fundamentals of what makes a mentor-mentee relationship successful
learning_outcomes:
Gain a deeper understanding of the role of a mentor and the scope of mentoring
Inquire into the conditions that make a mentoring relationship work, and the pitfalls to avoid
Enhance self-awareness in mentoring through the cultivation of personal reflective spaces
Begin building intentionality in your mentoring approach in order to meet the needs and goals of the mentees
Learn to apply core skills involved in mentoring with the intention to more effectively empower and nurture mentees
who_should_attend: You are new to mentoring, would like to become more effective in your role as a mentor, and hold the designation of Deputy Director to Directors or equivalent.

title: Robotic Process Automation (RPA) Programme for Senior Management
description:
(Do note that CRRPASM was the previous code for this programme)

Robotic Process Automation (RPA) has gained traction in the Public Service as a means to automate high volumes of tedious, repetitive and manual work processes. Several agencies have adopted RPA solutions and built bots to improve existing work processes in various functions, such as HR, Finance and Administration. But what processes are suitable for RPA, and how can leaders support their teams and agencies in successful RPA implementation?


"The first rule of any technology used in a business is that automation applied to an efficient operation will magnify the efficiency. The second is that automation applied to an inefficient operation will magnify the inefficiency" - Bill Gates.


This programme will equip Senior Management (Middle Managers to Directors) with foundational skills and knowledge on RPA use cases and the factors for successful RPA implementation. You will be able to lead your team to discover effective u
learning_outcomes:
Appreciate what RPA is about; how it works, its key features, functions, capabilities, risks and challenges
Appreciate how RPA can complement process improvement methodologies, e.g. Lean
Identify suitable work processes for automation
Describe organisational factors required to support successful RPA implementation
Describe the end-to-end RPA implementation approach including the activities, roles and key outputs required for a successful implementation
Build an RPA bot using UiPath
who_should_attend: You are a Deputy Director or Director looking into using RPA as a solution to improve your team’s or agency’s business processes.

title: Reading the Compass of Customers by World Class Benchmarking
description:
(Do note that ELRCC13 was the previous code for this programme)

Just as a compass provides guidance on the direction you want to take in a journey you are on, so too is a customer guided by an internal compass when it comes to decisions they are taking. 

[COMPLIMENTARY MODULE] EXCELLENCE AND YOU

This module provides an important overview of how leadership excellence, employee engagement, and great customer experiences provide for organizational excellence. It’s the formula for putting organizing all of the ideas shared in this curriculum.

This is one of the five eLearning modules of "Lead with Your Customer Foundation Series".
learning_outcomes:
Understand how to connect with your customers at a deeper level on five essential human needs
Apply the four individual and personal styles of how differently people respond to life around them to meet customer expectations
who_should_attend: You are a service practitioner (up to middle manager level) who is keen on Service Excellence.

title: Red-Teaming for Government Communications
description:
(Do note that CRRTC10 was the previous code for this programme)

Red-teaming is a strategic tool used to identify blind spots and counter-narratives, challenge existing assumptions, and explore diverse stakeholder perspectives. Mastery of this skill enables participants to mitigate risks, seize opportunities, and make informed decisions on how to communicate and engage the population on government policies/announcements.




This workshop is specifically designed to guide participants through the process of conducting red-teaming exercises for public communications tailored to their respective agencies or departments. Jointly organised by Messaging and Engagement Department (MED), MDDI, PMO-Comms Group and the Academy of Public Communications and Engagement (APCE), the workshop emphasises the critical role of red-teaming as part of the Government comms and engagement process.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Service Officer (Middle Manager) managing government communications and engagement in your organisation.

title: Prompt Engineering: A Practical Guide to Success
description:
(Do note that CRPME10 was the previous code for this programme)

The world of AI has changed the way we work- with increasing interest, use of chatbots and Generative Pre-Trained Transformers (GPTs). How can we leverage these models to help us do our work more effectively? The answer is in giving these models effective prompts 

Join us in this programme that was specially designed to provide you with a practical guide to prompt engineering, focusing on the key principles and techniques for designing and implementing effective prompts. You will also have the chance to design prompts for different specific tasks using LaunchPad (a GovTech platform to enable officers to experience AI safely and securely).
learning_outcomes:
Gain a better understanding of the architecture and key concepts of Large Language Models (LLMs), including generative AI and tools like ChatGPT.
Recognise the limitations, pitfalls, and ethical considerations of using LLMs, and discern between good use and misuse.
Grasp the concept of prompt engineering and its critical role in guiding LLM responses.
Appreciate the principles of good prompt design, including clarity, context setting, and managing output length and verbosity.
Apply key LLM parameters and techniques for specific use cases (generate, classify, search, cluster, extract, rewrite, summarise).
Explore advanced prompt engineering techniques, including iterative prompt development, eliciting specific or creative responses, reducing biases and improving reliability.
Explore real-world applications of LLMs, learn from case studies, and understand best practices for integration.
Comprehend the future evolution of generative AI, including its potential, key advancements such as Da
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who may be an individual contributor or a data business owner. You are keen to learn the power of prompts to get the most appropriate AI responses from generative language models.

title: Protocol Workshop For Event Organisers (International Event)
description:
(Do note that CRPWEO1 was the previous code for this programme)

Public officers are often tasked with organising and managing large‑scale international events, official functions, and high‑level visits. From arranging courtesy calls and official meetings to managing interviews and ceremonial elements, getting protocol and etiquette right is essential to upholding Singapore’s professional standing and ensuring smooth engagements.

This programme equips participants with practical knowledge and skills to plan and manage international events and functions with confidence, applying appropriate protocol, business etiquette, and cross‑cultural sensitivities in official settings.To aid public officers in their preparations leading up to Singapore’s ASEAN Chairmanship in 2027, the programme will also cover practices and protocol specific to ASEAN meetings.
learning_outcomes:
Understand and apply principles of protocol and business etiquette when organising and managing international events and functions
Demonstrate cross-cultural sensitivities and avoid common protocol mistakes at such events and functions
Incorporate and extend appropriate courtesies in events and functions for greeting, receiving and sending off guests with correct formalities
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer involved in organising international events. This programme is open to Singapore Public Officers only.

title: Procurement E-learning 1.1
description:
(Do note that ELPELPN was the previous code for this programme)

As a Singapore Public Officer, you need to have a good understanding of the Government Procurement policy framework and its operating principles and rules. In the 14 modules within this programme, you should be able to clearly understand and be able to apply the good practices in the Ministry of Finance's Instruction Manual (IM) on Procurement. This is a repackaged course from the previous "Procurement E-Learning Pathway: Tenders and Quotations" (ELPELP1), "Procurement E-Learning Pathway: Quotations" (ELPELP2) and "Procurement E-Learning Pathway: Tenders" (ELPELP3).
learning_outcomes:
Develop a clear mindset and understanding of the key principles of procurement.
Apply the key IM requirements and guidelines under the 8 stages of the Procurement Lifecycle.
Identify the key activities that need to be performed under the various procurement approaches.
who_should_attend: This programme is open for Singapore Public Officers who need to perform procurement duties in their line of work.

title: Procurement Leaders' Programme
description:
(Do note that MSPLDP1 was the previous code for this programme)

Even in the face of tightened fiscal funding, citizen expectations do not cease and are in fact, on the rise. As Procurement Leaders, it is imperative that you help your agencies navigate through this complex environment.




The Procurement Leaders Programme (MSPLDP1) is a leadership milestone programme jointly developed by the Finance and Procurement Academy (FPA) under the Ministry of Finance (MOF), and Civil Service College (CSC).




It has been specially designed to build up your capabilities as Procurement Leaders within the public sector by preparing you for the future operating context such as our fiscal environment, supply constraints and the global operating environmental forces.
learning_outcomes:
Examine the current trends in procurement and the changing operating context.
Describe the implications of Public Sector Transformation on the Procurement Function.
Adopt the mind-set to embrace business innovation in our resource-constrained environment.
Develop strategic procurement capabilities relevant to the public sector to drive innovation and deliver value beyond cost savings.
Gain deeper insights into emerging procurement capabilities from both public and private sectors to ensure organisation performance and success.
Gain strategies in stakeholder engagement and business partnering across organisations and government.
Build a stronger Whole-of-Government (WOG) procurement community.

title: Project Management Workshop For Support Officers
description:
(Do note that CRPRM23 was the previous code for this programme)

Project Management is an essential discipline and tool to ensure projects are implemented in an effective, efficient, and timely manner. Learn the core skills needed to become more effective in managing projects, and enhance your learning by sharing your work projects.
learning_outcomes:
Describe the need for Project Management and its components
Gain knowledge on how to achieve competency in the processes, tools and discipline of Project Management
Develop skills to deliver better project results in a timely and cost effective manner
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who supports your team or department in projects.

title: Principles Of Vetting For Senior Officers
description:
(Do note that CRPVT10 was the previous code for this programme)

Vetting is a critical step in any submission process. It is not simply about checking for grammatical errors or using the right format. Instead, it is about providing writers with a different set of lenses to review the documents and to ensure that messages are communicated effectively to the readers.

In this one-day programme, you will appreciate the importance of vetting and the role a vetter plays. You will also be taken through a systematic approach to vetting, specifically vetting for content alignment, organisation of points, writing style, language and formatting. There will be exercises for you to practise and apply these techniques. You will also learn about the developmental role of a vetter to provide constructive feedback and to communicate changes to the writers.
learning_outcomes:
Understand a vetter’s role, value and importance
Develop a systematic approach to vetting
Provide constructive feedback to writers as part of the vetting process
who_should_attend: You are a first line manager or equivalent and you are responsible for vetting your team’s work.

title: Process Improvement 101
description:
(Do note that CRPI101 was the previous code for this programme)

Are you expected to do more with less? How do you design workflows and business processes in your agency to optimise outcomes?

In this one-day programme, you will learn about process improvements tools and apply the methodologies to help your team and agency become more effective and efficient.

If you are new to Process Improvement, sign up for this introductory workshop now to learn more about the different process improvement tools that are available and how it can be relevant at work.
learning_outcomes:
Explain how Lean Management, Six Sigma, Business Process Re-engineering and Agile are used to drive improvements
Differentiate and assess the value of each methodology
Apply the appropriate methodology to optimise outcomes
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is looking to improve productivity with process improvement tools.

title: Organisational Speech Writing
description:
(Do note that CROSW10 was the previous code for this programme)

What does it take to write effective, compelling speeches? The key is to understand your target audience so that you can craft appealing messages that help build rapport between the speaker and them. You'll also need to know how to conduct in-depth research, and structure your speeches thoughtfully and with the appropriate language.
learning_outcomes:
Identify the key factors of a good speech.
Use the appropriate language when writing speeches for different target audiences.
Define the target audiences for different situations and discuss ways to craft speeches that build rapport.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who drafts speeches for your agency's principals.

title: No Wrong Door
description:
(Do note that ELNWD1 was the previous code for this programme)

Members of the public generally view the Public Service as a single entity. Since the No Wrong Door (NWD) policy was introduced in 2004, it has been largely successful in ensuring feedback from members of the public are addressed quickly and effectively, so as to provide a good service experience for them. Under NWD, when you receive a feedback that is not under your agency’s purview, you will act on the customer’s behalf to identify the right agency, ensure that the right agency will take up the case before referring the customer to the agency. If the feedback requires coordination from more than one agency, you may also need to coordinate and provide an integrated response.
learning_outcomes: Apply NWD streamlined process and protocols to simple and complex feedback to facilitate timely referral, coordination and resolution of public feedback at a Whole-of-Government level
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer whose work involves in inter-agency coordination as you deliver service to the public.

title: Narratives and Storytelling – Communicating to Engage
description:
(Do note that CRNSC10 was the previous code for this programme)

The use of strategic narratives, whether for internal communications, branding and campaign efforts or media pitching, can help people to understand, remember and connect with key messages better. This programme will guide you on how to use a 7-element structure to craft narratives for effective engagement of different audiences.
learning_outcomes:
Explain the importance of narratives and its application in public communications
Convert key messages (policy intents) into narratives to achieve desired campaign objectives
Craft meaningful narratives for different audience groups to communicate and influence
Conceptualise, develop and customise narratives for different media platforms
Anticipate sentiments/objections via narratives to achieve buy-in
who_should_attend: You are responsible for developing narratives and messages for communication efforts in your agency. You should preferably have at least 2-3 years of experience planning/running campaigns or doing media work.

title: Leveraging HR Analytics to Diagnose and Analyse People Management Issues
description:
(Do note that CRDSA12 was the previous code for this programme)

Are you keen to develop inferential statistical analysis skills to understand and explain the relationships that underpin HR issues?

This 2-day programme is designed to help you acquire the skills needed to develop an analysis framework, understand common statistical analysis techniques, and conduct statistical analysis with MS Excel.​


 

We offer different HR analytics courses that are tailored to different training needs across the HR analytics value chain. Please select and register for the HR analytics workshop that best suits your learning needs.

 

CRD​MRVL focuses on building skills in data management and reporting – the first link in the analytics value chain. If data is not managed or accessible, analytics cannot start.


CRHAZ12 focuses on the analytical processes after data management; more specifically, the analytical thought process, analytical methods, data visualization, and data storytelling.

CRDSA12
learning_outcomes:
Gain insights into the multifaceted nature of people management issues
Establish and explain relationships among key variables in issues
Diagnose and solve issues analytically
who_should_attend: You are a HR officer who is keen to go beyond descriptive analysis to apply inferential statistical analysis skills in solving people management issues.

title: Lead with Your Customer Foundational Programme by World Class Benchmarking
description:
(Do note that ELLCF13 was the previous code for this programme)

Organisations known for legendary customer engagement use special tools to go beyond merely knowing about their customers to truly understanding them.

When facing business challenges, many world class organisations understand that the most effective and fastest turnaround plan starts with really understanding people beyond the one-dimensional customer data. 

In this programme, you will receive the proven tools used by Fortune 500 companies and World Class public sector organisations to help your team to truly engage those you serve and earn loyalty during difficult times.

Here we’ve bundled all five programmes in the series together to offer you the most comprehensive approach to improving the customer experience, all the while providing the greatest value. You benefit by getting the most comprehensive approach for improving the customer experience. 

This bundle includes the following five eLearning programmes:

Readi
learning_outcomes:
Apply concepts to build a common direction and standards of behaviour that translate to consistently high levels of efficiencies as well as increased employee/customer engagement
Gain insights into practical tools used by leading public and private sector organisations that look at developing a compelling  Service Vision, Values and Behaviours
Apply '6P' Service Netting Framework [6P: Promise, People, Place, Processes, Products and Price] to go beyond just products and services to create an engaging experience that sets you apart as different and better
Gain insights on best practices in "soft-wiring" approaches to build a customer-centred service culture
Analyse "soft-wiring" approaches used by world class organisations and create a "soft-wiring" implementation plan for your organisation to build a customer-centred service culture
Apply the R.U.L.E.S. framework for winning compliance through use of world-class case studies and interactive exercises to help you to build your own plan f
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is keen on Service Excellence.

title: Investigative Interview Techniques
description:
(Do note that CRIIT13 was the previous code for this programme)

As an investigation officer, you use interviews extensively to establish the facts of the case or complaint you are looking into. A good interview will have a positive impact on the outcome of your investigation.

However, if you do not have the right interview skills, or apply the wrong interview techniques, not only would the interview results be rendered useless, it could also cause serious negative consequences for your organisation. Therefore, it is important for you to acquire the knowledge and skills needed to conduct interviews, and learn how to apply them.
learning_outcomes:
Articulate the legal issues involved in various techniques of interviewing
Identify the common signs of deception by interviewees
Illustrate the various techniques for interviewing through an experimental approach
who_should_attend: You are an Enforcement Officer who conducts investigations within your jurisdiction, or a public officer who is involved in investigative work. This programme is open to Singapore Public Officers only.

title: Introduction to Effective Writing in the Public Service
description:
(Do note that CRIEW10 was the previous code for this programme)

Officers are expected to write effectively with clarity, sensitivity and impact for different purposes, and in different formats. 

In this programme, you will learn strategies and techniques to think clearly and write concisely while considering the various situational or staffing context. You will also learn on the use of Large Language Model (LLM) in writing.
learning_outcomes:
Discuss the importance and value of good writing in the Public Service.
Apply the Planning and Producing framework to their writing.
Understand how AI and LLM work and the impact on writing
Distinguish and adapt writing to various situational or staffing context.
Evaluate and learn to re-write to improve on your writing.
who_should_attend: Senior officers and managers who are either new or will like to improve their writing skills.

title: Introduction to Data Management
description:
(Do note that CRDMG10 was the previous code for this programme)

Data management is the process of collecting, storing, processing, and analysing data to extract meaningful insights and make informed decisions. Effective data management can help organisations to improve their operations, increase efficiency, and gain a competitive advantage. However, with the increasing amount of date being generated daily in this digital age, how can you manage and analyse them effectively? 


This programme was designed for learners who wish to learn the basics of data management and the approach to ensure proper handling, access and use of data, join us in this one-day programme. Learners will also pick up the quick tips on the how-tos in the collecting and managing of data!
learning_outcomes:
At the end of the programme, the learner would be able to:
Recognise the importance of data management and its application.
Design an approach to ensure proper management, access and use of data in a responsible and secure manner.
Maintain quality data, and ensure it is fit for consumption and meets the needs of the agency/data consumers
Develop the basic know-how, correct mindset, and confidence in collecting and managing shared data in a responsible, secured and cost-effective manner.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who may be an individual contributor or a data business owner. You are keen to learn how to manage data effectively, maintain quality data and share data to meet organisational goals.

title: Introduction to Dialogic OD
description:
(Do note that ELDOD01 was the previous code for this programme)

What is Dialogic OD and how is it relevant? Dialogic OD helps enable change in systems and organisations through conversations, narratives, and descriptions etc. The approach is marked by surfacing and working with emergent ideas that people bring up. As organisations and people are dynamic and evolving, Dialogic OD practice can enhance the way we contract, design, facilitate and support our stakeholders in our complex and adaptive environment.
learning_outcomes:
Explain the definition of Dialogic OD
Differentiate between Diagnostic OD and Dialogic OD
Describe the key principles to observe when using the Dialogic OD mindset in practice
Identify situations where you can apply more Dialogic OD
Acquire knowledge of Dialogic OD in your intervention designs
who_should_attend: You are an OD practitioner in the Singapore Public Service interested to design and facilitate work in complex and adaptive organisations through dialogues.

title: Innovation Sandbox
description:
(Do note that CRINNSB was the previous code for this programme)

In this course you will learn the concept, process and tools of ‘Innovation Sandboxing’. ‘Innovation Sandbox’ is a ‘safe space’ to ‘test and validate’ your ‘innovative solutions’ in a ‘real-world’ environment with certain ‘boundary conditions’ as safeguards so that the failure does not result in damage to the organisation. Essentially sandboxing starts when you have generated an ideal solution and are ready to start to implement it. Whereas innovation process/tools like design thinking or something similar helps you to generate a user centred and innovative solution, sandboxing gives you a process and toolset to bring these solutions to life. Sandboxes can be mainly of two types:

1. Internal Sandbox

Organisational Sandbox - to test and validate organisational improvement solutions, such as those associated with change management, internal training or new organisational initiatives.

2. External Sandbox

Corporate Sandbo
learning_outcomes:
At the end of the programme, you will be able to:

Reinforce learning about fundamentals of innovation
Reflect and realise organisational and personal blind spots that kill innovation
Learn and understand the concept, process and tools of ‘innovation sandboxing’ explained through case studies from the public sector.
Learn to apply the process and tools of innovation sandboxing in the context of your own ‘solution idea/concept’ (that you have for a particular problem that you are trying to address in your organisation).
who_should_attend: Public officers from any domain who are looking to solve workplace challenges or public issues through innovative approaches. Whether you already have a solution idea to test, are facing problems that need creative solutions, or want to learn systematic innovation methods, this two-day Innovation Sandboxing course will equip you with practical tools to develop and validate solutions before full-scale implementation. Your focus can be on internal organisational improvements or external public-facing initiatives. Teams of 2-5 people from the same organization are strongly encouraged to attend together to maximize collaborative learning and ensure effective implementation of course outcomes in your workplace.

title: Human Resource Foundation Programme
description:
(Do note that MSHFP10 was the previous code for this programme)

The HR Foundation Programme (HRFP) is the first of a series of HR milestone programmes. It is a comprehensive five day programme that serves as an introduction to HR in the Civil Service. This programme will help you establish a common understanding of HR and gain a basic understanding of your operating environment.

Broadly, the programme will cover topics such as HR principles and philosophy, Public Service values, HR structures and accountabilities in the Public Service, and engaging employees and line managers through strategic communication. The programme will also draw from three areas that are fundamental to HR work in the Public Service – Recruitment and Selection, Performance Management, and Learning and Development. These will help you appreciate effective delivery of HR services.
learning_outcomes:
Appreciate your unique operating environment and the distinct HR philosophy and principles in the Public Service
Acquire key basic HR skills and knowledge to practise outcome-oriented and people-centric HR management
Engender a greater commitment to developing yourself for greater HR effectiveness and value-add to the Service 
Build peer networks within the Public Service HR Community
who_should_attend: You are a Human Resource (HR) officer who has one year or less experience in the Public Service's HR function. However, you may have had prior experience in HR from the private sector. Note: HR Leaders may assess the suitability and nominate an experienced or senior HR officer to attend this programme as well. This programme is open to Singapore Public Officers only.

title: Growth Suite for Support Officers
description:
(Do note that CRGRS34 was the previous code for this programme)

We are in an era where technological breakthroughs happen on almost daily basis. Change is a constant, and we need to continually rethink and reinvent what we do. To prepare our Support Officers for the future, CSC has developed the flagship Growth Suite built with learning experiences that will guide officers to develop the necessary mindset, knowledge and skills.
learning_outcomes:
Module 1: Creating Your Future - The Choice is Yours 

• Understand that the environment is constantly changing and at an increasing speed

• Understand the need to be resilient, agile and motivated to stay relevant

• Describe ways to stay relevant in the changing job landscape




Module 2: Think Big, Start Small

• Understand the importance of user-centricity and having an iterative mindset

• Apply user-centricity and rapid iteration in your daily work

• Demonstrate the spirit of ideation

• Apply techniques to enhance creative thinking at work and in daily life




Module 3: Thriving in a Digital Workplace

• Build an appreciation of the benefits of DWP tools to improve workplace productivity, especially for working remotely

• Practice and apply the DWP tools to improve workplace productivity




Module 4: Applying My Strengths To Work 

• Describe and understand your unique qualities

• Apply your qualities at work to enhance productivity, enable effective communications and sp
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who wishes to develop the mindsets, knowledge and skills necessary for the future.

title: Futurecraft 102
description:
(Do note that CRFC102 was the previous code for this programme)

How does future thinking challenge and disrupt existing assumptions to better prepare your organisation? Futurecraft 102 brings focus to uncovering deeper assumptions that might exist in how you think about certain issues. The programme will also provide you the tools and frameworks to address these assumptions and biases so you can expand your range of policy options and be better prepared for the future.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the various challenges to challenging assumptions
Use frameworks like the Cynefin framework, tools like Causal Layered Analysis and simple scenarios to challenge deep assumptions and inspire change
who_should_attend:
You are a Singapore Public Officer working in futures or foresight departments, conducting foresight research and/or involved in strategic planning for your agency. It is recommended (though not compulsory) that you attend Futurecraft 101 before attending Futurecraft 102.

About

title: Future Ready: Building Effective Team Collaboration through Emergenetics
description:
(Do note that CRETC10 was the previous code for this programme)

Work in the Public Service has become increasingly cross-cutting and multi-disciplinary, and Public Officers need to be able to work hand-in-hand with officers from different teams and even from different agencies.

Join this programme where you will receive your own Emergenetics® Profiling to recognise your strengths and perceived weaknesses.

Discover how to leverage an individual's thinking and behavioural preferences and make use of the Whole Emergenetics (WE) Framework to enhance your effectiveness in communication and collaboration with them!
learning_outcomes:
Increase self-awareness of thinking and behavioural preferences through the Emergenetics® Tool.
Build an appreciation of the different thinking and behavioural styles of others (e.g. Your work teams etc.)
Be able to navigate tactfully to communicate with colleagues and stakeholders of the same/different working styles
Be able to develop interpersonal, team and leadership strategies to collaborate effectively within your teams
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is keen to form effective partnerships to collaborate within and across agencies.

title: Futurecraft 101
description:
(Do note that CRFC101 was the previous code for this programme)

How important is foresight and how do you apply it to strategy and policy work? Discover futures work in this programme and learn the importance of scanning for possible futures, sense-making and sharing emerging insights.
learning_outcomes:
Understand why foresight matters to strategy/policy work
Distinguish between data points and signals when scouting
Use tools like the Futures Wheel to generate new insights and perspectives
Understand the various ways of communicating insights
who_should_attend:
You are a Singapore Public Officer working in futures or foresight departments, conducting foresight research and/or involved in strategic planning for your agency.

Public Service Learning Fest 2026 brings learning to life with keynotes, workshops, webinars, learning journeys, and curated LEARN resources. Dive in to level up your AI and innovation skills this July.​ Explore More

1/1
About

title: Foundations of Effective Presentation
description:
(Do note that CRFEP10 was the previous code for this programme)

Be equipped with the essential skills to deliver effective presentations by focusing on mastering the basics of clear and coherent communication, understanding the impact of body language and vocal delivery, and utilising visuals to support key points. You will also learn about audience analysis, building your presentation confidence, basic Q&A handling, and navigating common presentation challenges.
learning_outcomes:
Communicate information clearly and coherently. 
Understand and apply the impact of body language, facial expressions, gestures, and vocal delivery to support verbal messages.
Effective use of visuals to support key points and proposals.
Proficiency in audience analysis, including identifying diverse needs and interests, and customising presentations to meet stakeholder expectations.
Develop presentation confidence and poise in front of an audience.
Acquire basic Q&A handling skills and navigate presentation challenges with professionalism.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer in your first three years of public service, and your work involves preparing and delivering presentations.

title: Four Categories of Value by Ron Kaufman
description:
(Do note that ELFCV13 was the previous code for this programme)

In this course, you will explore the different ways in which your customers get value in your service. You will find new ways to improve your service and create more service value for your customers and colleagues.
learning_outcomes:
Understand how customers obtain service value
Identify categorise which you can increase value
Create plans to create more value in each category
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is keen on Service Excellence.

title: Fundamentals Of Investigation
description:
(Do note that CRBIC13 was the previous code for this programme)

You are a newly appointed Enforcement Officer who conducts investigations within your jurisdiction, or a public officer who is involved in investigative work.

In this course, you will learn basic investigation knowledge and skills to be more effective in your investigative work.
learning_outcomes:
Identify and exemplify the relevant laws governing investigations and the principles of investigation
Display principles, skills and techniques learnt when conducting investigations
Conduct investigations and write investigation papers for simple cases
who_should_attend: You are a newly appointed Enforcement Officer who conducts investigations within your jurisdiction, or a public officer who is involved in investigative work. This programme is open to Singapore Public Officers only.

title: Foundation Policy Programme: Systems Thinking for Middle Managers
description:
(Do note that CRSTM10 was the previous code for this programme)

Do you have a complex policy issue that you are working through? Are you frustrated with ‘the system’ and want to do things differently?  

As a Policy and Planning Officer, you play a crucial role in addressing complex and interconnected policy issues that impact our society. This 1.5 day in-person programme is designed to provide you with the tools to identify and intervene in complex systems, tap into their potential, and develop effective policies that address root causes of problems. You will also learn the mindsets and behaviours of successful systems leaders, enabling you to navigate the system and drive change.
learning_outcomes:
Describe what is meant by a System, and its properties and characteristics
Understand typical policy systems problems and apply tools to analyse them
Identify and apply levers for intervening and/or shifting systems
Reflect on mindsets and behaviours of a successful Systems Leader
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer in a Policy and Planning role (MX11, MX10) who wants to understand and apply Systems Thinking to policymaking.

title: Finance Management Programme
description:
Within this ever-changing public sector finance landscape filled with challenges, Finance Middle Managers are a key contributor to Finance Transformation. As a Finance Middle Manager, you play a critical role in championing and implementing changes to help your agency achieve financial and organisational outcomes efficiently and effectively. 

Launched by the Ministry of Finance (MOF), the Finance Management Programme is part of a suite of milestone programmes specifically designed to build your capabilities and prepare you for challenges in a dynamic work environment. 

This programme is built on the LEAP 2027 vision with the following objectives: 

Being a catalyst for change and being an effective business partner (LEADING) 
Having a strong one public service mindset (ENGAGED) 
Becoming leaders and officers with leading professional competencies (AGILE) 
Enabling the finance function to be a digital leader (PRODUCTIVE) 

In addition, this milestone programme will help you to forge d
learning_outcomes:
Explain the government structure and public finance functions 
Discuss the significant driving forces impacting Singapore’s financial position and fiscal policies 
Explain the key principles and policies in the management of resources 
Describe the duties, responsibilities, and roles of finance officer as a manager, steward and business partner
Take charge of your personal and professional growth through mentorship opportunities 
Apply human-centred design principles to develop stakeholder-centric finance processes and solution
who_should_attend: You are a middle manager, i.e. Manager / Head / Assistant Director equivalent positions within the Finance function of your agency with fewer than 5 years of managerial experience in Public Service. This programme is open only to Singapore Public Officers from Ministries and Statutory Boards.

title: Foundation Course
description:
(Do note that MSFDC10 was the previous code for this programme)

The Foundation Course (FC) is a 6-week, full-time milestone programme designed for new entrants to the Public Service Leadership Programme (PSLP) General Phase.
who_should_attend: New entrants to the Public Service Leadership Programme (PSLP) General Phase in their first two years of service. We require participants to commit to attending the programme in full (100% attendance) to fully benefit from the programme.

title: Executive Leadership Programme
description:
(Do note that MSELP10 was the previous code for this programme)

The Executive Leadership Programme (ELP) is a 5-week full-time leadership milestone programme for senior leaders in the Public Service, including Director-Generals, Senior Directors and Divisional Directors in Ministries, as well as Deputy Chief Executives and Assistant Chief Executives of Statutory Boards.

The programme comprises various governance, policy, and leadership-related sessions conducted locally and overseas study trips.
who_should_attend: Senior Leaders in the Public Service, including Director-Generals, Senior Directors and Divisional Directors in Ministries, as well as Deputy Chief Executives and Assistant Chief Executives of Statutory Boards.

title: Empowering Teams through Psychological Safety
description:
(Do note that CRETPS was the previous code for this programme)

When leaders in the public service face increasing demands to achieve more with fewer resources, have you considered how cultivating a culture of psychological safety can elevate your leadership effectiveness? Imagine leading a team where every member feels safe to voice concerns, share insights, and propose ideas without fear of negative consequences. This environment ensures you hear all perspectives and tap into collective wisdom, especially amidst uncertainty and complex challenges. 

Yet, embedding this culture can be challenging, as leaders come up against established cultural norms, behaviours, assumptions and values that may discourage open dialogue. 

Join us for a dynamic workshop focused on cultivating this critical culture within your teams. Through immersive role-plays, interactive case scenarios, and collaborative discussions, you will explore the psychological and systemic foundations of safety, reflect on y
learning_outcomes:
Reflect on your leadership style and understand how your actions and behaviours can matter to the psychological safety of your team.
Create actionable strategies to foster an inclusive environment that encourages open dialogue and collaboration, boosting team effectiveness and performance.
who_should_attend: You are an Assistant Director, Deputy Director, or Director seeking to enhance your impact by fostering a culture of psychological safety that drives team performance and innovation.

title: Designing Employee Experience for HR Practitioners
description:
(Do note that CREEXDT was the previous code for this programme)

Having a strong employer brand alone is not enough when it comes to attracting and retaining talent. The right employee experience strategy also plays a part. How can you as an HR practitioner design employee experiences to better attract talents, retain top performers, and develop them to be high performing employees?

Through this 2-day programme, you will be given tools and strategies to design the employee experience journey in your agency amidst public sector transformation.
learning_outcomes:
Describe employee experience and how it relates to the employee life-cycle
Use the Design Thinking approach in designing employee experience
Identify HR functions and processes that influence employee experience
Ideate and develop solutions to improve employee experience
Develop a stakeholder engagement plan
who_should_attend: You are an HR practitioner interested in designing the Employee Experience (EX) for your agency.

title: Building your Learning Agility for Career Fitness
description:
(Do note that CRDLA01 was the previous code for this programme)

Learning Agility has been identified as one of the most fundamental skills one needs in order to learn other skills by the World Economic Forum, McKinsey, and SkillsFuture. This is because:  

We are solving more complex and new problems with no precedence or Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).
Innovative solutions often come at the intersection of different areas of expertise.
Unforeseen changes in the operating environment may cause your job role to change completely, requiring you to pick up and adapt quickly.  

Research shows that people with high learning agility: 

Have wider areas of skills, expertise and interests.
Are more insightful and multi-disciplinary in perspectives.
Thrive in novel situations with no prior SOPs or answers, because they are good at finding answers to new questions.
Are happier, more engaged, and successful, and they also have more career options.

In this programme, learn how you can be
learning_outcomes:
Learn research-based learning strategies to accelerate your learning process
Understand your personalised Learning Agility Profile™; your motivation, attitudes, process and workplace conditions
Identify the future skills that leverage your experience and strengths for career success
Motivate yourself and create routines for learning consistency
Transfer learning to application and impact at work
who_should_attend: You are a Senior Officer looking to improve your learning capabilities and strategies to accelerate mastery in diverse skills to gain career and personal success.

title: Directors' Developmental Experience
description:
(Do note that MSDDE was the previous code for this programme)

The Directors’ Developmental Experience (DDE) aims to help prepare first-time first-year Directors for the leadership responsibilities and expectations of being leaders in the Public Service. Aligned with the Director Leadership Competency Framework, the DDE is designed to equip you with the key capabilities to successfully navigate your transition into this expanded leadership role.
learning_outcomes:
Through the DDE, you will:

 

Gain an appreciation of your role in context and what this means for your leadership
Acquire practical insights and skills to support your transition into directorship and to enhance your effectiveness in leading yourself, teams and organisation
Establish a strong peer network for professional and emotional support
Expand your capacity to lead as part of the Public Service Leadership Corps
who_should_attend:
You are a Singapore Public Officer who has taken on a Director role in the Public Service for the first time within the last one year. You lead a department/division and are part of your agency’s senior management team. 
 

Depending on your agency, you may be of Grade MX10 to 9 or equivalent.

title: Design Leadership
description:
(Do note that CRDLEAD was the previous code for this programme)

Leaders are not born. They are made. Great leaders lead not by chance; they design. 

The public sector is surrounded by a complex, dynamic and ambiguous future, and a new breed of public sector leaders can help to lead the public service with empathy, creativity, collaboration and agility.

Learning from the qualities of great design leaders, the Design Leadership Programme prepares leaders in the public service with the strategies, leadership behaviours and mindsets to lead their teams as they shape and design innovative solutions, policies and services to tackle the wicked problems of the 21st century. 

Modelled after design leadership principles, leaders will learn the four pillars of good public sector design leadership 1) networked leadership; 2) empathetic leadership; 3) creative leadership and 4) adaptive leadership that will prepare them to turn challenges into opportunities, nimbleness and creative confidence t
learning_outcomes:
Learning outcomes:

Explore and investigate public sector leaders' inhibitions in creatively leading change and solving wicked problems.
Learn how to explore perspectives and shape opportunities for change in a complex situation and network of players to achieve shared outcomes and success
Gain how to listen first, tap on your senses, acquire alliances and find the shared purpose to co-create solutions, identify insights and deeper issues with your team and stakeholders
Learn the leadership behaviours to foster creative and innovative and practical creative rituals that will empower your team and others with the creative courage and confidence to develop innovative solutions
Explore adaptive leadership principles and mindsets that will boost your readiness to act, lead experiments, and manage change when faced with ambiguity and complexity with agility, openness and optimism.
who_should_attend: Public Service leaders (Deputy Directors and above) who are keen to lead creatively and support their organisations on their transformation journey.

title: Delivering a Great Customer Experience by World Class Benchmarking
description:
(Do note that ELDCE13 was the previous code for this programme)

Discover the “behind-the-scenes” secrets of legendary organisations who successfully deliver compelling experiences for both their customers and employees. With these practical tools, you can use existing resources to go beyond just products and services to create an engaging experience that sets you apart as different and better. Be inspired by the best practices of world class public and private sector organisations' experience in delivering a great customer experience by using the '6P' Framework of 'Promise', 'People', 'Place', Processes', Products' and 'Price'. 

Whoever your team serves, the interactive exercises in this course will provide solutions to transform your employee and customer experience and lead to successfully accomplishing your organisational goals and mission.

 

[COMPLIMENTARY MODULE] EXCELLENCE AND YOU

This module provides an important overview of how leadership excellence, employee engagement, a
learning_outcomes:
Apply the '6P' Service Netting Framework to go beyond just products and services to create an engaging experience that sets you apart as different and better
Understand the best practices in implementing '6Ps' of:
Promise - What are you offering in terms of your vision and values? Is what you are delivering living up to what you promise?
People - How are your human resources delivering excellence on the front line and to those who deliver on the front line?
Place - Physically and virtually, how can you provide the right setting for a superior experience.
Processes - What are the policies, procedures and rules that make it easier for customers and employees to do business with you.
Products - What can you do to deliver great products and services to those you are creating a superior experience to?
Price - What is the tangible and intangible cost of doing business with you. How do you make sure that you deliver a great experience through your People, Place, Processes and Products to ensu
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who needs to deliver excellence for both external & internal customers (workforce).

title: Delivering Municipal Services 101
description:
(Do note that ELELDMS was the previous code for this programme)

“How might we work together as OneService to better serve our citizens?”

As a frontline officer in the municipal services sector, you play an important role in managing citizens' feedback in your daily work. Do you want to understand how you can make better use of the Municipal Reference Guide (MRG) and the other resources available to help address citizens’ needs better? 

At the end of the learning, you will be better equipped to employ the useful protocols and tools to resolve citizens’ feedback. You will also understand the importance of working collaboratively for issues that straddle multiple agencies.
learning_outcomes:
Recognise the roles and responsibilities of agencies in the municipal sector
Identify the tools that would be useful to resolve inter-agency issues
who_should_attend: You are a frontline officer from one of MSO’s OneService partner agencies.

title: Delivering What Customers Value by World Class Benchmarking
description:
(Do note that ELDCV13 was the previous code for this programme)

World class organisations achieve success based on a foundation of culture-driven guidelines that result in superior operational consistency, workforce unification, and continuous improvements. 

This proven framework introduces concepts that allow the entire organisation to share a common direction and standards of behaviour that translate to consistently high levels of efficiencies as well as increased employee/customer engagement.

Through this programme, you will discover examples and case studies with practical tools used by leading public and private sector organisations that look at developing a compelling:

Vision - This is where you want to be as an organisation. As a tool, your vision should be a compelling articulation that inspires your workforce and points everyone in the same direction.
Values - this is a declaration of what you believe matters most to your customers. They underscore the foundation of your o
learning_outcomes:
Apply concepts to build a common direction and standards of behaviour that translate to consistently high levels of efficiencies as well as increased employee/customer engagement
Gain insights into practical tools used by leading public sector and private sector organisations that look at developing a compelling Service Vision, Values and Behaviours
who_should_attend:
You are a Service manager who wants to work with your teams to build a solid operational strategy for delivering a consistently superior customer experience.

$147.15/ participant
Including 9% GST of $12.15
