About

title: Data-driven decision making and culture – An overview for Public Service Leaders
description:
(Do note that CRDMA10 was the previous code for this programme)

Analytics has been growing in popularity over the past decade,and many organisations saw it as THE competitive advantage for the future. So much so that organisations have invested heavily into building such capabilities, including investing intechnology, training staff in analytics, and hiring analytics talent.

However, many continue to struggle to realise sustainable value from analytics. A 2019 survey of 65 Fortune 1000 companies revealed that while leaders recognised the value of analytics for their organisations, they faced challenges in analytics adoption and realising many of the promised outcomes due to:

Lack of organisation alignment
Cultural resistance - not committed to making decisions or changes based on data
Understanding of how to strategically deploy analytics
Lack of leadership support - unclear of the role they should/could play in analytics

 

This 1-day programme aims to help you better appreciate w
learning_outcomes:
Understand what analytics is and how it creates value in organisations
Appreciate the analytics process and how it is deployed to support informed decision making, and the role that leaders and organisation culture play in the value chain
Appreciate when and how to deploy analytics under different situations, and when not to
Understand the key ingredients to building sustainable analytics capabilities and culture
Assess the state of analytics maturity and identify key steps that could be deployed to advance the analytics agenda in your respective organisations
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer in a senior management (Director and above) role. You are keen to find out how data analytics can support organisational decision-making.

title: Dealing with Public Sector Internal Controls
description:
(Do note that CRICF10 was the previous code for this programme)

How can we work better with our Compliance team, Internal Audit and external auditors to implement internal controls?




Learn about various internal controls and enhance your ability to help your organisation meet regulatory obligations and achieve its objectives. With a broader understanding of internal controls, you will be able to better manage exceptions and respond to dynamic and challenging circumstances.
learning_outcomes:
Describe internal control concepts and the importance of internal controls
Explain how good controls are necessary for a public agency to carry out its mission
Explain the integral part you play in ensuring effective controls over financial operations, compliance to law and regulations
Describe how to implement internal controls within existing processes
Identify control risk areas and manage exceptions
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer interested in how basic internal controls could improve your work and organisation's processes. You will find this programme especially useful if you are new to Finance, Procurement and Internal Audit.

title: Data Analysis with Python
description:
(Do note that CRDPY10 was the previous code for this programme)

As a data practitioner, picking up ways to complete your data analysis quicker helps you to work smarter and more efficiently. Cue in Python, the most popular programming language for data science according to the June 2023 TIOBE index. This versatile, general-purpose language is widely used for data cleaning, analysis & visualisation, with many existing libraries that you can tap on instead of coding from scratch.




Through hands-on activities and guided resources in this programme*, discover how to simplify your data science workflow with Python programming and pick up its different methods to clean, prepare, analyse and visualise data.




*This programme is suitable for officers in data analytics or data science fields who need to perform statistical analysis on large datasets.
learning_outcomes:
Apply data manipulation and cleansing using Python, assessing the impact of data cleaning on analysis
Evaluate common datasets, detect & identify imperfect data
Guide & facilitate in manipulating and cleansing data for informed business decisions
Utilise Python to create data visualisations, convey insights & provide actionable recommendations
Evaluate the purpose & limitations of basic descriptive measurements (mean, median, mode, standard deviation) for decision making
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is keen to pick up Python programming language to perform data analysis. If you are curious about Python programming or would like a refresher of your dormant programming knowledge, you may be interested in attending our introductory Python programming course 'Introduction to Python Programming' (CRIPP10).

title: Crucial Conversations® Focused For Officers
description:
(Do note that CRCCO10 was the previous code for this programme)

Crucial Conversations® Focused is a customised version of the Crucial Conversations® programme designed specifically for officers without a supervisory role in the Public Service.

If you are an individual contributor who wants to speak more candidly to your peers and Reporting Officers to achieve the results that you want without hurting relationships, this programme is for you.

The programme will cover a subset of the Crucial Conversations model. You will learn the necessary crucial skills to stay in respectful dialogue even when you are angry, sad, or hurt, in order to preserve the relationship with those you are speaking to.
learning_outcomes:
Speak up more candidly to your supervisors and peers when faced with challenges at the workplace – e.g., being unable to handle the workload, perceived unfair treatment, dissatisfaction with peers’ work and behavior, etc. 
Approach difficult and sensitive topics in a more respectful and productive manner while maintaining amicable working relationships with peers, supervisors and department heads
Contribute to the creation of an open, candid and more innovative culture within your teams and the entire organisation
who_should_attend: You are an individual contributor in the Singapore Public Service with no supervisory role.

title: Cost Benefit Analysis Workshop for Beginners
description:
(Do note that CRCBAFC was the previous code for this programme)

This is a foundation programme for officers who want an overview of key introductory economic concepts and their application to Cost-Benefit Analysis, a structured and quantitative method, to determine whether a proposed policy has net positive or negative impacts on society.
learning_outcomes:
Examine the key introductory economic concepts in CBA and how they can be applied in policy work.
Identify different stakeholder groups that could be affected by a proposed policy.
Describe different methods for measuring and quantifying monetary and non-monetary costs and benefits, including those that are intangible.
Examine the concepts of sensitivity and risk analysis, and how they can improve the robustness of CBA.
Recognise the limitations and the appropriate role of CBA in the policy process.
Illustrate the CBA process through case studies and Excel-based exercises.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer involved in policymaking, policy analysis, budgeting, or project management and approval. This programme is especially relevant for officers with at least two years of work experience, prior knowledge of A-level economics and Excel calculations and operations. (Knowledge of macros, complex functions and add-ins is not required.)

title: Creating Impact Using Photos and Videos
description:
(Do note that CRCPV10 was the previous code for this programme)

This 3-day workshop delves into the techniques of creating compelling online content for digital communications. You will gain a deeper understanding of the production process and acquire essential skills in mobile photography and videography to produce engaging visual stories. The class will also focus on event coverage for social media sharing.
learning_outcomes:
• Apply visual storytelling techniques to communicate ideas and messages to target audience

• Apply principles of photography and videography to create content for social media posting and event coverage

• Develop a simple storyboard for video production

• Produce simple scripts for different online content

• Demonstrate mobile editing software skills for photos and videos

• Create a pitch deck, including plans for pre-production, shooting, and post-production
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer involved in your organisation's communication work and are responsible for creating and curating your organisation’s digital content.

title: Crisis Communications
description:
(Do note that CRCCC10 was the previous code for this programme)

Managing communications during a crisis is vital to preventing chaos and ensuring public safety and security. Timely and accurate communications is also important to maintain the people's trust in the government and ensuring the stability of the country. As the impact of a crisis is usually far reaching and may impact health, social, economic and security sectors, it is important for a coordinated whole-of-government approach to crisis communications.
learning_outcomes:
1.1: Develop a crisis communications plan  – Six step plan

1.2: Propose communication platforms, activities and timelines for different crisis scenarios

1.3: Prepare drawer responses to ensure agency’s readiness in managing communications in a crisis



2.1: Coordinate with relevant agencies during the implementation of crisis communications plans

2.2: Provide situational picture of the crisis and implications for communications during a crisis

2.3: Manage media and online media



3.1: Identify events and activities post crisis e.g COI report or outcome of investigation, Parliamentary questions

3.2: Prepare responses to regain public trust and confidence
who_should_attend: Singapore Public Officers with 1 to 3 years of experience in government communications departments and who are involved in developing and executing crisis communications plan.

title: Compliance and Enforcement Middle Managers Programme
description:
(Do note that CRCEMMP was the previous code for this programme)

This 3-day executive programme aims to equip compliance and enforcement middle managers with critical compliance and enforcement knowledge and skills to lead and drive regulatory changes. It delves into the regulatory landscape, providing a systems overview by exploring the spectrum of regulatory functions. You will gain strategic and practical insights into differentiated compliance and enforcement strategies, fostering a nuanced approach to regulatory challenges. Stakeholder engagement takes centre stage as we examine effective communication strategies, ensuring you are equipped to act in the public interest and navigate diverse relationships crucial to successful regulatory outcomes. You will also be empowered with the know-how to drive effective strategic changes.

Through interactive group exercises, real-world case studies, and role-playing scenarios, you will hone your leadership skills, making informed decisions i
learning_outcomes:
At the end of the programme, you will develop an understanding of the following:


Effective Regulatory Competence:
Acquire a keen understanding of the regulatory ecosystem, applying insights to improve interconnectivity and stakeholder dynamics.


Implementing Compliance Strategies & Enabling Robust Enforcements:
Apply the 6 levers from the Strategic Compliance Toolkit, enabling effective decision-making and adaptation to diverse regulatory scenarios.


Effective Stakeholder Engagement:
Demonstrate communication skills to engage stakeholders, ensuring collaborative relationships and alignment with organisational objectives.


Change Management Expertise:
Implement and develop strategic transformation within regulatory domains, anticipating challenges and enabling successful implementation.


Effective Leadership Models:
Develop leadership capabilities specific to middle management roles, guide teams and organisations through dynamic and evolving landscapes.
who_should_attend: This programme is for compliance and enforcement middle managers (e.g. Supervisors, Managers, and Assistant Directors) with at least 3 years of supervisory experience.

title: Coaching Skills for Authentic Conversation (Fundamental)
description:
(Do note that CRCTC10 was the previous code for this programme)

Interested to apply coaching at your workplace? This programme is designed for managers and directors responsible for engaging and developing individuals and teams within the organisation. No prior experience in coaching is required.




Learn and practise fundamental coaching skills in this in-person programme, where you will appreciate the power of coaching conversations and adopt a coaching mindset in developing individuals and teams. Besides becoming more familiar with the GROW model in coaching, you will also gain confidence in applying your coaching skills across different contexts. 




Your learning doesn't stop when the programme ends. You will be supported via a small group coaching supervision session post-programme, where you can discuss challenges relating to the application of coaching skills at work.
learning_outcomes:
Adopt a coaching mindset and develop your fundamental coaching skills
Apply a range of coaching skills and tools to coach team members
Develop confidence and capability to apply your coaching skills in various contexts
Deepen your awareness of your personal styles and preferences as a coaching manager, in order to integrate coaching as part of your authentic leadership style
who_should_attend: You are responsible for engaging and developing individuals and teams within your organisation. No prior experience in coaching is required.

title: Communicating Towards Effective Relationships Through Transactional Analysis
description:
(Do note that CRTA101 was the previous code for this programme)

A theory of communication applicable to a range of personal, professional and social contexts, Transactional Analysis (TA) provides a systematic approach to understanding the links between human needs and behaviours.

It will also make you effective in solving problems your organisation faces and in serving your customers. In this programme, you will undergo psychological profiling to understand your own communication style, thus increasing your effectiveness in communicating with the people around you.

Certification: You will receive a TA101 Certificate -equivalent to a US introductory university course - issued by the Training and Certification Council of Transactional Analysts, Inc. [ITAA (US), WPATA (Aust), ITA (UK), EATA (Europe)]. This certificate will be useful if you wish to obtain further academic qualifications in this field. 90% attendance is necessary for you to be given the certificate.
learning_outcomes:
Interact more effectively with your customers, peers, subordinates, superiors and significant other
Create win-win relationships
Seek solutions to service and people problems
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who wants to improve your interpersonal communication skills.

title: Building the Agile Organisation
description:
(Do note that ELELBAO was the previous code for this programme)

The Singapore Public Service is at a key turning point in designing organisations that are fit for the future of work, workplace, and workforce. Today, there are many known and unknown factors that influence the operational effectiveness and long-term direction of the Singapore Public Service. Our opportunity, in this context, is not just to be good at change, but to thrive.

Agility can be viewed as a psychological capacity that individuals, teams, and whole organisations, can demonstrate to manage complexity, and bring about transformation. The programme does not provide a single-size-fits-all method but rather a framework within a guided learning experience, which offers practical tools and concepts you can apply to your own situation.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the key challenges senior leaders face leading in times of volatility, uncertainty, complexity and ambiguity​
Learn the core elements of an agile organisation and how it can be applied to your organisation​
Examine your organisation’s strategy and the role agility plays ​
Improve your ability to work with systemic change processes​
Acquire skills to develop individuals, teams and organisations in a way that works for an era of disruption
who_should_attend: You are an OD Practitioner, change agent in your organisation or a senior leader who is responsible for setting and implementing the strategic direction for your teams and organisation.

title: Building a Successful Data Strategy for your Organisation
description:
(Do note that CRDSG10 was the previous code for this programme)

Over the past decade, analytics has become increasingly popular and many organisations see it as a competitive advantage for the future. That is why many have invested heavily in building analytics capabilities. However, as the name suggests, without data, there is no analytics. That is why investing in databases, data lakes, data management tools, and related technology is often the first step organisations take towards data analytics. Unfortunately, many organisations struggle to realise sustainable value from their investments and analytics due to a few common challenges:
Poor understanding of the analytics value chain and the role of data
Misunderstanding the purpose and scope of a data strategy
More data and a bigger budget don't always mean better return on analytics
Choosing between data security and data analytics is unnecessary - you can have both
Deploying a data strategy that doesn't align with the organisation
learning_outcomes:
Appreciate the role that data plays in Organisation Strategy
Synthesise the key elements of the organisation’s strategic framework to build an effective data strategy
Recognise the key components of a data strategy and their interrelationships
Identify strategic master data required to support the organisation's strategy, and develop strategies to acquire, process, and distribute the data effectively
Establish key components to build effective Data Governance in organisations
Appreciate how data analytics process is deployed to support Organisation Strategy
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer in a management (Assistant Director and above) role. You are keen to create, develop and execute a data strategy using a more structured approach for your data analytics journey.

title: Building Positive Relationships Through Effective Conversational Skills
description:
(Do note that CRECS12 was the previous code for this programme)

Having a great conversation is something many people enjoy. The ability to converse and connect well helps you develop positive relationships with people. While you may not naturally be a great conversationalist, you can become one by acquiring useful conversational skills and techniques. This highly interactive course will teach you techniques to converse effectively with people and build positive relationships with them. You will also learn how to manage difficult people using suitable conversational techniques.
learning_outcomes:
Identify your personal communication blocks and apply the conversational techniques to improve your conversational skills
Display confidence in making small talk with strangers and connecting with them in the first five minutes
List the difficulties that you will encounter in any communication situation and use the dialogue model to effectively manage them
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who wants to improve your interpersonal communication skills.

title: Building Team Resiliency For Enforcement
description:
As an enforcement supervisor, it is crucial to be a role model for resilience. This is the first step towards guiding and empowering your team members to be calm and alert under pressure. It also helps in building a resilient team culture.

In this programme, you will be equipped with the skills to support your team in managing their emotions, develop a supportive team culture and coach your team to cope with difficult situations and experiences.
learning_outcomes:
Strengthen your resilience in managing change, crises and difficult emotions
Prepare mentally for negative scenarios
Demonstrate calmness when responding to stressful situations
Role model what it means to be a resilient individual
Understand and support your team in managing stress
Create a supportive culture to help your team members manage and recover from stress
who_should_attend: You are a supervisor in the Singapore Public Service, involved in Enforcement Operations/Policy/Regulation.

title: AI for Management
description:
(Do note that CRAIM10 was the previous code for this programme)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) are transforming how we work, play and live. Many organisations are exploring ways to introduce and deploy AI in their businesses, but many face challenges implementing it. This could be due to a lack of understanding of how AI can be strategically deployed, lack of organisation alignment and culture, and leaders who are unsure of the role they could play.

This 1-day programme is designed for participants in leadership roles to gain an appreciation of what AI is, and the possibilities and limitations of AI. You will also explore how AI can affect the development and execution of strategy in organisations, and the role you could play in this journey. You will also explore ethical considerations when adopting AI.
learning_outcomes:
Develop a basic understanding of what Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) are, their possibilities and limitations
Appreciate the state of AI maturity, its potential applications in both public and private sector
Appreciate when and how to deploy AI under different situations, and when not to
Understand the AI lifecycle management
Recognise the role that leaders and organisations play in building an AI-ready culture and mindset
Appreciate how AI fits into the organisation's overall digital transformation efforts
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer in a middle management (Deputy Director and above) role. You are keen to explore how AI can affect the development and execution of strategy in organisations, and the role you could play in this journey. You will also explore ethical considerations when adopting AI.

title: Applied Change Management: Prosci® Change Management Certification Programme
description:
(Do note that CRACM01 was the previous code for this programme)

Faced with a rapidly changing environment and growing citizen expectations, organisations in the Public Service are increasingly required to respond by implementing change initiatives which can range from the introduction of new work processes or systems to organisational transformation. While some of these initiatives have found success, there are likely many others that have not. This can be attributed to the lack of attention paid to securing acceptance of change by affected stakeholders.

What can we do as OD practitioners to improve the effectiveness of the change initiatives? How should we structure the management of these initiatives for success? What systematic approaches can we adopt to manage the human side of change? What can we do to foster the acceptance of change by the affected stakeholders?

Programme Methodology: This 3-day programme is designed to be practice-oriented and includes a simulation experience
learning_outcomes:
Build the business case for effective change management in your organisation
Develop a change management strategy and plan for your project
Apply the ADKAR™ Model in helping managers and supervisors effectively manage the people side of change
Apply Prosci’s® change management methodology, tools and templates to an actual project from your organisation
who_should_attend: You are an OD practitioner or a member of a change project team involved in implementing change management efforts in your organisation.

title: A Public Officer’s Programme Evaluation Journey
description:
(Do note that CRPJE14 was the previous code for this programme)

Conducting evaluations allows us to know if our policies or programmes have achieved their goals, and whether the objectives and interventions need to be further adjusted. Through this programme, you will learn how to design and manage a programme evaluation, as well as the basic tools needed to plan, carry out and use evaluation on the ground.
learning_outcomes:
Explain the purposes of programme evaluation, types of programme evaluation, and the core standards of evaluation
Develop a Theory of Change for a policy/programme to articulate how a policy/programme leads to its desired outcomes
Discuss the use of Logic Model to articulate how a policy/programme leads to its desired outcomes  
Practise “SMART” principles when setting indicators for a policy/programme
Describe practical methods for data collection and analysis
Evaluate different study designs (e.g. pre-post / developing suitable proxies) to identify the most suitable design for an evaluation plan, given stakeholder needs and practical constraints
Formulate a realistic evaluation plan for execution
who_should_attend: Public Officers who are interested to find out how evaluations can help them improve their programmes.

title: A Practical Approach To Proactive Procurement In The Public Sector
description:
(Do note that CRPAP10 was the previous code for this programme)

Singapore faces an increasing scarcity of resources and rising costs. Hence, it is important that resources are wisely utilised with due diligence. Ensuring that procurement strategies are well executed will enable your agency to reap the maximum benefit of every dollar spent. You will learn the practical skills, techniques and best practices to help you make a good, value-for-money purchases for your agency.
learning_outcomes:
Explain the concept of value-for-money in procurement
Write precise and clear requirement specifications
Evaluate proposals using relevant techniques
Manage contracts effectively
Implement smart procurement for your agency throughout the procurement lifecycle
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer directly involved in the various stages of the procurement lifecycle.

title: [Virtual Event] Finance & Procurement Middle Managers Forum
description:
(Do note that EVFPMMV was the previous code for this programme)

As a middle manager, you play a key role in driving the successful implementation of finance and procurement policies and strategies both in your respective agencies as well as within the wider community. You are also at the forefront of finance and procurement transformation, embodying the vision for finance and procurement as trusted and effective business partners. 

We are here to support you in your journey. The inaugural Finance & Procurement Middle Managers Forum brings together middle managers of both the finance and procurement communities. Through this forum, you will have the opportunity to:

Engage with your respective Heads of Profession to better understand your role in finance and procurement transformation
Find out more about how the Finance and Procurement Academy will support you in capability development and help you grow as a professional 
Hear from two agencies who will share more on tools and resourc
who_should_attend: Middle Managers from Finance and Procurement functions. By nominations only.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Write For Your Audience: Service in Words for Support Officers
description:
(Do note that CRWFAVL was the previous code for this programme)

As an officer representing your agency and the Public Service, you may need to write emails to members of the public frequently.




In this programme, you will learn how to engage your audience while maintaining a professional tone. You will also be taught to write clearly and concisely, personalise your correspondences and display empathy in your writing.




This programme is a virtual version of 'Write For Your Audience: Service in Words for Support Officers'. There will be pre-course as well as mid-course assignments in between synchronous sessions.
learning_outcomes:
Write to your audience with clarity and concision
Personalise your correspondences
Discern what issues should and should not be addressed in your written correspondences
Display empathy in your writing
Adopt appropriate manners when writing to the public
who_should_attend: You are a new Singapore Public Officer who wants to write concisely and display empathy in your writing.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Think on Your Feet for Support Officers
description:
(Do note that CRTYFVL was the previous code for this programme)

We are often called to think on our feet. Be it at a meeting, presentation or a public forum, we need the ability to gather our thoughts fast and communicate them with clarity and impact. The good news is that this is a skill that you can learn and master with practice. The Think On Your Feet communication system is built on a simple premise: clear speaking is clear thinking. The approach emphasises structure when communicating, ensuring that you present your ideas in ways that can be understood and remembered, thus enhancing your oral communication skills.
learning_outcomes:
Analyse situations and select the most suitable communication plan
Structure ideas coherently
Handle difficult situations and conversations with bridging techniques
Apply the skills and techniques taught to analyse, organise and present ideas clearly and persuasively
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who wants to learn how to present your ideas and thoughts quickly in different situations.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Using Power Well in Leadership
description:
(Do note that CRPWMVL was the previous code for this programme)

As a leader, do you know how important power is in leadership? How can you tell if you are using power effectively? Studies have proven that employees do not leave organisations; they leave bosses, specifically bosses who do not manage their authority effectively. Power problems can also be costly as it has a potentially significant impact on employee engagement and productivity within the team. This programme offers a unique opportunity for you to examine how you can use power effectively within your team, agency, and other stakeholders.

Key features:
Diamond Power Index Profiling tool
– Receive your personal Diamond Power Index (DPI) report. This 360-assessment tool provides insights into the three dimensions of your Personal Power. It includes both a self-rating and others-rating via a specifically researched and designed survey. Please note that you will need at least 5 responses in order for a report to be generated
learning_outcomes:
Describe the six kinds of Power and how to reliably access each kind of Power
Understand the Power Paradox and how this relates to de-railers that may reduce a manager’s leadership effectiveness
Gain knowledge on the importance of Personal Power and what the effective use of power looks like in your team and organisation
Gain insights into your personal Power Print through the Diamond Power Index profiling tool
Build stakeholder competencies via the Diamond Power Index
who_should_attend: You are a middle manager in the Public Service and you could be managing teams or a group of individuals. You are keen to learn practical tools and examples to flex your leadership authority effectively in the team and organisation.

title: [Virtual Classroom] The Art of Specification Writing
description:
(Do note that CRTSRV1 was the previous code for this programme)

How do you specify requirements of what you want to procure? And how can you write it clearly and effectively? What steps can you take to ensure you do not miss out important details? 

Learn the basics of writing specifications and recognise mistakes from previous cases.
learning_outcomes:
Recognise the importance of specification writing and our responsibilities to uphold Government Procurement principles
Identify and apply the principles, rules and guidelines related to specification writing on Instruction Manuals (IM)
Discover an effective process to develop specifications
Recognise common mistakes and be skilled with key writing tips
Explore innovative procurement practices such as Outcome Based Specification and Crowdsourcing and how they impact specifications writing
Prepare a specification for a case study and apply what is learnt in the programme
Assess if your specification is effective through an interesting activity
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is new to writing specifications and tasked to generate an effective set of specifications. You are recommended to complete Procurement e-Learning 1.1 (ELPELPN), before attending this programme.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Think on Your Feet for Senior Officers
description:
(Do note that CRTOF10 was the previous code for this programme)

We are often called to think on your feet. Be it at a meeting, presentation or a public forum, you need the ability to gather your thoughts fast and communicate with clarity and impact. The good news is that this is a skill that you can learn and master with practice. The Think On Your Feet communication system is built on a simple premise: clear speaking is clear thinking. The approach emphasises structure when communicating, ensuring that you present your ideas in ways that can be understood and remembered.
learning_outcomes:
Analyse situations and select the most suitable communication plan
Structure ideas coherently
Handle difficult situations and conversations with bridging techniques
Apply the skills and techniques taught to analyse, organise and present ideas clearly and persuasively
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who wants to learn techniques on presenting your ideas clearly and with impact in different situations.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Supporting Mental Well-being for a Thriving Workplace (for Supervisors)
description:
(Do note that CRWMHVL was the previous code for this programme)

When team performance drops and sick leave increases, many supervisors suspect stress but don't know how to help. What if you could spot mental health challenges before they escalate and have the confidence to support your team effectively?

Your staff may be showing early warning signs - missed deadlines, irritability, withdrawal - but without the right approach, well-meaning conversations can backfire. Learn the practical skills to recognise mental health concerns, initiate supportive conversations that actually help, and build a team environment where seeking support becomes normal, not stigmatised.
learning_outcomes:
Recognise early warning signs of mental health challenges and initiate supportive conversations with confidence
Respond effectively when staff disclose mental health concerns, providing appropriate support and guidance
Create a workplace environment that encourages help-seeking behaviour and builds team resilience
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who manages staff and wants to learn how to support their mental well-being while practising effective self-care.

title: [Virtual Classroom] The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People ® Foundations
description:
(Do note that CRSHFVL was the previous code for this programme)

In a VUCA environment, especially during these unprecedented times of Covid-19, public officers are expected to embrace the changing workplace. At the same time, public officers are still required to serve and meet the citizens’ needs effectively. In order to function effectively, officers would need to be well-equipped with the skills and knowledge to manage both current needs and the need to be ready for future demands.

This programme introduces the key principles and practices of the 7 Habits of Highly Effective People, and helps you to learn how they could enhance the effectiveness of your work and personal life. In addition to participating in the virtual learning session (activities and videos), you will also receive a digital copy of the 7 Habits Foundations Participant Guide Book.
learning_outcomes:
Recognise your responsibility in your choices, moods, actions and outcomes.
Set goals that are aligned to your mission and execute them.
Collaborate effectively by having a lasting influence and impact.
Communicate effectively with others and work synergistically to solve problems.
Learn how to renew and invest yourself.
who_should_attend: You are a support officer who is interested to develop your personal and professional effectiveness in the changing workplace.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Selection Interview - Competency Based Skills & Techniques
description:
(Do note that CRSCBV was the previous code for this programme)

Increasingly, organisations not only look for talent who can do the job and fit into the organisation culture today, but also for potential leaders of the organisation for tomorrow. How might you, as a manager, hire the right person for the job and ensure that your time and effort are not wasted?

You will learn how to systematically evaluate a candidate for the critical competencies and qualities that are pertinent for a specific job role through the use of competency-based interviewing techniques.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the fundamentals of an end-to-end Recruitment and Selection process
Uphold an objective and professional approach of the Recruitment and Selection process 
Establish the business need for a required position and the desired critical competencies and qualities
Discover best practices in selecting the best fit candidates applying
Apply competency-based interviewing techniques to assess competencies and qualities of potential candidates
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who conducts selection interviews.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Strategic Workforce Planning for HR Practitioners
description:
(Do note that CRSWPHR was the previous code for this programme)

As the public sector transforms with the evolving operating context, how can HR drive Strategic Workforce Planning (SWP) to successfully build workforce competencies and capabilities to better serve the needs of citizens?




In this 2-day programme, deepen your understanding and application of SWP through practical frameworks and hands-on activities to implement successful workforce changes within your organisation.
learning_outcomes:
Identify the key stakeholders involved in the SWP process and understand HR’s role in the change management process and SWP execution
Discern the specific steps and thought processes involved in developing an executable Strategic Workforce Plan
Gain exposure to tools, templates, and frameworks for competency development and mapping in relation to SWP
Articulate your strategy, business model and core processes to highlight SWP implications, in preparation for your SWP work
who_should_attend: You are a Strategic Workforce Planning practitioner (who may be a representative from the Human Resources, Strategic Planning, Organisational Design, or Organisation Development Team) and about to embark on a Strategic Workforce Planning project.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Procurement for Approving Officer
description:
(Do note that CRPARVL was the previous code for this programme)

This programme aims to help you gain key knowledge on the basics of Singapore Government Procurement. It also highlights the key roles and responsibilities of an Approving Officer.

Through interactive e-learning and engaging scenario discussions, you will discover the possible dilemmas you may face as Approving Officer. You will debate what role you play to uphold transparency, open and fair competition, and value for money principles in Singapore Government Procurement.
learning_outcomes:
Identify key principles of Singapore Government Procurement and state the different stages of Procurement
Describe the roles and responsibilities of the Approving Officer and how it differs from that of the Quotation/Tender Evaluation Committee
Identify common pitfalls and mistakes
Understand the key processes of your role and deliberate how these processes help you navigate common dilemmas
Debate with other Approving Officers in a virtual classroom on existing procurement challenges and dilemmas, and reflect on possible and effective solutions.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is new in your role as an Approving Officer or Approving Authority for procurement and may be of Assistant Manager to Director level. You are recommended to complete Procurement e-Learning 1.1 (ELPELPN), before attending this programme.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Project Management with Agile Scrum
description:
(Do note that CRIAGLV was the previous code for this programme)

The recent spate of global events has brought about much uncertainty to the workplace and project management teams in the public sector have faced much challenges and are forced to quickly adapt and improvise on projects that they have been managing.

With flexibility and prioritisation becoming key features of project management, agile methodologies may be the solution to help public officers manage both the requirements of the organisation and customers.

This programme provides a sandbox for you to build a cross-functional team to manage projects and achieve outcomes with Agile Scrum methodology and principles. Although frequently associated with IT and software development, Agile principles and mindset can be applied in any context.

Sign up for this workshop now to learn how you can adopt an Agile mindset in managing risks and complexities, and in enhancing teamwork, customer interaction, productivity and flexibility
learning_outcomes:
Explain the Agile Scrum methodology and how it differs from other methodologies
Apply Scrum principles to plan and manage work and projects
Facilitate effective Scrum meetings with your team
Undertake respective responsibilities of core and/or non-core roles in your team
Identify your team's Scrum maturity and the next steps for effective implementation of Scrum
who_should_attend: You are looking to use Agile Scrum methodologies to manage projects and/or to develop a product, service or solution. This programme provides you with the opportunity to pull together a 4-7 member team with expertise and voices your project needs, to give the team hands-on experience in applying Agile to actual work across a 10-day sprint. This programme is suitable for technical and non-IT related projects and it is a requirement to sign up as a team of 4-7 public officers.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Procurement Evaluation Methodologies
description:
(Do note that CREMGVL was the previous code for this programme)

Grasp the intricacies of evaluation methodologies in public sector procurement and learn how to effectively and fairly evaluate different vendors for your Invitation to Quote (ITQ) and Invitation to Tender (ITT).




You will also learn how to distinguish between Requirement Specifications and Evaluation Criteria, how to prepare a Tender Evaluation Plan, and eventually be able to write an Evaluation Report.
learning_outcomes:
Identify components of an Invitation to Quote (ITQ) and an Invitation to Tender (ITT)
Recall IM Dos and Don’ts on Requirement Writing
Distinguish between Requirement, Specifications and Evaluation Criteria
Prepare a Tender Evaluation Plan
Conduct an Evaluation exercise, bearing in mind the 5 facets of the evaluation process
Write an evaluation report
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is new to the evaluation process in procurement and will be required to handle procurement and procurement evaluation in the course of your work. You are recommended to complete Procurement e-Learning 1.1 (ELPELPN), before attending this programme.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Media Relations & Engagement
description:
(Do note that CRMRE11 was the previous code for this programme)

Effective media engagement is a vital part of the overall communication strategy. Fostering and building a good relationship with the media helps to provide a balanced media reporting, publicise programmes, services and initiatives launched by the agency and help to build the basis of trust for communications during a crisis in a timely manner.

Join this programme to learn how to develop and implement a media communications plan such as pitching stories to the media, managing and drafting replies to media queries, writing media releases and preparing factsheets and FAQs.
learning_outcomes:
Recognise the local and international media landscape as well as online media
Identify the types of media engagement
Engage the media proactively – building relationships such as media lunches and engagement sessions with senior management, being responsive, pitching stories, correcting inaccuracies and misrepresentations
Develop and write a media communications plan
Develop media kit materials
Write a media release
who_should_attend: Singapore Public Officers with 2 to 3 years of experience in government communications or have attended the PaCE – Public Communications and Engagement Executive Programme (MSPCE10) (previously Public Communications & Engagement 101 (CRPCE11 or MSPCE12) course).

title: [Virtual Classroom] Preparing Effective Minutes of Meetings in the Public Service
description:
(Do note that CRWRMVL was the previous code for this programme)

Minutes are an important element of any formal meeting. They ensure that any information given, decisions made and actions to be taken are documented accurately and clearly.

This blended digital course will explain the purpose and process of formal meetings in the Public Service. You will learn how to perform the secretarial duties needed to staff these meetings. You will also learn how to take minutes effectively and efficiently, applying the principles and conventions of good professional meeting minutes.
learning_outcomes:
Undertake the secretariat duties associated with a minute taker
Apply active listening techniques to listen to discussions objectively
Extract and summarise relevant information from discussions and decisions made during meetings
Present minutes in an organised, accurate and clear manner
who_should_attend: You are a senior officer in the Public Service who wants to learn how to take minutes effectively and efficiently.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Managing Staff Performance using Our Core Competencies for Supervisors
description:
(Do note that CRMCSV was the previous code for this programme)

With the launch of “Our Core Competencies”, supervisors will need to manage the performance and development of their officers using the OCC competencies.

 

As the core competencies prepare public officers to deliver excellence today and build the Public Service of tomorrow, this programme also seeks to equip supervisors with a more progressive performance management approach.
learning_outcomes:
Articulate the underlying philosophy and principles of Performance Management in the Civil Service
Flex appropriate Performance Management tools by considering People, Policies and Processes
Apply Our Core Competencies to effectively manage the performance and potential of your officers
Design Performance Management interventions within the four-phase performance management cycle to effectively motivate and improve performance of officers
Develop capabilities and enlarge the capacities of your officers
who_should_attend: You are a team leader or supervisor who wants to gain a better understanding of the performance management approach in Public Service.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Managing the Human Side of Change at Work
description:
(Do note that CRMCE11 was the previous code for this programme)

Change affects people. The main goal of the program is to equip individual officers and change agents with the knowledge, skills and tools (after understanding how people move through transition differently) to motivate and support their staff, peers, teams and themselves to embrace change. By understanding the change process and the emotional impact on people, you will be able to formulate effective strategies for more successful outcomes.

In this programme, you will learn how to respond more proactively to changes at the workplace as well as to enhance your ability to effect and manage change.
learning_outcomes:
Identify the challenges commonly associated with implementing large-scale technological change in the context of your organisation.
Explain the key concepts and frameworks in managing the people side of change.
Assess the current state of your team and acquire relevant tools, skills and approaches to raise team effectiveness.
Strengthen your impact and influence as a leader for your team
Draft an action plan to address your selected challenge in the area of team effectiveness and/or change management.
Assess the impact of change on people and organisations
Determine where people are in the 3-phase transition process.
Develop ways for people to let go of the old ways of doing things, and make a clear ending, by leading people through the neutral zone.
Develop an action plan to embrace the new way of doing and being.
Learn how and when to use tools and strategies to guide themselves and others through organisational change and transition processes.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is interested in finding out how individuals respond to change in an organisation.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Leading with Emotional Intelligence for First-Time Managers
description:
(Do note that CRLEQ11 was the previous code for this programme)

The most effective leaders are all alike in one crucial way: they all have a high degree of what has come to be known as emotional intelligence. Without EQ, a person can have the best training in the world, an incisive, analytical mind, and an endless supply of smart ideas, but he still won’t make a great leader. (Daniel Goleman, “What Makes a Leader”, HBR, 1998) Research shows that a high level of emotional intelligence (EQ) is associated with better performance in areas such as self-awareness, decision-making and developing relationships with others. As a manager, you are expected to work with and through team members, peers and stakeholders to achieve results. When perspectives differ, emotions show up,  impacting the people you work with as well as your own work performance. How aware are you of your emotions and their impact on your behaviour and others around you? How do you strengthen your impact, presence and inte
learning_outcomes:
Recognise the role of EQ in leadership

Develop EQ behaviours and practices at work to better lead your officers through the understanding of EQ competencies, tools and knowledge

Increase your emotional self-awareness and understanding

Acquire skills to develop resilience, mental wellbeing and greater capacity for leadership

Develop interpersonal competencies to understand, interact and relate well with others in an emotionally intelligent way

Identify your current EQ strengths and areas of development based on the EQ-Assessment for Leaders (EQ-i 2.0) and relate the findings to your leadership context to enhance your effectiveness as a manager

Develop a personal action plan to enhance your leadership impact and presence
who_should_attend: You are a first-time manager or equivalent with supervisory responsibilities.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Leading teams effectively for Agile projects
description:
(Do note that CRATMVL was the previous code for this programme)

As a manager, you play a pivotal role in driving the success of Agile team structures and practices. This programme is tailored to equip you with the essential techniques and templates to elevate the performance of Agile teams.



While Agile is often linked to IT and software development, its principles and mindset are universally applicable. This programme will demonstrate how you can leverage Agile methodologies across diverse contexts, unlocking the potential for enhanced performance and success.


You will gain valuable insights on adopting an Agile mindset to effectively manage risks and complexities, while also learning strategies to optimise teamwork, elevate customer interactions, and boost productivity and flexibility within your Agile teams.
learning_outcomes:
Explain the key differences between Agile methodologies and frameworks.
Apply Agile values and principles when managing Agile teams.
Facilitate effective Agile team meetings with your team.
Manage the performance of respective responsibilities of core and/or non-core roles in your team.
Identify your team's Agile maturity and the next steps for effective continuous improvement
who_should_attend: You are a manager and above who is keen to lead your teams in managing projects using the Agile methodology.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Leading Innovation for Managers
description:
(Do note that CRLIMVL was the previous code for this programme)

In the current times of ambiguity, how can you help your team innovate while embracing failure and uncertainty? As a manager, what are some ways you can embed innovation into the functional and operational core of your team? 


The purpose of this programme is to inspire, educate and empower middle management to become more innovative, agile, and build innovative structures within your teams. Find out how to adopt and leverage an innovator's mindset, and be equipped with tools to lead, manage and foster innovation in hybrid teams. 


Sign up for this programme now to learn how to empower your team to innovate in uncertain times!
learning_outcomes:
Adopt the innovator’s mindset, methods and skills to effectively embed innovation within hybrid teams
Identify new growth opportunities to innovate on via building a well-balanced innovation portfolio and roadmap
Develop a more agile team culture and systems to encourage and catalyse innovation
Experience an innovation mini-sprint to develop and pitch solutions ro real world problems
who_should_attend: You are a manager (and above) looking to embrace transformational leadership, learn how to successfully innovate in the public sector and foster innovation in your team.

title: [Virtual Classroom] HR Analytics: Data Management and Reporting
description:
(Do note that CRDMRVL was the previous code for this programme)

The Human Resource (HR) function manages large sets of employee and transactional data for purposes of compliance, and reporting and analysis. As an HR professional, you are often required to organise, manage and synthesise these large datasets. How can you do so effectively and efficiently?

This 1-day programme was designed to help you acquire the essential skills needed to manage, prepare, transform and report data.








We offer different HR analytics courses that are tailored to different training needs across the HR analytics value chain. Please select and register for the HR analytics workshop that best suits your learning needs.


CRD​MRVL focuses on building skills in data management and reporting – the first link in the analytics value chain. If data is not managed or accessible, analytics cannot start.


CRHAZ12 focuses on the analytical processes after data management; more specifically, the analytical thou
learning_outcomes:
Develop a comprehensive understanding of the end-to-end analytics processes that is applicable to address most HR problems; and the role that data management & reporting plays along the value chain 
Build fundamental data processing skills to become more adept and efficient in making HR Data accessible for analytics 
Develop fundamental skills to become more efficient at generating consistent HR reports 
Appreciate how the above better enables the organization to build HR Analytics capabilities
who_should_attend: You are an HR Officer whose job functions include data management and reporting.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Institute For Human Resource Professionals Certification for HRMP and HRFP Alumni: IHRP-CP
description:
(Do note that CRIHCPV was the previous code for this programme)

 

Established as an HR Professional body, the Institute of Human Resource Professionals (IHRP) sets the benchmark for the competencies and behaviours of future-ready HR professionals through the national HR certification framework. The IHRP certification is thus a practitioner’s certification and a validation of a practitioner’s HR experience. This certification can complement and validate HR professionals within the Public Service with competencies and capabilities that define a good HR professional. The certification is now extended to the alumni of HRMP before 2018 and alumni of HRFP with at least 3 years of HR experience. Through CSC, HRMP alumni would not need to complete the experience assessment, typically required of private candidates of IHRP.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the intent of IHRP and how it dovetails with HR competencies
Understand the structure of the IHRP certification papers and be familiar with the possible questions from both papers
who_should_attend:
You are an alumnus of the HR Managers Programme (HRMP) or HR Foundation Programme (HRFP) with at least 3 years of experience in HR, who intends to pursue the IHRP-CP certification.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Future Ready: Manage Relationships with Stakeholders Effectively
description:
(Do note that CRMRSVL was the previous code for this programme)

Amidst rapid disruption, public officers would have to equip themselves with the tools and skills to thrive in a workforce where diversity and uncertainty are the new constants. In an era where working together becomes more collaborative, inclusive and strategic, it is important for public officers to be able to manage stakeholder relationships in the workplace through effective communication approaches.
learning_outcomes:
Describe the impact of Emotional Intelligence in communication and relationship management using Goleman's 4 EQ Domains
Describe the four styles of communication to manage relationships effectively
Apply skills such as assertive communication, empathy and conflict resolution to communicate and collaborate effectively in the workplace
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is keen to strengthen your stakeholder relationships in the workplace through effective communication approaches.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Fundamentals of Public Finance Management Module 2 - Government and Statutory Board Accounting
description:
(Do note that CRFPFMB was the previous code for this programme)

Do you want to better understand Government and Statutory Board Accounting?

You are at the right place if you are a Finance officer who has less than two years of experience working in the Public Sector or keen to learn about public finance.

Through three blended learning modules, you will learn about Public Finance policies, government accounting and become familiar with central financial systems. This helps you understand your role and other roles in finance better. You may attend the modules in any order.
learning_outcomes:
List out the government accounting principles
Explain how the principles are applied within agencies, supported by central financial system
Describe key government accounting and reporting concepts such as Chart of Accounts, Fund Accounting
Explain how to apply the key accounting and reporting concepts in your work
Explain the need for separate accounting standards for Statutory Boards (SBs) and the key SB-FRS that apply to SB accounting
who_should_attend: You are a Finance Public Officer who has two years or less experience in the Public Service Finance Function

title: [Virtual Classroom] Fundamentals of Public Finance Management Module 3 - Central Systems and Operations
description:
(Do note that CRFPFMC was the previous code for this programme)

Do you want to better understand the Central Systems Landscape and Public Sector Corporate Systems?

You are at the right place if you are a Finance officer who has less than two years of experience working in the Public Sector or keen to learn about public finance.

Through three blended learning modules, you will learn about Public Finance policies, government accounting and become familiar with central financial systems. This helps you understand your role and other roles in finance better. You may attend the modules in any order.
learning_outcomes:
Describe how government cash management and payments work as well as centralised liquidity management, and the key government financial systems supporting these functions
Describe key processes such as procure-to-pay and year-end closing cycle
Explain how Human Resource, Finance and payroll are interrelated
Explain the processes related to payroll, claims and medical billing
Describe future developments in Finance systems
who_should_attend: You are a Finance Public Officer who has two years or less experience in the Public Service Finance Function

title: [Virtual Classroom] Future Ready: Building Emotional Resilience with EQ
description:
(Do note that CRBERVL was the previous code for this programme)

Emotional intelligence helps people to understand themselves well, so as to better relate to and connect with others. A person who is self-aware, socially-adapt and empathetic can survive and thrive well through challenging times because they have the social and relational skills to handle the difficult situations.

Being empathetic to others also has a positive impact on our own emotional adjustment – we feel needed and become more confident about what we can offer to others, and we become stronger and more resilient in the process.

Sign up for this programme to build emotional intelligence and resilience as a foundation for your personal and professional success.
learning_outcomes:
Describe the different components of EQ and the importance of EQ in the workplace.
Discuss and apply tools to develop key EQ competencies to build resilience and deal with ambiguity or unforeseen circumstances in the workplace.
Discuss and apply tools to develop key EQ competencies to build critical work relationships and support network .
Demonstrate EQ and resilience in difficult conversations and other challenging interpersonal situations.
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is keen to develop stronger emotional resilience to stay resilient in the workplace.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Facilitating Meetings that Matter: Energising People, Achieving Outcomes
description:
(Do note that CRLAFVL was the previous code for this programme)

As a leader, how can you guide your team through effective decision-making processes that result in quality decisions? How can you facilitate strong buy-in and commitment for decisions made?

Important decisions and critical work are usually made and set in motion during meetings. Whether they are big or small, a well-structured and purposeful meeting delivers quality decisions that enjoy strong buy-in from its members. Yet, many of the meetings we attend do not deliver such results.

In fact, facilitating such decision-making processes has become one of the biggest challenges managers face today.

Find out how you can facilitate effective processes that will help your team make high quality decisions that they believe in and are committed to. This programme will provide you with the framework and skills to do so.
learning_outcomes:
Design your meeting with the appropriate structure to achieve effective, collective decision-making
Develop and deliver questions that effectively guide your participants through your meeting design and its associated agenda
Learn how to open your meeting in a way that supports your participants being engaged, informed, on the same page, and enthusiastic to get going
Learn how to wrap up your meeting to ensure no loose ends and to dramatically increase the chances of post-meeting action
who_should_attend:
You are a manager overseeing one or more teams. You would like to conduct effective meetings (virtual, face-to-face and even hybrid) that facilitate ownership of issues and optimal decisions for your team(s).
