Public Service Learning Fest 2026 brings learning to life with keynotes, workshops, webinars, learning journeys, and curated LEARN resources. Dive in to level up your AI and innovation skills this July.​ Explore More

1/1
About

title: [Virtual Classroom] Finance 101 for Public Officers
description:
(Do note that CRBFMVL was the previous code for this programme)

In this programme, you will learn fundamental skills in accounting, interpreting financial statements and financial management. Designed for Public Officers with no prior accounting knowledge, you will gain a clearer understanding of financial management through the use of simple illustrations and activities.
learning_outcomes:
Describe the importance of financial management
Use key financial and accounting jargons appropriately
Understand the key components of financial statements
Recognise the importance of financial ratios
Explain Singapore’s fiscal policy approach and the MOF’s Budget Management Framework (BMF)
Identify elements of risk management and internal controls
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore public officer who has no prior finance knowledge.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Employer Branding to Attract and Retain the Right Talent
description:
(Do note that CREMBVL was the previous code for this programme)

The Society for Human Resources Management (SHRM) estimates that it would cost an organisation 6 to 9 months’ worth of salary to replace an employee! Attracting and retaining the right talent is thus key to any organisation’s success — how would employer branding help?


Find out in this hands-on, practical 2-day programme, where you will learn to leverage your organisation’s unique employee value proposition to develop and bring your employer branding to life.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the importance and benefits of Employer Branding in attracting and retaining talents
Define Employer Branding and identify its components
Define Employee Value Proposition and the factors that attract talent
Define and identify Employee Moments of Truth to bring to life the employer brand
Assess and define goals, strategy and action plan to kick-start or enhance the Employer Branding initiative
who_should_attend: You are a Human Resource officer whose job functions involve HR business partnering, recruitment, talent development and management.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Developing My Career Story
description:
(Do note that CRDCSVL was the previous code for this programme)

Our careers are like books, each with a beginning, filled with chapters of plots leading to a desired ending. As authors of our own lives, the onus of writing our career story has always been in our hands. However, most of us do not know how to take control, develop and shape our careers. Changes in the surrounding environment, which include the workplace and our own personal life circumstances, often lead us to consider possible plots for our ongoing career storylines. So how can you take charge and develop your own career story?


In this programme, you will be guided to learn about yourself as the story author, review your written career story and develop a narrative to shape the plot of your future career story.



The programme will include the use of diagnostic profiling reports, and group discussions around concepts and personal experiences. It will include pre- and post- reflective and summative assignments to sup
learning_outcomes:
By the end of the programme, you will be able to:


Discuss perspectives to shape your worldview of career
Develop self-awareness of your interests, values and personality using a comprehensive diagnostic assessment
Apply data collected to determine person-to-work fit
Develop a career narrative to guide career decision making
Access and utilise various Public Service career resources that will aid you in your career development
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who wants to take ownership of your career and have a better understanding of how to do so.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Creating my Career Opportunities
description:
(Do note that CRERAVL was the previous code for this programme)

Ready to take your career to the next level but not sure how to update and optimise your resume? Look no further! Creating My Career Opportunities is here to help you master the art of resume writing and interviewing. To increase your career mobility, a powerful resume and exceptional interview skills are the keys to unlocking new opportunities and propelling yourself forward.

This programme is designed to equip you with the essential tools and strategies needed to take your career to soaring new heights. Whether you're aiming for a promotion, a career switch, or a fresh start, our programme will empower you to achieve your goals.


 Invest in yourself and break free from career stagnation. Join Creating my Career Opportunities today and unlock a world of limitless growth and we'll be there to help you seize it with confidence!
learning_outcomes:
This course will help participants to achieve the following objectives:

Understand the evolution of jobs and work traits and today’s recruitment and job seeking behaviours
Build up self knowledge by analysing the work characteristics required of different jobs
Identify transferable traits for new areas of work by analysing important work characteristics in demand
Communicate the analysis of your work characteristics and transferable skills at work in resume and interview preparation
who_should_attend: Senior officers of MX 10 – 12 public and civil officers of varying employment experiences who would like to gain deeper self-awareness of their skills and abilities in their jobs as part of their long-term career planning.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Basic Systems Thinking (using 3Quest™)
description:
(Do note that CRSTB10 was the previous code for this programme)

Systems are all around us. Our workplaces, organisations, families, finances, policies and the economy are all examples of human systems. Thus, our success in life and work is very much dependent on how well we manage such systems. Unfortunately, many of us are unable to deal effectively with systems. Very often, our attempts at improving our lives and work simply end up making things worse. Or, the system resists our best efforts and does not change at all. Naturally, we end up feeling frustrated, puzzled and even helpless.

The reason for this limited success in dealing with human systems lies in the difficulty in seeing the connections that exist among its various components (i.e. the stakeholders within the system). When we fail to see such connections, our well-intentioned actions often create unintended consequences.

Systems Thinking is a set of simple tools to help us see these connections better and create more e
learning_outcomes:
Know the 3 questions that System Thinkers ask (3QueST™)
Identify leverage within the system to get the results we want
Create strategies that can be sustained over time, and grow the results we want by creating a Success Loop
Anticipate and deal with unintended consequences using the TFD tool
Identify and deal with the causes of resistance using the MARS framework
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is interested in understanding systems and how seeing the connections systemic components can lead to better results.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Agile Leadership for Directors
description:
(Do note that CRALDVL was the previous code for this programme)

In today's fast-paced business environment, your ability to adapt and thrive is crucial. The "Agile Leadership for Directors" programme equips you with the knowledge and tools to establish a platform for success through Agile practices. By defining a clear vision, fostering a culture of adaptability, and setting strategic priorities, you will learn how to maximize the potential of Agile teams.


While Agile is often associated with IT and software development, its principles and mindset are universally applicable. This programme will demonstrate how you can effectively implement Agile across diverse contexts, empowering you to lead with agility and resilience.


Throughout the programme, you will gain insights on adopting an Agile mindset to provide your teams with clarity on expectations and effectively manage performance against set goals. Additionally, the programme offers valuable opportunities for discussion and enga
learning_outcomes:
Explain the key differences between Agile methodologies and frameworks.
Apply Agile values and principles when defining your visions, culture, and priorities.
You will facilitate effective Agile team meetings with your teams.
Manage the performance of Agile teams against your vision and strategies.
Identify your Agile structure's maturity and the next steps for effective continuous improvement.
Identify areas of work where you can adopt agile ways of working into your Dept’s work.
Identify potential resistance when implementing agile ways of work and how to overcome them.
who_should_attend: You are an Assistant Director, Deputy Director or Director, and are keen to lead and support your organisation's transformation using the Agile method.

title: [Virtual Classroom] Advanced Systems Thinking (using 3Quest™)
description:
(Do note that CRSTAVL was the previous code for this programme)

This is an Advanced programme that builds on the Basic Systems Thinking Using 3QueST™ workshop. You would have been familiar with The 3 Questions That Systems Thinkers Ask (3QueST™) and the basic tools shared in the Basic programme.




In this advanced programme, we will explore more advanced strategies.  Previously, you have learnt some basic tools to anticipate and deal with unintended consequences.  In this programme, you will learn how to recognise the following 4 common types of unintended consequences and explore the corresponding strategies to deal with each.

Fixes That Fail
Shifting The Burden
Eroding Goals
Escalation




You will also learn that a period of sustained success can lead to new challenges, layering on what you have learnt about Success Loops previously. Following are some archetypes that reflect the kinds of challenges we face today even as our economy grows! We will discuss strategies to address t
learning_outcomes:
Understand Systems Archetypes and how they occur frequently in our lives and work
Use Of Systems Archetypes To Answer The 3 Questions Systems Thinkers Ask (3Quest™)
Identify the patterns these Systems Archetypes create over time and the mental models driving them.
Learn strategies to deal with Systems Archetypes by using Challenge Questions
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who is an individual contributor/ oversees individual contributors/ a team of individual contributors/ managers/ multiple teams. You are interested in understanding systems at a more advanced level, its impact on organisations, and how better results can be created through seeing the connections among systemic components. **Pre-Requisite: You should have attended the Basic Systems Thinking (using 3Quest™) before attending this programme.

title: [Virtual Classroom] A Practical and People-centric Approach to Job Redesign for Practitioners
description:
(Do note that CRJRWV was the previous code for this programme)

As Public Sector Transformation gathers pace, learn to optimise your organisation's workforce for the future with Job Redesign. Gain greater clarity and deeper insights into redesigning jobs that will continue to be meaningful and fulfil business requirements in your organisation. Identify what is required to facilitate this process effectively and create solutions for transitioning the workforce to its ideal future state.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the Job Redesign Methodology, processes and outcomes for effective implementation
Apply suggested tools and methodologies to facilitate the Job Redesign process
Distinguish the various steps to Job Redesign and how they relate to one another
Identify Job Redesign opportunities within your organisation
who_should_attend: You are an HR Practitioner, Director, Business Unit Manager, or Practitioner who is directly involved in a Job Redesign Project.

title: [Blended Classroom] The E.M.M.A Experience
description:
(Do note that CREMMA3 was the previous code for this programme)

The Electronic Middle Manager Aide (E.M.M.A) Experience brings you leadership development content via Harvard Manage Mentor (HMM). Carefully curated and supplemented by CSC, the E.M.M.A Experience will support you through your transition in a personalised and timely manner, with rich content and engaging learner interactions. You can learn at your own time and pace, and participate in small groups for deeper reflection, sharing and practice. You will also gain a network of peers for support as you continue your leadership journey.




Download the prospectus to learn more about this programme.
learning_outcomes:
Gain an honest and candid appraisal of your personal strengths and developmental needs through
the FIRO-B self assessment tool
other Middle Managers in the learning cohort

Gain leadership development insights, practical tools and handles for application to your own context and leadership challenges

Form networks with other Middle Managers for continued support in your leadership journey
who_should_attend: You are newly promoted into middle management (e.g. SAD DD at MX10 or equivalent level), within the 1st year of transition into this role and will not be attending the EMpowered to Lead Programme (EMLP). A middle manager is typically an experienced manager of a team or multiple teams, and is directly accountable to senior management. This definition might differ in organisations with a flatter hierarchy, in which case the HRO can exercise discretion in consultation with CSC.

title: Accelerating Your Agency's Digital Transformation
description:
(Do note that EVADT was the previous code for this programme)

The challenge for many leaders is to embed digital transformation into every part of the organisation. Leaders are struggling to fully align the organisation, create the right measures that hold everyone responsible and drive the right actions, change the mindset of employees to become truly customer-centric, and create a more agile culture.

Crafting strategy in a digital world is a top-down driven initiative. Digital transformation, however is a bottom-up driven initiative involving, for example, hackathons, customer journeys, and design thinking. As a result, leaders no longer go to meetings to make sure people have done what they were asked. Rather, they attend them to find out what is happening and adjust from being the first to speak to the last to speak.

In this seminar, you will learn common digital transformational challenges, and how you could transform your agency in a digital world with the help of frameworks a
learning_outcomes:
By the end of the session, you will be able to:

Define your agency's digital ambition.
Explain how different moving parts of digital transformation come together from a strategic and operational perspective using a proven model.
Identify suitable digital technologies and methodologies for your agency. 
Adopt frameworks to guide and accelerate agency through its transformation journey.
who_should_attend: This seminar is open to Directors and Deputy Directors in the service delivery and digital capability function.

title: Level Up Your Career Game Plan
description: This interactive game experience is part of the career fitness movement, designed to help you tackle real career challenges in a risk-free and interactive environment.​
learning_outcomes:
Reflect on your own career fitness in a low-stakes environment​
Discover new ways to help officers in your organisation with their career fitness

title: Building Teams That Thrive During Transformation: The Science Behind Team Effectiveness
description:
Date: 24 April 2026, 9.30am – 11.00am

Venue: Civil Service College, Auditorium Level 1

Overview

What separates teams that thrive during transformation from those that struggle? Drawing from Harvard research and real-world applications, this keynote reveals the evidence-based framework that drives 80% of team success.

Dr Melissa Sayer will share insights from the 6 Team Conditions model. Through compelling case studies from transformation initiatives, participants will discover how successful leaders create the conditions for team effectiveness during change and learn from examples where teams failed when these conditions weren't established.

This interactive session is designed for Deputy Directors and Directors leading multiple teams and driving cross-agency collaboration. Dr Sayer will guide participants through practical applications, addressing real challenges such as leading change across silos, managing competing priorities, and building adaptive capacity during organisation
learning_outcomes:
How you will benefit

Recognise the tensions and trade-offs that exist in team leadership in complex systems;
Master the fundamentals of team effectiveness using an evidence-based framework–including how to apply them during change and transformation
Diagnose your team using the 6 Team Conditions framework—the 6 Conditions account for up to 80% of the variance in team performance,
Learn from case study examples that successfully navigated major changes and common pitfalls that cause teams to struggle during transformation
Identify where breakdowns occur in interconnected teams working toward whole-of-government outcomes
Gain practical tools to diagnose team performance issues and build adaptive capacity immediately in your own teams

title: The Career Game Workshop
description:
Looking for an engaging way to raise awareness about career fitness across your agency? 

The Career Game Workshop is a 2 hours in-person workshop for agencies wanting to launch or energize their Career Fitness Movement in a large-scale event (e.g. townhall) to create interest and build awareness in an accessible, memorable format. 

This is an interactive gamified experience where participants discover foundational career development concepts through play and personal reflection. Through interactive gameplay and guided reflection, participants explore what career fitness means for them individually and why taking ownership of their career matters. 

Note: This workshop is only available by in-house requests.
learning_outcomes:
By the end of this workshop, participants will be able to:

Recognize the importance of taking ownership of their own career development and building CareerFitness
Identify a key aspect of self-awareness relevant to career planning (i.e. career interests)
Discover Public Service career development resources and opportunities (e.g., STIP, GIG)
Reflect personally on their current career situation and the importance to take action.
who_should_attend:
This workshop is ideal for organisations:

Planning large-scale engagement events or townhalls
Launching career fitness initiatives

This workshop is designed for officers at all levels who want to reflect on their career journey and explore what career ownership means for them

 

Note: This workshop works best at raising awareness in an engaging way and serves as an excellent complement to deeper learning programmes such as:

Career Fitness Fundamentals Programme (CFFP) – A mandatory 3-hour webinar for all officers covering essential career development foundations
CSC's supplementary career programmes – Life-stage specific programmes with tailored tools and frameworks for officers at different career stages

Building your agency's career fitness plan? If you're exploring ways to integrate The Career Game Workshop into a broader career development strategy or want to discuss customization options, reach out to Esther [esther_leow@cscollege.gov.sg] or Wei Ting [ng_wei_ting@cscollege.go

title: Transforming Service Delivery with AI Webinar: Huawei's Smart City Solutions for Futian District Government
description:
(Do note that EVTSDWA was the previous code for this programme)

Join us for an insightful webinar exploring how AI is revolutionising public service delivery, featuring Huawei's smart city solutions implemented in Futian District, Shenzhen. Through this session, discover how the Futian district government successfully leveraged AI to enhance citizen services.


Our speaker, Simon (En Hua) Guo, brings extensive experience from Shenzhen's city governance initiatives and will share practical insights from Futian's digital transformation journey. Learn about innovative solutions including drone patrol systems, intelligent service assistants, and automated ticketing systems that have significantly improved service delivery efficiency.
learning_outcomes:
By the end of the session, participants will be able to:
Understand the benefits of AI-driven solutions in improving citizen service delivery and recognise how they can transform government operations.
Discover potential AI solutions and opportunities that can improve government service delivery efficiency in Singapore.
Identify key steps for establishing successful public-private partnerships that drive innovation and enhance service delivery.
Outline
Future of Smart Cities & Digital Services


- Evolution of citizen expectations in a digital age


- Reimagining future public services: Anytime, anywhere, one-stop solutions
- Impact of AI and big data on urban governance


Futian's Digital Transformation Story


- From Challenge to Innovation: Managing population growth with reduced workforce
- Citizen-centric service redesign
- AI Solutions in Action (Case Studies)
Smart city surveillance and monitoring
AI-powered assistants
Automated document processing
Citizen query resolution
- Mea

title: Essentials for Effective Coaching
description:
(Do note that EVEVEFE was the previous code for this programme)

Organisations with a strong coaching culture have higher employee engagement and productivity levels. Beyond building coaching skills, leaders can adopt a coaching mindset that balances being directive and giving people time/space to leverage on their own resources while manage self during coaching conversations.
learning_outcomes:
Greater clarity between performance management conversations, problem solving conversations, coaching, mentoring and counselling, and learning to be self-aware and discerning in the use of these in leadership
Frameworks/concepts/techniques to enhance listening and coaching skills, along with use of a Personal Development Plan template for structuring the regular staff development conversations
Enhanced coaching skills, self-awareness and confidence to coach through the structured practices in listening, asking powerful questions that provoke thinking, and solution-focused approach to coaching
who_should_attend:
Directors

title: CareerPower®: Career Conversations for Busy Leaders
description:
(Do note that EVEVCPC was the previous code for this programme)

Research shows that career development is one of the top drivers of retention, productivity, and results. People want to learn, develop, and grow in their roles and leave their jobs in search of better career development. This session sheds much-needed light on what leaders can specifically do – within the time-starved, priority-rich, pressure-cooker environment in which they operate – to support employees’ careers.
learning_outcomes:
Reframe myths that stop us from productive career conversations
Recharge your will to make time for career conversations
Revive the spark to grow yourself and your people

title: Leadership and Organisation Development (L&OD) Conference 2024 – Flourishing Organisations: Moving Beyond Performance
description:
(Do note that EVILODC was the previous code for this programme)

REGISTRATION IS NOW CLOSED

Thank you for your interest. We have reached full capacity for this conference and regret that there is no waitlist available.

For those who have registered, we will see you April!

//

As leaders and OD practitioners in the Public Service, we are confronted with a rapidly changing world that presents unique challenges. Citizens' expectations are rising, talent shortages are becoming more prevalent, and our workforce is increasingly fatigued. This is not sustainable. To overcome these challenges, we cannot afford to remain where we are. Instead, we must strive to build organisations and individuals that flourish.​​

Flourishing organisations are those that excel in achieving their goals and mission while also fostering a culture that values and supports their employees. Flourishing people are not only successful in their work, but also experience a sense of well-being and fulfilment in their p
who_should_attend: Senior Leaders, Organisation Development (OD), Leadership Development (LD), Human Resource (HR), Change Practitioners and Transformation Teams within the Singapore Public Service.

title: Brick or Break: The Customer Experience Challenge
description:
(Do note that EVBBCX was the previous code for this programme)

'Brick or Break' is a hands-on simulation that shows you what it really takes to deliver customer-centric government services. Navigate complex service requests by coordinating across teams. Experience how your decisions impact customers firsthand and discover how effective cross-functional teamwork transforms fragmented touchpoints into meaningful customer experiences.
learning_outcomes:
Describe the relationships among various functional teams in supporting the desired customer experience.
Explain how collaborative approaches improve service delivery when compared to siloed operations.
Adopt the customer-centric service mindset to support your agency's customer experience priorities.
who_should_attend: Public officers who shape customer experience, be it in policymaking, strategy development, operations management, or technology solutions.

title: ALTITUDE 2026: AI & Learn Tech Summit
description:
About the Summit

Civil Service College (CSC) proudly presents ALTITUDE 2026: AI & Learn Tech Summit, an inaugural conference that explores the evolving role of AI and learning technologies in public service.
learning_outcomes:
Examine emerging learning technologies using structured foresight frameworks when assessing new tech solutions for learning programmes
 
Understand structured problem definition to prevent downstream technology glitches before launching AI-related learning pilots and projects
 
Balance AI personalisation tools with human storytelling and peer interaction to create meaningful engagement in digital learning experiences
 
Articulate clear ROI and align learning technology investments with organisational goals when securing stakeholder support, drawing from government agencies workforce transformation insights
 
Consider scalability of pilot programmes while balancing standardisation with government-specific customisation needs, while keeping in mind ground realities
who_should_attend: HR Leaders and L&D Professionals within the Public Service

title: AI & The Human Element:  Leading Change in the Age of Artificial Intelligence
description:
(Do note that EVEV-AI was the previous code for this programme)

In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape, Artificial Intelligence is reshaping how organisations operate, communicate, and deliver value. This sharing session equips Organisation Development practitioners with evidence-based strategies to lead AI-driven change initiatives whilst prioritising the human element.


Drawing from Prosci's comprehensive research on AI adoption, participants will explore the intricate dynamics of managing technological change through a people-first lens. The session delves deep into understanding individual responses to AI integration, team dynamics during digital transformation, and organisation-wide readiness factors that determine success.


Through interactive discussions, participants will develop a nuanced understanding of how to balance technological advancement with human needs, ensuring that AI adoption enhances rather than diminishes the workplace experience.


The sharing session
learning_outcomes:
By the end of this session, participants will:


Develop a comprehensive understanding of Prosci's latest research findings on successful AI adoption, including key human and organisational enablers of successful implementation.


Build confidence and clarity to navigate AI adoption challenges and opportunities within your organisation.


Apply targeted, people-centric strategies to guide AI-related change within your organisation, based on evidence-backed insights from the research.
who_should_attend: This session is ideal for: - Organisation Development practitioners who are currently supporting or preparing to support AI-related change initiatives within their organisations - Change management professionals seeking to enhance their capability to lead digital transformation projects with a human-centric approach - HR professionals and internal consultants who play a key role in managing the people aspects of technological change - Leaders and managers responsible for implementing AI solutions while maintaining team engagement and performance

title: Masterclass in Generative Change through Dialogic OD
description:
(Do note that CRMCDOD was the previous code for this programme)

This advanced, highly experiential masterclass is designed for seasoned OD practitioners seeking to deepen their practice through the lens of Dialogic Organisational Development. Dialogic OD is not a single method or set of techniques, but a way of understanding organisations, leadership, and change that places emphasis on emergence, shared meaning-making, and generative inquiry.




Participants will explore how to engage complex organisational challenges by involving stakeholders as co-creators of change. The course focuses on enhancing dialogic presence, co-designing, strategising and contracting with sponsors on the approach to complex adaptive change and applying dialogic principles to group processes and large-system interventions. It also invites critical reflection on how and when to integrate diagnostic and dialogic approaches in a contextually congruent way.
learning_outcomes:
Understand the foundations and evolution of Dialogic Organisational Development, and what distinguishes it from more traditional, diagnostic approaches.
Recognise why Dialogic OD offers a more generative and transformative approach to change.
Deepen one’s dialogic presence and mindset through greater self-awareness and intentional use-of-self in facilitation and change processes.
Apply dialogic principles to the design and facilitation of group and large-system interventions, including when and how to engage emergent, self-organising processes.
Apply key strategies for establishing and enabling change teams to effectively co-design and implement generative change interventions/events.
Master the principles and practice techniques for facilitating generative conversations that catalyse organisational transformation
Explore and implement generative change strategies that support sustainable transformation in complex and evolving organisational contexts.
who_should_attend: You are an Organisation Development practitioner in the Singapore Public Service seeking to deepen your expertise in facilitating transformative change through enhanced understanding of organisational dynamics. This advanced masterclass will strengthen your capability to navigate emergent change processes, implement generative strategies, and develop a strong dialogic presence that influences system-wide transformation. Prerequisites The programme is designed for experienced OD practitioners, including those who integrate dialogic approaches within other functional roles. Prerequisites include completion of either the OD Graduate Certificate Programme (MSGCOD), Intermediate Programme for OD Practitioners (MSIPOVL), or OD Practitioner Certification Programme (MSODCP). These foundational programmes ensure participants have the necessary grounding in OD principles to engage meaningfully with advanced dialogic methodologies and self-organising change processes. If you have equivalent exper

title: Train the Trainer Workshop for EES
description:
(Do note that CREES03 was the previous code for this programme)

Train-the-Trainer: for Agency Reps to teach your colleagues how to interpret EES findings and plan for follow up actions (Oct).
learning_outcomes:
Gain familiarity with EES description and interpretation of key findings and with EES dashboard
Understanding key components of an action planning discussion
Practise conducting the sharing and supporting your colleagues
Resources on the running of action planning workshops – sample session outlines & activities

title: Team TenaCity: Interactive Tools for Team Success
description:
(Do note that CRTRTT was the previous code for this programme)

Are you leading a team for the first time, looking to strengthen team relationships, or seeking to enhance team wellbeing in your workplace? This hands-on half-day workshop gives Singapore Public Officers practical frameworks to understand what makes teams tick and how to make them work better.

You'll explore proven frameworks through our brand-new Team TenaCity board game, which is uniquely developed using positive psychology principles to build team resilience. Through interactive gameplay, you'll experiment with different team strategies to address common workplace challenges and scenarios, discovering strengths-based approaches that turn obstacles into opportunities for growth.

Whether you're enabling teams to prepare for addressing common team and work challenges, building resilience, or strengthening collaborative relationships, the workshop experience will show you how to apply these frameworks in real situations
learning_outcomes:
At the end of the programme, you will be able to:

• Describe PEL (Performance, Experience, Learning), GRPI (Goals, Roles, Process, Interactions) frameworks and the 6 Conditions for Team Effectiveness

• Apply these frameworks to your own team context and challenges

• Identify strengths-based GRPI team strategies and practices that can be applied or adapted to your own context

• Apply Team TenaCity as a conversational tool to enhance team relationships

• Facilitate a Team TenaCity session to promote team wellbeing and generate solutions for team challenges
who_should_attend: You are a Singapore Public Officer who leads or works within an intact team and seeks to understand the key dynamics in a team, strengthen team resilience, enhance collaborative relationships, and improve communications through engaging, experiential learning methods. First-time managers or soon-to-be team leaders are very welcomed! This workshop is also suitable for you if you are a Wellness Ambassador who is keen to promote wellbeing and resilience amongst teams or OD Practitioner designing and leading team interventions.

title: EES Results Interpretation and Dashboard Familiarisation Workshop
description:
(Do note that CREES01 was the previous code for this programme)

The EES Results Interpretation and Dashboard Familiarisation Workshop is designed to equip EES reps with knowledge and skills on how to navigate the survey dashboard, sense-make, and present EES findings.
learning_outcomes:
Description and interpretation of key findings from EES dashboard
Development of narrative on findings
Identification of areas for further diagnosis
Familiarisation with new survey dashboard

title: Coach to Influence – For OD Practitioners
description:
(Do note that CRCF101 was the previous code for this programme)

In today's ever-changing organisational landscape, effective Organisational Development (OD) practitioners require a versatile skill set that goes beyond conventional consulting approaches. This programme equips OD professionals with vital coaching skills, underscoring their pivotal role in engaging with diverse stakeholders, including leaders, staff, and across organisational boundaries.


Centered on experiential learning and strategies for system-wide influence through relationships, you will undergo a series of experiential learning to contextualise coaching skills and principles within your roles as OD Practitioners. By the end of the programme, you will not only have cultivated a robust understanding of coaching principles but will also have honed practical skills to seamlessly integrate coaching into your OD practice. With a dual emphasis on action and mindset, you will embark on a transformative journey of self-di
learning_outcomes:
Articulate and share coaching principles relevant to professional practice
Integrate the "doing and being" of an OD practitioner through the application of 4 coaching principles
Demonstrate competence in at least 6 of the identified coaching skills
Contextualise the practical application of coaching skills to facilitate positive change within individuals and teams.
Explore methods for integrating coaching into broader OD strategies to drive organisational effectiveness.
Assess personal coaching strengths and develop a plan for continuous improvement and growth as a coach-like practitioner
who_should_attend: You are an Organisation Development Practitioner, or a Change/ Transformation/ Strategic Planning Officer. To qualify for entry to the “Coach to Influence - for OD Practitioners” programme, you would have attended either MSOD101 OD Practitioner Foundation Programme or CRACMVL Prosci Change Management.

title: Professional Mentoring Programme for OD Practitioners
description:
(Do note that CRPMPOD was the previous code for this programme)

Looking to deepen your Organisation Development practice beyond traditional training? 

As an intermediate-level OD practitioner, you will benefit from the guidance of advanced practitioners who will serve as mentors and sounding boards, helping you effectively use yourself as an instrument of change within the Singapore Public Service context.

Through 4 one-and-a-half hour personalised mentoring sessions over 6 months, you will enhance your personal and professional growth with your mentor. Together, you will explore your goals, strengths, and areas for development through reflective practice. You will receive expert insights, discuss challenges, and develop strategies to maximise your potential in organisation development, all while working with a mentor who understands the unique context of the Singapore Public Service.

This programme forms part of the completion of the Executive Certification for OD Professionals (E
learning_outcomes:
By the end of this programme, you will be able to:

Articulate personal and professional OD goals and create a tailored development plan aligned with these aspirations
Exhibit improved self-awareness of strengths, limitations, and biases as an OD practitioner
Implement a structured reflective practice approach to improve OD interventions and personal effectiveness
Show increased confidence in navigating complex organisational dynamics and proposing context-appropriate OD solutions
who_should_attend: You are an intermediate to advanced level OD practitioner who is looking to develop your organisation development professional practice, through a mentoring relationship with a more seasoned OD practitioner. You may also be working towards your Executive Certificate in Organisation Development (ECOD). Prerequisites include completion of either the OD Graduate Certificate Programme (MSGCOD), Intermediate Programme for OD Practitioners (MSIPOVL), or OD Practitioner Certification Programme (MSODCP).

title: Thriving Together: A Practice Guide to build High-performing, Cohesive and Resilient Teams
description:
(Do note that ELETD01 was the previous code for this programme)

“Team, team, team!” - a common exclamation we hear, often mingled with emotional nuances. Building a high-performing, yet cohesive and resilient team, is both an art and a science. There are several conditions to note, some good practices to build and develop teams, and some personal leadership and “use of self” skills to apply and adopt, be it in a full physical or hybrid team environment. Learn all these and more with our e-module and become an informed and adaptable team developer and helper.
learning_outcomes:
Apply the frameworks and concepts of team effectiveness back to a team.
Identify, build and sustain key team development elements in both virtual and hybrid teams.
Design interventions aimed at improving team performance and health across levels of the organisation.
Enhance understanding of key human factors for team effectiveness, like psychological safety, emotions, covert processes and culture.
Create a change process for teams to address areas of concern according to modality and contexts.
who_should_attend: You are an OD Practitioner, or a team lead in your organisation.

title: Masterclass in Human Systems Dynamics and Adaptive Action
description:
(Do note that CRMSCA was the previous code for this programme)

Are you ready to master the art of navigating complex and rapidly changing environments? Join us for an exclusive Masterclass in Human Systems Dynamics and Adaptive Action, jointly accredited by the Human Systems Dynamics (HSD) Institute and Civil Service College.

Led by Glenda Eoyang, Founding Executive Director of HSD Institute, this masterclass is designed to empower leaders and advanced Organisational Development (OD) practitioners with the essential skills and insights needed to thrive in today’s dynamic and unpredictable landscape.

Through a blend of theory and practical applications, participants will delve into the intricacies of Human Systems Dynamics, learning to navigate complexities arising from systemic change. They will also gain hands-on experience in implementing Adaptive Action principles to effectively influence complex change as it emerges.

This masterclass offers a unique opportunity to harness the p
learning_outcomes:
Identify and navigate through four types of complexities arising from rapid, unpredictable, systemic change
Implement three principles of Adaptive Capacity to effectively perceive, understand and influence complex change as it emerges
Utilise HSD tools to uncover potential patterns within current, seemingly intractable or unsolvable issues
Apply theories – such as Adaptive Action, Pattern Logic, and Strategic Inquiry to address intricate challenges
Reflect on personal and organisational capacities to work with complex environments
Develop action plans to shift the patterns of wicked issues
who_should_attend: You are a leader or advanced-level Organisation Development practitioner in the Singapore Public Service. You would like to develop your competence in adapting OD designs appropriate for complex and unpredictable situations, through deep OD grounding and developmental, systemic, and sustainable approaches. Officers who also play an OD role in other functions are welcome. To qualify for entry to the Masterclass: Being an Agent of Change with Human System Dynamics (HSD), you will need to have attended either of these programmes: OD Graduate Certificate Programme (MSGCOD)/ Intermediate Programme for OD Practitioners (MSIPOVL)/ OD Practitioner Certification Programme (MSODCP).

title: Masterclass in Power, Politics and Partnership
description:
(Do note that CRMPPP was the previous code for this programme)

Can you lead change in your organisation, even if you're not in charge?


This Masterclass invites you to shift your perspective from being an effect of change to the cause of change. By mastering the dynamics of power, embracing positive influence, and fostering collaborative partnerships, you will gain the expertise to shape an organisational future defined by autonomy and empowerment. You will be guidedthrough an in-depth process, from crafting your personal vision to reforming management practices and policies, and readjusting organisational architecture. Don’t miss this opportunity to become a catalyst for positive change.


This is exclusive Masterclass in Power, Politics, and Partnership, is jointly accredited by the Effiqual Consultants Inc. and Civil Service College.
learning_outcomes:
Develop a mindset that embraces empowerment, partnership, and autonomy within the organisation
Understand the benefits of self-management strategies and the importance of accountability
Gain insights into organisational politics and how to renegotiate power structures effectively
Clarify personal vision and mission to align with organisational goals and possibilities
Learn assertiveness and related behavioural choices to enhance personal and organisational dynamics
Acquire skills to manage resistance, conflict, and difficult relationships constructively
Develop strategies for building alliances, nurturing trust, and fostering a partnership culture
Apply knowledge to initiate credible and lasting reforms in management practices and organisational structure
who_should_attend: You are an Organisation Development practitioner in the Singapore Public Service. You would like to develop your competence in managing power dynamics, embracing positive politics, and cultivating strong partnerships. Officers who also play an OD role in other functions are welcome. To qualify for entry to the Masterclass in Power, Politics and Partnership Programme, you need to have attended either of these programmes: OD Graduate Certificate Programme (MSGCOD) / Intermediate Programme for OD Practitioners (MSIPOVL) / OD Practitioner Certification Programme (MSODCP). If you do not have these qualifications but believe that this programme is for you, please write in with reasons why.
